description: Unique surrogate identifier for each physical mining asset record in the master register. Primary key for the asset data product.

Foreign key linking to equipment.asset_class. Business justification: The asset table currently stores asset_class as a STRING attribute, but a dedicated asset_class master table exists with PK asset_class_id. This is a classic denormalization pattern that should be nor

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Each asset is owned by a cost centre for maintenance budget allocation, depreciation charging, and AISC unit cost reporting. The asset table carries a denormalized cost_centre_code; replacing it with

Foreign key linking to customer.counterparty. Business justification: Mining assets are frequently leased, hired, or jointly owned with external counterparties. The asset register must reference the owning/leasing counterparty for contract management, lease expiry track

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Every physical mining asset (haul truck, excavator, drill rig) is capitalised in the fixed asset register for depreciation, NBV reporting, and IFRS/AASB116 compliance. EAM-to-FAR reconciliation is a m

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Core EAM business process: every physical asset is installed at a functional location. Maintenance planners use asset-to-FL assignment for work order routing, PM scheduling, and statutory compliance r

Foreign key linking to hse.hazard. Business justification: Each mining asset (haul truck, crusher, conveyor) is associated with a primary hazard profile (rollover, entrapment, conveyor nip). Linking assets to their primary hazard enables pre-start checklist g

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In multi-entity mining groups, assets are legally owned by specific entities for tax, insurance, and intercompany transfer purposes. Asset legal ownership drives transfer pricing, insurance valuations

Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: Asset register management requires knowing which mine site each asset is deployed at for maintenance planning, cost allocation, and capital budgeting. A mining engineer expects every asset to have a h

Reference to the parent asset in a multi-component asset hierarchy (e.g., an engine assembly belonging to a haul truck). Enables parent-child asset relationship modelling for complex equipment units. Null for top-level assets.

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Mining assets are often dedicated to specific product streams (iron ore haul trucks, coal handling equipment). Production planning, asset allocation, and performance reporting require tracking which p

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Mining equipment operates within specific tenement boundaries. Real operations track which licence area each asset is deployed to for regulatory compliance, expenditure allocation, and operational pla

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Assets permanently stationed at a TSF (decant pumps, slurry pipelines, monitoring equipment) must be registered against the specific facility for asset register management, closure liability attributi

Foreign key linking to procurement.vendor. Business justification: Mining equipment has OEM suppliers; tracking vendor enables warranty claims, parts procurement, and supplier performance analysis. Real business process: OEM parts sourcing and warranty management. Ne

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Assets under construction or acquired via capital projects are linked to a WBS element for CAPEX tracking and project cost roll-up. The asset table carries a denormalized wbs_element text field; a pro

Original capital expenditure (CAPEX) purchase price of the asset in the reporting currency. Used for depreciation calculations, asset register valuation, and capital replacement planning under IAS 16 and IFRS.
Human-readable descriptive name of the asset, typically combining equipment type and fleet number (e.g., 'Haul Truck HT-042', 'SAG Mill 01'). Used in reporting, dashboards, and work orders.

Date the asset was formally commissioned and placed into operational service at the mine site. Marks the start of the asset's operational lifecycle for maintenance scheduling, warranty tracking, and depreciation under IFRS.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; IFRS 16; IAS 16

description: SAP S/4HANA cost center code to which the asset's operating expenditure (OPEX) and maintenance costs are allocated. Enables financial reporting by asset and supports OPEX tracking against budget.

Maintenance criticality classification of the asset based on its impact on production, safety, and environmental compliance if it fails. Drives maintenance strategy selection, spare parts stocking levels, and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) prioritisation.
reference: Infor EAM Criticality Analysis; ISO 55001 Asset Management; ICMM

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the acquisition cost and financial values associated with the asset (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency asset register reporting.

Date the asset was permanently removed from operational service and decommissioned. Null for assets still in service. Used for asset lifecycle reporting, capital replacement planning, and mine rehabilitation cost estimation.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; IFRS 16; IAS 16

description: Source system identifier for the asset record in Infor EAM. Used for system-of-record traceability and integration with maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance schedules, and asset lifecycle management.

OEM-rated engine or motor power output of the asset in kilowatts (kW). Used for fuel consumption benchmarking, energy efficiency analysis, and equipment selection for mine planning. Null for non-powered assets.

Operational fleet number assigned to the asset at the mine site. Used by operators, dispatchers, and Fleet Management System (FMS) to identify and track the asset in the field. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar and Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Master; Caterpillar MineStar FMS

description: Maximum fuel tank capacity of the asset in litres. Used for fuel logistics planning, refuelling scheduling, and fuel consumption rate benchmarking. Null for electric or non-fuel-powered assets.
Primary energy source or fuel type powering the asset. Used for fuel consumption tracking, emissions reporting under GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement, and fleet electrification planning.
reference: GRI 305 Emissions; ISO 14001; IsoMetrix Environmental Monitoring

description: Indicates whether the asset is a mobile mining unit (True) such as a haul truck, excavator, or drill rig, or a fixed plant asset (False) such as a conveyor, crusher, or mill. Drives different tracking, reporting, and maintenance workflows.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Classification; Caterpillar MineStar FMS

description: Date of the most recent major overhaul or rebuild performed on the asset. Used to assess remaining component life, plan future overhauls, and calculate Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in the maintenance management system.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order History; ICMM Asset Management Guidelines

description: Descriptive operational location of the asset within the mine site (e.g., 'Pit 3 North Wall', 'Processing Plant - Grinding Circuit', 'ROM Pad'). Complements the site code with granular positional context for maintenance and dispatch.
reference: Infor EAM Location Hierarchy; Caterpillar MineStar FMS

description: Calendar year in which the asset was manufactured by the OEM. Used to calculate asset age, depreciation schedules, and Life of Mine (LOM) planning for fleet replacement.
OEM model designation of the asset (e.g., CAT 793F, Komatsu 930E, Sandvik DR461i). Used for spare parts management, maintenance procedures, and performance benchmarking against OEM specifications.
Date of the next planned preventive maintenance service for the asset as scheduled in Infor EAM. Used for maintenance planning, production scheduling, and availability forecasting.

Current cumulative odometer reading in kilometres for mobile assets. Used alongside SMR hours for maintenance scheduling, tyre life management, and haul road condition monitoring. Null for fixed plant assets.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; Caterpillar MineStar FMS

description: OEM-rated operating weight of the asset in metric tonnes. Used for road and haul route load limit compliance, ground bearing pressure calculations, and mine infrastructure design.

Current operational lifecycle status of the asset indicating whether it is available for production, undergoing maintenance, on standby, or permanently decommissioned. Drives availability calculations and maintenance scheduling in Infor EAM and MineStar.

Indicates whether the asset is company-owned, leased under a finance or operating lease, short-term hired, or contractor-supplied. Drives financial treatment under IFRS 16 and determines maintenance responsibility.

OEM-rated maximum payload capacity of the asset in metric tonnes. Applicable to haul trucks, loaders, and material handling equipment. Used for production planning, haulage cycle optimisation, and OEE calculations. Null for fixed plant assets.
Timestamp when the asset record was first created in the master register. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with asset register governance requirements.
Timestamp when the asset record was most recently updated in the master register. Used for change tracking, data quality monitoring, and integration refresh management in the Databricks Silver Layer.

business_glossary_term: Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) Tag
description: SCADA tag identifier used to link the asset to its real-time process data streams in the OSIsoft PI System historian. Applicable primarily to fixed plant assets such as conveyors, crushers, mills, and processing equipment.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SCADA Integration Standards

description: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) serial number uniquely identifying the physical asset unit. Used for warranty tracking, OEM service records, and regulatory compliance. Sourced from Infor EAM.

Code identifying the mine site where the asset is currently assigned and operated. Enables site-level fleet reporting, OEE benchmarking, and cross-site asset utilisation analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Plant Master.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Master; Infor EAM Location

description: Current cumulative Service Meter Reading (SMR) in engine hours recorded on the asset. Used as the primary trigger for preventive maintenance scheduling, component life tracking, and MTBF calculations in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health

description: Internal asset tag or barcode label affixed to the physical asset for inventory and maintenance identification. Distinct from fleet number and serial number. Sourced from Infor EAM.

Identifier of the onboard telematics or GPS tracking unit installed on the asset. Used to link real-time positional and operational data from Caterpillar MineStar and OSIsoft PI System for fleet management and equipment health monitoring.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar FMS; OSIsoft PI System

description: OEM-specified or engineering-assessed useful operational life of the asset in years. Used for straight-line depreciation calculations, Life of Mine (LOM) fleet planning, and capital replacement scheduling under IAS 16.
reference: IAS 16; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; Infor EAM

description: Date on which the OEM warranty for the asset expires. Used to trigger warranty claim processes, plan post-warranty maintenance strategies, and manage OEM service agreements in Infor EAM and Pronto Xi.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Master; Pronto Xi Procurement

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each asset class record in the mining equipment classification hierarchy. Primary key for the asset_class reference master.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Classification; SAP S/4HANA PM Equipment Category

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Mining fleet planning and capex budgeting is commodity-specific — crusher asset classes for copper differ from iron ore. Asset class configuration (payload capacity, PM intervals, OEE targets) is desi

Expected useful life of the asset class expressed in calendar years for financial depreciation and LOM planning purposes. Used alongside useful_life_operating_hours to determine the binding depreciation constraint. Supports CAPEX modelling and NPV/IRR analysis.
reference: IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; IFRS Capital Budgeting Standards

description: Unit of measure for the nominal_capacity field (e.g., t for payload tonnes, t/h for throughput tonnes per hour, m3 for bucket/bowl volume, mm for drill hole diameter, kW for power rating). Ensures correct interpretation of capacity values across equipment types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: t|t/h|m3|m3/h|mm|kW|MW|m|km/h — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 80000-1 Quantities and Units; Infor EAM Unit of Measure; SAP S/4HANA Unit of Measure

description: Capital expenditure (CAPEX) budget category under which assets of this class are typically funded. Sustaining CAPEX covers replacement of existing capacity; expansion CAPEX covers new capacity additions. Used for capital allocation, LOM planning, and financial reporting under IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-IM Capital Investment Management; IFRS Capital Expenditure Classification; ICMM Financial Reporting

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the asset class within the equipment classification hierarchy (e.g., HT-320T for 320-tonne haul truck class, SAG-36FT for 36-foot SAG mill class). Used as the business key across ERP, EAM, and FMS systems.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Class Code; SAP S/4HANA PM Asset Class (ANLKL); Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Class

description: Detailed narrative description of the asset class including its primary function, operational context within the mine, and key distinguishing characteristics. Supports procurement specifications and capital budgeting documentation.

Human-readable name of the asset class describing the equipment type and key specification (e.g., 'Ultra-Class Haul Truck 320T', 'Semi-Autogenous Grinding Mill 36ft', 'Rotary Blasthole Drill 270mm'). Used in reports, dashboards, and maintenance planning documents.
reference: Infor EAM; Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Classification

description: Current lifecycle status of the asset class record. Active classes are in current use for fleet planning and procurement. Phasing_out classes are being replaced by newer models. Retired classes are no longer procured but may still have operating assets. Controls visibility in procurement and planning systems.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Carbon dioxide (CO2) emission factor for assets of this class in kilograms per operating hour, derived from OEM fuel consumption specifications and applicable emission conversion factors. Used for Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions reporting under GRI, ICMM, and regulatory environmental disclosure requirements.
reference: GRI 305 Emissions; ICMM Environmental Reporting; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; IsoMetrix Environmental Monitoring

description: Timestamp when this asset class record was first created in the system, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for data governance and lineage tracking in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; Data Governance Audit Trail Standards

description: Indicates whether this asset class is designated as critical equipment whose failure would cause significant production loss, safety risk, or environmental incident. Critical equipment classes receive priority maintenance resourcing, enhanced spare parts stockholding, and elevated monitoring frequency in OSIsoft PI System.
reference: Infor EAM Criticality Assessment; ISO 55001 Asset Management; ICMM Critical Equipment Management

description: Accounting depreciation method applied to assets of this class for financial reporting and capital budgeting purposes. Units_of_production is common for mining equipment tied to ore tonnes mined. Aligns with IAS 16 and IFRS reporting requirements.
reference: IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Depreciation Key

description: Date from which this asset class definition became effective and valid for use in fleet planning, procurement, and maintenance scheduling. Supports temporal validity tracking for the asset class master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; Infor EAM; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Date on which this asset class definition ceases to be effective (nullable for open-ended active classes). Used to manage class version transitions when OEM specifications change or when a class is retired from the fleet register.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA; Infor EAM; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Functional equipment type within the fleet category (e.g., haul_truck, excavator, blasthole_drill, dozer, grader, water_cart, light_vehicle, conveyor, crusher, sag_mill, ball_mill, hpgr, flotation_cell, thickener, stacker, reclaimer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haul_truck|excavator|blasthole_drill|dozer|grader|water_cart|light_vehicle|conveyor|crusher|sag_mill|ball_mill|hpgr|flotation_cell|thickener|stacker|reclaimer — promote to reference product]
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Classification; Infor EAM Equipment Type; SAP S/4HANA PM Equipment Category

description: Top-level classification distinguishing between mobile fleet assets (haul trucks, excavators, drills, dozers, graders, water carts, light vehicles) and fixed plant assets (conveyors, crushers, mills, flotation cells, thickeners). Drives maintenance strategy, depreciation treatment, and fleet sizing models.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Infor EAM Asset Hierarchy; ICMM Asset Management Framework

description: Standard OEM-rated fuel consumption rate for assets of this class in litres per operating hour under typical mine operating conditions. Used for fuel budget forecasting, OPEX modelling, carbon emissions estimation, and fleet energy management reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fuel Management; OSIsoft PI System Fuel Monitoring; OEM Fuel Consumption Specifications; GRI 302 Energy

description: Indicates whether assets of this class involve or carry hazardous materials (e.g., explosives transport vehicles, acid handling equipment in SX-EW circuits, cyanide dosing systems in leaching). Triggers mandatory HSE compliance controls, PPE requirements, and IsoMetrix risk assessment workflows.
reference: ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; IsoMetrix HSE Risk Assessment; MSHA Hazardous Materials Regulations

description: Corresponding asset class identifier in the Infor EAM system used for asset lifecycle management, preventive maintenance scheduling, and work order management. Enables bi-directional data reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational EAM system.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Classification; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Timestamp when this asset class record was most recently modified, recorded in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and audit compliance in the silver layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time; Data Governance Audit Trail Standards

description: Primary maintenance strategy applied to assets of this class: time_based (fixed interval PM schedules), condition_based (triggered by measured condition thresholds), predictive (AI/ML-driven failure prediction from sensor data), or run_to_failure (for low-criticality components). Drives maintenance planning in Infor EAM and CMMS scheduling.
reference: Infor EAM Maintenance Strategy; ISO 55001 Asset Management; ICMM Asset Management Framework; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Plan

description: Operating hour interval at which a major component overhaul or rebuild is required for assets of this class (e.g., 20,000 hours for engine rebuild, 40,000 hours for full structural overhaul). Drives long-range maintenance CAPEX planning and LOM cost modelling.
reference: Infor EAM Major Overhaul Planning; SAP S/4HANA PM; OEM Overhaul Specifications; ICMM Asset Lifecycle Management

description: Equipment class identifier as defined in the Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System (FMS). Used to align telematics data, productivity metrics, and equipment health signals from MineStar with the enterprise asset class master.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System; FMS Equipment Classification

description: Principal quantitative capacity specification for the asset class expressed in the relevant unit of measure (e.g., payload tonnes for haul trucks, throughput tonnes per hour for mills and crushers, bucket capacity m³ for excavators, hole diameter mm for drills). The principal numeric fact defining the class's productive capability.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Infor EAM Asset Specifications; OEM Technical Specifications

description: Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) product category or model family designation as defined by the manufacturer (e.g., Caterpillar 793, Komatsu 930E, Liebherr T 284, Sandvik DR416i). Used for OEM warranty tracking, parts compatibility, and service agreement alignment.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; Infor EAM OEM Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer for this asset class (e.g., Caterpillar, Komatsu, Liebherr, Sandvik, Metso, FLSmidth). Used for vendor relationship management, warranty claims, and procurement sourcing strategies.
reference: Infor EAM Manufacturer Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; Pronto Xi Supplier

description: Gross operating weight of the asset class in metric tonnes as specified by the OEM. Used for haul road design, bridge load ratings, ground bearing pressure calculations, and site infrastructure planning.
reference: OEM Technical Specifications; Infor EAM Asset Specifications; Mine Planning Standards

description: Standard preventive maintenance (PM) service interval in operating hours for assets of this class (e.g., 250 hours for minor service, 1000 hours for major service). Defines the base PM scheduling cadence in the CMMS and Infor EAM maintenance plans.
reference: Infor EAM Preventive Maintenance; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Plan; OEM Service Manual

description: Installed engine or motor power rating for the asset class in kilowatts (kW) as specified by the OEM. Used for energy consumption modelling, fuel burn estimation, electrical infrastructure planning, and OPEX forecasting.
reference: OEM Technical Specifications; Infor EAM Asset Specifications; OSIsoft PI System Energy Monitoring

description: Current estimated replacement cost for a single asset unit of this class in US dollars. Used for insurance valuation, capital budgeting, CAPEX planning, and NPV/IRR analysis in LOM financial models. Classified as confidential due to commercial sensitivity in procurement negotiations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Valuation; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS Capital Budgeting; Pronto Xi Procurement

description: Corresponding asset class code (ANLKL) in SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Accounting (FI-AA) module. Used to reconcile the equipment master data with the financial asset register and ensure consistent depreciation treatment across ERP and EAM systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Class (ANLKL); IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Standardised size and capacity tier classification for the asset class used in fleet sizing models and capital budgeting (e.g., ultra_class for 300T+ haul trucks, large for 150-299T, medium for 90-149T). Enables fleet mix optimisation and benchmarking across mine sites.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Sizing; ICMM Fleet Management Guidelines; Deswik Mine Planning

description: Target physical availability percentage for assets of this class expressed as a percentage (e.g., 85.00 for 85%). Physical availability is the proportion of scheduled time the equipment is mechanically available for operation. Used as the benchmark for OEE calculations and maintenance performance KPIs.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar OEE Metrics; Infor EAM KPI Framework; ICMM Mining Equipment Performance Standards

business_glossary_term: Target Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) Hours
description: Target Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) in operating hours for assets of this class. MTBF is the average operating time between unplanned mechanical failures. Used to set maintenance performance benchmarks, evaluate reliability improvement initiatives, and support predictive maintenance model calibration.
reference: Infor EAM Reliability KPIs; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 14224 Reliability Data Collection; ICMM Asset Management

business_glossary_term: Target Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) Hours
description: Target Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) in hours for assets of this class. MTTR measures the average time required to restore equipment to operational status following an unplanned failure. Used to benchmark maintenance crew efficiency and spare parts availability strategies.
reference: Infor EAM Reliability KPIs; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 14224 Reliability Data Collection

description: Target Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) percentage for assets of this class, representing the combined product of availability, utilisation, and performance rate (e.g., 65.00 for 65%). OEE is the primary productivity benchmark for mining equipment classes used in production reporting and capital justification.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar OEE; ICMM Mining Equipment Performance; ISO 22400 Manufacturing Operations Management KPIs

description: Target utilisation percentage for assets of this class expressed as a percentage of available hours actually used for productive work (e.g., 75.00 for 75%). Distinct from availability — utilisation measures productive use of available time. Used in fleet sizing models and production planning.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Infor EAM Utilisation KPI; ICMM OEE Framework

description: Indicates whether assets of this class are equipped with telematics and real-time monitoring capability integrated with Caterpillar MineStar or OSIsoft PI System. Determines data availability for real-time OEE tracking, predictive maintenance, and fuel consumption monitoring.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: Expected useful life of the asset class expressed in total operating hours as defined by OEM specifications and mine engineering standards (e.g., 80,000 hours for a large haul truck, 120,000 hours for a SAG mill). Drives depreciation schedules, LOM capital replacement planning, and fleet renewal strategies.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; SAP S/4HANA FI-AA Asset Accounting; IFRS 16 Leases; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each asset lifecycle event record in the silver layer. Primary key for the asset_lifecycle_event data product.
Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor, processing equipment) that this lifecycle event applies to. Links to the equipment master register.
reference: Infor EAM Equipment Master; SAP S/4HANA Fixed Asset Register (FI-AA)

description: Foreign key linking to equipment.component_register. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events such as major rebuilds, engine replacements, and component overhauls directly reference specific components tracked in the component_register. The asset_lifecycle_event table cu

Foreign key linking to hse.corrective_action. Business justification: Corrective actions from HSE incidents or audits can mandate asset lifecycle changes (decommission, modification, relocation). Mining MoC and corrective action closure processes require tracing which c

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (transfers, disposals, overhauls) are charged to a cost centre for financial reporting and AISC cost allocation. The event table carries a denormalized cost_centre_code; a prope

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (disposal, revaluation, impairment, transfer) directly trigger fixed asset accounting entries under IFRS/AASB116. The lifecycle event must reference the fixed asset record to dr

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Asset lifecycle events (installation, transfer, decommission) occur at specific functional locations. Mining EAM systems track asset-to-FL history for maintenance history continuity, insurance valuati

Foreign key linking to tenement.heritage_clearance. Business justification: Ground-disturbing asset lifecycle events (commissioning new infrastructure, earthworks) require heritage clearance before proceeding. Mine planning and tenement teams must confirm a valid heritage cle

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Major lifecycle events (commissioning, decommissioning, major overhauls) can trigger incidents (commissioning accidents, decommissioning spills) or be incident outcomes (asset written off after major

Reference to the mine site from which the asset was relocated, applicable when event_type is 'relocation'. Enables tracking of inter-site asset movements for fleet optimisation and fixed asset register updates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA) Asset Transfer; Infor EAM Location History

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_purchase_order. Business justification: Capital expenditure tracking: major asset lifecycle events (acquisition, major overhaul, disposal) are funded by specific purchase orders. Mining capital project reporting requires linking lifecycle e

Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Mining Management of Change (MoC) process mandates a risk assessment before commissioning, decommissioning, or major overhaul of equipment. Links lifecycle events to the governing risk assessment for

Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Major asset lifecycle events (capital rebuilds, major overhauls, decommissions) are executed within planned shutdowns. Linking asset_lifecycle_event to shutdown_plan enables capital expenditure tracki

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Asset commissioning, decommissioning, and transfer events on a tenement must be reported to regulatory authorities as part of tenement compliance and mine closure planning. Tenement managers track whi

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Major lifecycle events (commissioning, major overhauls, decommissioning) may be capitalized and tracked against WBS elements for capital project accounting and asset valuation.
SAP Plant Maintenance or Infor EAM work order number associated with this lifecycle event. Provides traceability to the originating maintenance work order for audit and cost reconciliation purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM); Infor EAM Work Order Management

description: Assessed physical condition of the asset immediately following the lifecycle event, as determined by the maintenance engineer or inspector. Used for insurance valuation, capital planning, and remaining useful life estimation. Ratings: excellent = like-new post-rebuild; good = minor wear; fair = moderate wear requiring monitoring; poor = significant degradation; critical = imminent failure risk.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Condition Assessment; ISO 55001 (Asset Management); ICMM Asset Management Guidelines

description: Total cumulative operating hours recorded on the asset at the time of the lifecycle event (e.g., engine hours at rebuild). Used to calculate Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), validate rebuild intervals, and benchmark against OEM recommended service intervals.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Meter Readings; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) Standards

description: Net book value of the asset on the fixed asset register at the time of the lifecycle event, in the reporting currency. Used for CAPEX capitalisation decisions, impairment testing, and disposal gain/loss calculations.
reference: IAS 16 (Property Plant and Equipment); SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA); IFRS

description: Financial classification of the cost incurred for this lifecycle event. CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) = costs that extend asset life or add capability, capitalised to the fixed asset register; OPEX (Operating Expenditure) = costs expensed in the period. Critical for fixed asset register reconciliation, tax treatment, and financial reporting under IFRS.
reference: IAS 16 (Property Plant and Equipment); IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards); SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA)

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the event_cost amount (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-currency operations across international mining jurisdictions.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: Timestamp when this lifecycle event record was first created in the data platform (silver layer ingestion time). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and reconciliation with source systems.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer Audit Standards; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents

description: Depreciation method applied to the asset following this lifecycle event. May be updated after a major rebuild or change in remaining useful life estimate. Straight_line = equal annual charge; units_of_production = charge based on actual production output; declining_balance = accelerated depreciation.
reference: IAS 16 (Property Plant and Equipment); SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA); IFRS

description: Externally-known business identifier for the lifecycle event as recorded in Infor EAM (e.g., work order number, event reference number). Used for cross-system reconciliation with SAP Fixed Asset Register and insurance systems.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Module; SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA)

description: Total cost incurred for this lifecycle event in the reporting currency. Includes labour, materials, contractor fees, and overheads. Used for CAPEX/OPEX tracking, insurance valuation, and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) calculations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Controlling (CO); IAS 16; AISC (World Gold Council Standard)

description: The actual calendar date on which the lifecycle event occurred or was formally executed (e.g., commissioning date, rebuild completion date, decommissioning date). This is the principal real-world business event date used for fixed asset register updates and insurance valuation.
reference: IAS 16 (Property Plant and Equipment); SAP S/4HANA Asset Accounting (FI-AA); Infor EAM

description: Precise date and time when the lifecycle event work was completed and the asset was returned to service or formally closed. Used to calculate event duration and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) impact.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order; SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM)

description: Free-text field for additional technical or operational notes recorded by the maintenance engineer or planner regarding this lifecycle event (e.g., observed failure mode, special conditions, deviations from standard procedure, OEM recommendations).
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Notes; SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) Long Text

description: Precise date and time when the lifecycle event work commenced (e.g., when the rebuild began, when the asset was powered down for decommissioning). Used for duration calculation and OEE impact analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health

description: Current workflow status of the lifecycle event. Planned = scheduled but not yet started; in_progress = work underway; completed = event fully executed and closed; cancelled = event voided before execution; on_hold = temporarily suspended pending approval or parts.

description: Classification of the significant lifecycle milestone for the asset. The shift classification during which the fleet assignment is active. Typically day or night shift in a 12-hour rotation. Used for shift-based production reporting and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) analysis.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System; SAP S/4HANA HR

description: The mine location code identifying where material is being loaded or work is being performed (dig face, blast block, stockpile). Used for material tracking and production reconciliation in MineStar.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System; Hexagon MinePlan

description: Free-text operational notes recorded by the shift supervisor regarding this fleet assignment, including any special instructions, safety observations, or operational exceptions. Sourced from MineStar dispatch comments.

The target or design payload weight in metric tonnes for the fleet asset during this assignment, based on equipment specifications and mine planning targets. Used to measure payload efficiency and identify overloading or underloading.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System; Hexagon MinePlan

description: The primary operational task type assigned to the mobile fleet asset during this assignment. Classifies the nature of work being performed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haulage|drilling|dozing|blasting_support|loading|grading|water_cart|ancillary — promote to reference product]
Unique surrogate identifier for each fuel dispensing event or shift-end fuel measurement record in the silver layer lakehouse.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Infor EAM

description: Reference to the SAP cost centre to which the fuel cost is allocated. Supports OPEX cost allocation to fleet and C1 cash cost per tonne reporting.

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Fuel spills during dispensing are reportable environmental incidents. Fuel transactions involved in spills, overfills, or contamination events must link to the incident record for regulatory reporting

Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: Fuel consumption is tracked per mine site for GHG emissions reporting, fuel budget management, and cost allocation. mine_site_code is a denormalized plain-text representation of mine_site. A mining op
Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (haul truck, drill, shovel, dozer, etc.) that consumed the fuel. Links to the equipment master register in the equipment domain.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Fuel transactions reference bulk fuel purchase orders (contracts with fuel suppliers); linking enables reconciliation of dispensed fuel against contracted supply, invoice verification, and fuel cost a

Foreign key linking to procurement.material_master. Business justification: Fuel is a procured material managed in the material master (fuel type, unit cost, storage conditions, hazard class). This link enables fuel cost reconciliation against procurement pricing, inventory b

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Fuel costs are a primary component of tenement minimum expenditure commitment obligations under mining licences. Tenement compliance teams allocate fuel expenditure to specific tenements for statutory

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Fuel consumed during capital development projects (mine construction, infrastructure) is capitalized and tracked against WBS elements for project cost control and capitalization accounting.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Fuel issued during maintenance tasks is recorded against work orders in EAM systems for cost capture and AISC allocation. fuel_consumption.work_order_number is a denormalized text reference to mainten

Free-text or coded description of the reason the transaction was flagged as an anomaly (e.g., 'Quantity exceeds tank capacity', 'Location outside mine boundary', 'Duplicate transaction within 30 minutes'). Populated only when is_anomaly_flagged is True.
Name or employee ID of the supervisor or fuel controller who authorised the dispensing event. Required for fuel theft detection controls and audit compliance.

Calculated Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions in kilograms of CO2 equivalent for this fuel consumption event, derived by applying the applicable emissions factor for the fuel type to the quantity dispensed. Used for GRI and ICMM carbon reporting.
reference: GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ICMM; ISO 14001; GHG Protocol Scope 1

description: Timestamp when this fuel consumption record was first created in the silver layer lakehouse. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data pipeline latency.
reference: ISO 14001; IFRS; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fuel cost amounts (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-jurisdiction mining operations where fuel is procured in different currencies.
Reference to the physical location where fuel was dispensed (fuel bay, ROM pad field refuelling point, tank farm). Links to the site location master.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: Physical method by which fuel was delivered to the asset: fixed_bay (permanent fuel bay infrastructure), bowser (mobile tank truck field refuelling), drum (manual drum dispensing for remote equipment), or pipeline (direct pipeline connection at processing plant).
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: The greenhouse gas emissions factor applied to this transaction, in kg CO2e per litre, specific to the fuel type. Captured at transaction time to preserve the factor version used for regulatory reporting and audit purposes.
reference: GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ICMM; ISO 14001; GHG Protocol

description: Cumulative engine hours recorded on the asset at the time of the fuelling event. Used to calculate fuel consumption per engine hour, track MTBF intervals, and schedule preventive maintenance in Infor EAM.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; Infor EAM; MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures)

description: Instantaneous or average fuel consumption rate in litres per engine hour at the time of the fuelling event or measurement period. Sourced from MineStar telematics. Used for OEE analysis and fleet fuel efficiency benchmarking.
Physical or electronic docket/receipt number issued at the point of dispensing. Used for three-way reconciliation between FMS records, physical dockets, and SAP purchase orders.
reference: Pronto Xi Procurement; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Type of fuel dispensed: diesel (standard ultra-low sulphur diesel), LNG (liquefied natural gas for LNG-powered haul trucks), biodiesel B20 blend (20% biodiesel), biodiesel B100 (pure biodiesel), or HVO (hydrotreated vegetable oil). Drives emissions factor selection for Scope 1 carbon accounting.
reference: ISO 14001; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ICMM

description: WGS84 decimal latitude of the fuelling event location as recorded by MineStar telematics or the FMS. Used for GIS-based fuel consumption heat mapping and field refuelling route optimisation.
WGS84 decimal longitude of the fuelling event location as recorded by MineStar telematics or the FMS. Used in conjunction with gps_latitude for spatial analysis of fuel consumption patterns.

Boolean flag set to True when the fuel transaction has been identified as a potential anomaly by automated detection rules (e.g., quantity dispensed exceeds tank capacity, fill rate outside normal range, location mismatch). Triggers investigation workflow for fuel theft detection.

Cumulative distance travelled in kilometres recorded on the asset at the time of the fuelling event. Applicable to mobile fleet (haul trucks, light vehicles). Null for fixed plant or tracked equipment without odometers.

Volume of fuel dispensed during this transaction, measured in litres to three decimal places. The primary quantitative fact for OPEX cost allocation, C1 cash cost per tonne calculation, and Scope 1 emissions accounting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; ICMM; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: The native primary key or transaction identifier from the originating source system (MineStar, FMS, PI System, or Infor EAM). Retained for data lineage, deduplication, and reconciliation back to the system of record.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; Infor EAM

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this fuel consumption record was ingested: MineStar Fleet Management, a standalone Fuel Management System (FMS), OSIsoft PI System SCADA, Infor EAM, or manual entry. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; Infor EAM

description: Maximum rated fuel tank capacity of the asset in litres. Used to calculate fill percentage and validate that dispensed quantity does not exceed physical tank capacity.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Fuel level in the asset's onboard tank immediately after the dispensing event, in litres. Used with tank_level_before_litres to validate dispensed quantity and detect discrepancies indicative of fuel theft.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: Fuel level in the asset's onboard tank immediately before the dispensing event, in litres. Used with tank_level_after_litres to validate dispensed quantity and detect fuel theft or leakage anomalies.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: Total monetary cost of fuel dispensed in this transaction (quantity_dispensed_litres × unit_cost_per_litre), in the site operating currency. Used for OPEX cost allocation to fleet and C1 cash cost per tonne reporting. Note: this is a transaction-level cost capture, not a derived aggregate.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS; OPEX (Operating Expenditure); C1 (C1 Cash Cost)

description: Externally-known unique reference number assigned by the Fuel Management System (FMS) or MineStar to identify this dispensing event or measurement. Used for reconciliation with source systems and audit trails.

Current workflow state of the fuel transaction record. Confirmed transactions are included in OPEX and emissions reporting. Voided or disputed records are excluded from cost allocation until resolved.

The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the fuel dispensing event commenced or the shift-end measurement was recorded. This is the principal real-world event time used for time-series analysis and Scope 1 emissions accounting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; ISO 14001

description: Classifies the nature of the fuel record: a discrete refuelling event at a fuel bay (refuel), a periodic shift-end fuel measurement (shift_measurement), a manual tank dip reading (tank_dip), a field refuelling by bowser (field_refuel), or a bulk fuel transfer between storage tanks (bulk_transfer).

Contracted or spot price paid per litre of fuel at the time of the transaction, in the site operating currency. Confidential commercial pricing data. Used for OPEX cost allocation and C1 cash cost per tonne calculation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Pronto Xi Procurement; AISC (All-In Sustaining Cost); C1 (C1 Cash Cost)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this fuel consumption record in the silver layer. Tracks corrections, status changes, and anomaly flag updates for audit compliance.
reference: ISO 14001; IFRS; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer

description: Unique system-generated identifier for each individual truck payload cycle record (load-haul-dump event) as captured by Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management.

Foreign key linking to sales.commodity_order. Business justification: Production-to-sales reconciliation: mining operations reconcile actual payload cycles (tonnes moved) against commodity orders to verify delivery commitments are met. Mine controllers and sales teams u

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Haulage cycles generate costs allocated to cost centres based on material type (ore, waste) and destination for mining cost accounting and cost per tonne reporting.

Foreign key linking to equipment.fleet_assignment. Business justification: Each payload cycle (individual load-haul-dump cycle for a haul truck) occurs within the context of a specific fleet assignment. Fleet assignments define the operational context — which asset is assign

Foreign key linking to geology.grade_control_sample. Business justification: Each truck payload cycle is directed to crusher, stockpile, or waste dump based on grade control sample results. Ore reconciliation and grade control reporting require linking payload cycles to the gr

Foreign key linking to mine.haulage_cycle. Business justification: Payload cycles (FMS/dispatch system) and haulage cycles (mine production system) represent the same physical truck cycle from two system perspectives. Production reconciliation requires matching these
Reference to the haul truck or loader asset that performed this payload cycle, linking to the equipment master register in the equipment domain.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Infor EAM

description: Reference to the destination location where the truck dumped its load during this cycle (e.g., ROM pad, crusher, waste dump, stockpile). Links to the mine operations location master.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; Hexagon MinePlan

description: Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each haul cycle transports a specific saleable product. Production reconciliation, grade control, and stockpile accounting require product-level cycle tracking. Material_type and commodity are denorma

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Payload cycles are the source data for royalty calculation — royalty_payment.production_tonnage is derived from payload cycles per tenement. Royalty obligations are tenement-specific; mine accountants

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Payload cycles delivering construction material for TSF raises must reference the destination TSF for raise progress tracking, cost allocation, and volume reconciliation against tsf_raise records. dum
The date and time when this payload cycle record was first created in the data platform, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the payload cycle was completed, typically recorded as the moment the truck completed the dump and cleared the dump point in MineStar.
Externally-known alphanumeric reference number assigned by MineStar Payload Management to uniquely identify this load-haul-dump cycle for cross-system reconciliation and reporting.
The date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) when the payload cycle commenced, typically recorded as the moment the truck arrived at the load point or the loading sequence began in MineStar.

Current lifecycle status of the payload cycle record. Completed indicates a full load-haul-dump sequence was executed. Partial indicates the cycle was interrupted. Aborted indicates the cycle was terminated before dump. Cancelled indicates the cycle was voided.

Total elapsed time for the complete load-haul-dump cycle in minutes, from arrival at the load point to completion of the dump. Sourced from MineStar cycle time tracking. Key input for haulage productivity analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; OEE Standard

description: Indicates the data quality status of this payload cycle record as assessed during ingestion into the Silver layer. Valid indicates the record passed all quality checks. Suspect indicates anomalies detected (e.g., payload outside expected range). Invalid indicates the record failed quality checks. Corrected indicates the record was manually reviewed and adjusted.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; JORC Code 2012 Data Quality

description: Time in minutes taken to complete the dump operation at the dump point during this cycle, from arrival at the dump to departure. Used to identify dump point congestion and assess dump efficiency.

Volume of diesel fuel consumed in litres during this payload cycle as recorded by the truck's on-board fuel monitoring system integrated with MineStar. Used for fuel cost allocation, OPEX tracking, and carbon emissions reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ISO 14001

description: Distance in metres travelled by the truck empty from the dump point back to the load point during this cycle, as recorded by MineStar GPS/telematics. Used for total cycle distance calculation and fuel consumption analysis.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; GIS Standards

description: Distance in metres travelled by the truck from the load point to the dump point while carrying a full load during this cycle, as recorded by MineStar GPS/telematics. Used for haulage cost modelling and route optimisation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; GIS Standards

description: Time in minutes the truck spent travelling from the load point to the dump point while carrying a full load during this cycle. Used for haulage route analysis and cycle time optimisation.
Time in minutes taken to load the truck at the dig face or load point during this cycle, from first bucket to last bucket. Used to assess loader productivity and number of passes per load.
The native cycle identifier assigned by Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management system at the source. Retained for traceability and reconciliation back to the operational system of record.

The number of bucket or dipper passes (swings) made by the loading unit to fill the truck to the target payload during this cycle. Used to assess loader efficiency and optimise pass-matching strategy.

Full name of the equipment operator who performed this payload cycle as recorded in MineStar at the time of the cycle. Retained for operational reporting and shift reconciliation where the operator ID may not be immediately resolvable.

Indicates whether the truck was loaded beyond its rated payload capacity (overloaded) during this cycle as determined by MineStar payload monitoring. True indicates an overload event. Used for truck health monitoring, tyre management, and compliance with OEM load limits.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: The method used to measure the payload weight for this cycle. Onboard scale uses the truck's integrated weighing system. Strut pressure uses suspension strut pressure sensors. Load cell uses a fixed weighbridge. Estimated indicates a calculated value. Manual indicates a manually entered value. Affects data quality confidence for ROM reconciliation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; JORC Code 2012

description: The difference between the actual payload weight and the target payload weight in metric tonnes (actual minus target). Positive values indicate overloading; negative values indicate underloading. Used for truck health monitoring and haulage efficiency analysis. Note: this is a raw recorded field from MineStar, not a derived KPI.

The measured weight of material loaded onto the truck during this cycle, expressed in metric tonnes as recorded by the on-board payload measurement system (OBMS) in MineStar. This is the principal quantitative fact for the cycle and the primary input to ROM tonnage reconciliation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; JORC Code 2012

description: The calendar date on which this payload cycle was executed, used for daily production reporting, ROM tonnage reconciliation against the Life of Mine (LOM) plan, and shift-based aggregation.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; JORC Code 2012; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Time in minutes the truck spent waiting in queue at the load point before loading commenced during this cycle. Used to identify loader bottlenecks, optimise truck-shovel matching, and reduce non-productive time.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; Fleet Management System (FMS)

description: Time in minutes the truck spent travelling empty from the dump point back to the load point during this cycle. Used for haulage route analysis and cycle time component breakdown.
Classification of the production shift during which this cycle occurred (day, night, or afternoon). Used for shift-comparative productivity analysis and fatigue risk management.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: The planned or nominal target payload weight in metric tonnes for this truck model and material type as configured in MineStar. Used to calculate payload variance and assess overloading or underloading against truck rated capacity.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Payload Management; OEM Truck Specifications

description: Human-readable fleet identification number assigned to the haul truck or loader (e.g., HT-042, LD-017) as registered in the Fleet Management System (FMS). Used for operational reporting and shift reconciliation.
The date and time when this payload cycle record was last modified in the data platform, used for change tracking and incremental data processing.
Primary key for telemetry_event
Reference to the master equipment or fleet asset record that generated this telemetry event. Links to the equipment master register in the Equipment domain.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Geofence breaches, overspeed events, and fault codes in telemetry directly trigger HSE incidents. Post-incident investigation requires linking the triggering telemetry event to the incident record for
Reference to the mine site or operational location where the equipment was operating when this telemetry event was captured.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Hexagon MinePlan Mine Design

description: Reference to the geofence zone in which the equipment was located at the time of the telemetry event. Used for geofence compliance monitoring, blast exclusion zone enforcement, and haulage route adherence.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; GIS (Geographic Information System); MSHA Mine Safety and Health Administration

description: Foreign key linking to tailings.waste_rock_dump. Business justification: Telemetry from equipment operating at waste rock dumps (dozers, compactors, haul trucks) requires WRD attribution for geofence compliance monitoring, operational reporting, and environmental boundary

Ambient air temperature in degrees Celsius recorded by the equipment's external temperature sensor at the time of the telemetry event. Used for equipment performance normalisation and heat stress risk assessment under ISO 45001.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Real-time Monitoring; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Onboard electrical system battery voltage in volts at the time of the telemetry event. Low voltage readings indicate potential electrical system faults and are used to trigger preventive maintenance actions in Infor EAM.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Preventive Maintenance

description: Indicates whether the service brake was applied at the time of the telemetry event. Used for operator behaviour analysis, brake wear monitoring, and safety compliance reporting under ISO 45001.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Engine coolant temperature in degrees Celsius at the time of the telemetry event. A critical health indicator; values exceeding thresholds trigger predictive maintenance alerts and potential equipment shutdown.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System Real-time Monitoring

description: Quality assessment flag applied during silver layer processing to indicate the reliability of this telemetry record. Suspect or invalid records may result from sensor malfunction, communication dropout, or out-of-range values. Used to filter records in analytics and predictive maintenance models.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Process Data Historian; ISO 8000 Data Quality

description: Altitude of the equipment above mean sea level in metres at the time of the telemetry event. Used for bench-level positioning, grade resistance calculations, and mine design alignment.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Hexagon MinePlan Mine Design

description: Cumulative engine operating hours recorded on the equipment odometer at the time of this telemetry event. Primary driver for preventive maintenance scheduling and Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) calculations in Infor EAM.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Preventive Maintenance; MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures)

description: Engine load as a percentage of maximum rated capacity at the time of the telemetry event. Indicates whether the equipment is operating within design parameters. Used for OEE and fuel consumption analysis.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness)

description: Instantaneous engine speed in revolutions per minute at the time of the telemetry event. Used for engine load analysis, fuel efficiency monitoring, and predictive maintenance trigger detection.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System Process Data Historian

description: The operational state of the equipment at the moment of this telemetry event as reported by the Fleet Management System (FMS). Supports Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and utilisation calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operating|idle|standby|down|tramming|loading|dumping — promote to reference product]
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) standard

description: The precise date and time (UTC) at which the sensor snapshot or event trigger was recorded by the onboard telematics system. Sub-minute frequency. This is the principal real-world event time, distinct from ingestion audit timestamps.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Process Data Historian; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health

description: Discriminator classifying the nature of the telemetry event. Periodic snapshots are time-driven; threshold breach, engine, geofence, payload, and fault code events are trigger-driven. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: periodic_snapshot|threshold_breach|engine_event|geofence_event|payload_event|fault_code_event — promote to reference product]
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System

description: Diagnostic fault or alarm code generated by the equipment's onboard diagnostic system (OBD/J1939) at the time of the event. Null for periodic snapshots with no active fault. Used to trigger work orders in Infor EAM and predictive maintenance workflows.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Work Orders; SAE J1939 Diagnostic Standard

description: Severity classification of the fault code raised at the time of this telemetry event. Critical and high severity faults may trigger immediate equipment shutdown or dispatch of maintenance crew. Null when no fault is active.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; SAE J1939

description: Instantaneous fuel burn rate in litres per hour as reported by the onboard fuel flow sensor at the time of the telemetry event. Used for OPEX (Operating Expenditure) monitoring and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) calculations.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; AISC (All-In Sustaining Cost); OPEX (Operating Expenditure)

description: Current fuel tank level expressed as a percentage of total tank capacity at the time of the telemetry event. Used for fuel management, dispatch planning, and refuelling schedule optimisation. Distinct from discrete fuel dispensing events recorded in fuel_consumption.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; OSIsoft PI System

description: Indicates whether the equipment was in breach of an active geofence boundary at the time of this telemetry event. True when the equipment has entered a restricted or exclusion zone without authorisation. Supports safety compliance and HSE reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; IsoMetrix HSE Incident Management; ISO 45001

description: Compass bearing of the equipment direction of travel in degrees (0–360) at the time of the telemetry event. Used for directional analysis and geofence zone entry/exit detection.

Hydraulic system pressure in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Used for monitoring hydraulic system health on shovels, drills, and haul trucks. Anomalies indicate potential hydraulic failure.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System Process Data Historian

description: The date and time at which this telemetry record was received and loaded into the lakehouse silver layer from the source system (MineStar, Wenco, Modular Mining, or OSIsoft PI). Used for data latency monitoring and pipeline audit.
reference: OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration; Caterpillar MineStar

description: WGS84 decimal degrees latitude of the equipment position at the time of the telemetry event. Sourced from onboard GPS unit. Used for geofence compliance, haulage route analysis, and GIS integration.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; GIS (Geographic Information System); WGS84

description: WGS84 decimal degrees longitude of the equipment position at the time of the telemetry event. Sourced from onboard GPS unit. Used for geofence compliance, haulage route analysis, and GIS integration.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; GIS (Geographic Information System); WGS84

description: Cumulative distance travelled by the equipment in kilometres as recorded on the onboard odometer at the time of the telemetry event. Used for tyre life management, component replacement scheduling, and haulage distance reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Instantaneous payload weight in metric tonnes as measured by the onboard payload monitoring system at the time of the telemetry event. Used for haulage productivity analysis, truck factor compliance, and ROM (Run of Mine) tonnage tracking.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; ROM (Run of Mine)

description: Quality classification of the wireless or satellite communication signal at the time the telemetry event was transmitted. Used to assess data reliability and identify underground or remote areas with poor connectivity.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; OSIsoft PI System SCADA Integration

description: The originating operational system or IoT integration platform that produced this telemetry event record. Supports data lineage and multi-source reconciliation across MineStar, Wenco, Modular Mining, and OSIsoft PI.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; SCADA Integration

description: Instantaneous travel speed of the equipment in kilometres per hour at the time of the telemetry event. Used for speed limit compliance monitoring, operator behaviour analysis, and haulage cycle time analysis.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; MSHA Mine Safety and Health Administration speed compliance

description: Current transmission gear position of the equipment at the time of the telemetry event. Used for operator behaviour analysis, ramp grade compliance, and drivetrain health monitoring.
Tyre pressure of the front-left tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Tyre pressure monitoring is critical for haul truck safety and tyre life management on large mining vehicles.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Tyre pressure of the front-right tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Tyre pressure monitoring is critical for haul truck safety and tyre life management on large mining vehicles.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Tyre pressure of the rear-left tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Rear tyres on haul trucks carry the majority of loaded payload weight and are subject to higher wear and failure risk.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Tyre pressure of the rear-right tyre in kilopascals at the time of the telemetry event. Rear tyres on haul trucks carry the majority of loaded payload weight and are subject to higher wear and failure risk.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety Management

description: Reference to the associated HSE incident record in IsoMetrix when is_safety_related is True. Links the downtime event to the formal safety investigation and regulatory reporting workflow. Null when no safety incident is associated.

description: Reference to the mine site or operational location where the downtime event occurred (e.g., open pit, underground, processing plant).

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Downtime events record production_tonnes_lost and production_impact_hours, which must be attributed to a production schedule period for schedule variance reporting and forecast updates. Mine planners

Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Stop-work orders and compliance breaches (tenement regulatory conditions) directly cause equipment downtime. Mine compliance teams must link downtime events to the specific regulatory condition that t

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Production loss reporting requires knowing which saleable product was impacted by each downtime event. Fields production_tonnes_lost and production_impact_hours are meaningless without product context

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Regulatory production-loss reporting to mining authorities requires downtime events to be attributed to the specific tenement licence area affected. Mine compliance teams query downtime directly by te
