Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Major repair and overhaul costs exceeding capitalisation thresholds are charged to WBS elements as CAPEX rather than OPEX. Downtime events triggering major component replacements must reference the WB
Reference to the Infor EAM maintenance work order raised in response to or associated with this downtime event. Null for non-maintenance downtime categories such as weather or standby.

Actual number of labour hours expended to complete the repair or maintenance activity for this downtime event. Recorded upon event closure. Used to calculate Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) and to benchmark against estimated_repair_hours for continuous improvement.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Actual Hours; ISO 22400 (MTTR); SAP S/4HANA PM

description: The date and time at which this downtime event record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or Caterpillar MineStar) and ingested into the silver layer. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance monitoring.
reference: Infor EAM — Record Audit; Caterpillar MineStar; ISO 55000

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the maintenance_cost field (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency operations across international mine sites.

Method by which the downtime event was first detected. Operator_report indicates the equipment operator raised the fault. SCADA_alarm indicates detection via OSIsoft PI System or PLC alarm. FMS_alert indicates detection via Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management System. Condition_monitoring indicates detection via predictive maintenance sensors. Scheduled_inspection indicates detection during a planned inspection round. Third_party_report indicates detection reported by a contractor or external party.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System (SCADA); Infor EAM — Condition Monitoring

description: High-level classification of the downtime event type. Unplanned breakdown indicates an unexpected mechanical or electrical failure. Planned maintenance covers scheduled preventive or corrective maintenance windows. Standby covers equipment waiting for work assignment. Weather delay covers stoppages due to adverse environmental conditions. Operational delay covers delays caused by operational constraints such as blasting exclusion zones. Process delay covers upstream or downstream process bottlenecks.
reference: Infor EAM — Downtime Category; ISO 22400 (OEE Definitions); Caterpillar MineStar

description: Standardised reason code identifying the specific cause of the downtime event (e.g., engine failure, tyre change, hydraulic leak, conveyor belt tear, electrical fault). Sourced from Infor EAM failure code library and Caterpillar MineStar reason code catalogue. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine_failure|tyre_change|hydraulic_leak|conveyor_belt_tear|electrical_fault|structural_damage|operator_error|fuel_system|cooling_system|brake_system — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM — Failure Code Library; Caterpillar MineStar Reason Codes; ISO 14224 (Reliability Data Collection)

description: Current lifecycle status of the downtime event record. Open indicates the event has been logged but resolution has not commenced. In_progress indicates maintenance or corrective action is underway. Resolved indicates the equipment has been returned to service. Cancelled indicates the event was logged in error. Pending_approval indicates the event record is awaiting supervisory sign-off.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Status; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Total elapsed duration of the downtime event expressed in whole minutes, calculated as the difference between end_timestamp and start_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support direct reporting and MTTR calculations without requiring timestamp arithmetic at query time. Null if end_timestamp is not yet recorded.
reference: ISO 22400 — Mean Time To Repair (MTTR); Infor EAM; Caterpillar MineStar

description: The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the equipment was returned to operational service and the downtime event concluded. Null if the event is still open or in progress. Used in conjunction with start_timestamp to calculate actual downtime duration.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar — Equipment Health; Infor EAM; ISO 22400

description: Indicates whether this downtime event resulted in or had the potential to cause an environmental impact (e.g., hydraulic fluid spill, dust emission exceedance, tailings pump failure). True triggers environmental notification and reporting obligations under ISO 14001 and site Environmental Impact Statement (EIS) conditions.
reference: IsoMetrix — Environmental Monitoring; ISO 14001 (Environmental Management Systems); EIS Conditions

description: Estimated number of labour hours required to complete the repair or maintenance activity associated with this downtime event, as assessed at the time of event creation. Used for maintenance scheduling, resource planning, and comparison against actual repair duration to improve future estimates.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Estimated Hours; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Free-text narrative description of the downtime event as recorded by the operator or maintenance technician at the time of reporting. Captures contextual details not covered by structured fields, including observed symptoms, environmental conditions, and initial diagnosis. Sourced from Infor EAM notification text or Caterpillar MineStar operator notes.
reference: Infor EAM — Notification Long Text; Caterpillar MineStar Operator Notes

description: Externally-known business identifier for the downtime event, formatted as DT-YYYY-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with Infor EAM work orders, MineStar dispatch logs, and SAP PM notifications. Supports traceability across operational systems.
reference: Infor EAM — Downtime Notification Reference; Caterpillar MineStar

description: Describes the specific manner in which the equipment failed or the mechanism of the downtime (e.g., fatigue fracture, corrosion, overheating, wear, contamination, misalignment). Aligned with ISO 14224 failure mode taxonomy and used in Reliability Centred Maintenance (RCM) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fatigue_fracture|corrosion|overheating|wear|contamination|misalignment|vibration|leakage|blockage|electrical_short — promote to reference product]
reference: ISO 14224 — Reliability and Maintenance Data Collection; Infor EAM RCM Module

description: Indicates whether this downtime event was caused by or resulted in a safety incident, near-miss, or equipment condition that posed a risk to personnel. True triggers mandatory notification to the Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) team and may require IsoMetrix incident reporting. Supports Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and Total Recordable Injury Frequency Rate (TRIFR) compliance reporting.
reference: IsoMetrix — HSE Incident Management; ISO 45001 (Occupational Health and Safety); MSHA Regulations

description: Total direct cost incurred to resolve this downtime event, including labour, parts, and consumables as captured in Infor EAM and SAP S/4HANA CO. Expressed in the site operating currency. Used for Operating Expenditure (OPEX) tracking, cost-per-hour analysis, and maintenance budget variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO — Cost Object; Infor EAM — Work Order Cost; IFRS (Operating Expenditure)

description: Classification of the maintenance activity associated with this downtime event. Corrective maintenance addresses failures after they occur. Preventive maintenance is time-based scheduled work. Predictive maintenance is triggered by condition monitoring data. Condition-based maintenance is triggered by measured parameter thresholds. Emergency maintenance addresses safety-critical failures requiring immediate response. Null for non-maintenance downtime categories.
reference: Infor EAM — Maintenance Type Classification; ISO 14224; ISO 55000 (Asset Management)

description: Classification of how this downtime event impacts the Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation. Availability losses arise from unplanned breakdowns and planned maintenance. Performance losses arise from reduced speed or throughput. Quality losses arise from off-spec production. Used to decompose OEE into its three constituent factors for targeted improvement initiatives.
reference: ISO 22400 — Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE); Caterpillar MineStar; ICMM

description: Priority classification assigned to the downtime event to drive maintenance resource allocation and response urgency. Critical indicates immediate safety or production-stopping impact. High indicates significant production impact requiring same-shift response. Medium indicates moderate impact with next-shift response acceptable. Low indicates minor impact with scheduled response acceptable.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Priority; SAP S/4HANA PM Priority Classification

description: Estimated or actual number of production hours lost as a direct consequence of this downtime event. Accounts for the impact on the production schedule and is used to quantify the effect on Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and Life of Mine (LOM) plan adherence. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar productivity tracking.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar — Productivity Tracking; ISO 22400 (OEE); ICMM Reporting

description: Estimated tonnage of ore or material (Run of Mine — ROM) that was not moved or processed as a direct result of this downtime event. Used to quantify the financial and operational impact of equipment unavailability on the mine production schedule.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar — Productivity Tracking; Mine Production Schedule (Deswik/Hexagon MinePlan)

description: Free-text description of the corrective actions taken to resolve the downtime event and return the equipment to service. Recorded by the maintenance technician upon event closure. Supports knowledge management, recurring fault analysis, and Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) improvement.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Completion Notes; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: The precise date and time at which the equipment was formally cleared and returned to operational service following resolution of the downtime event. May differ from end_timestamp if post-repair inspection or commissioning steps are required before the equipment is released to operations.
reference: Infor EAM — Work Order Completion; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health

description: Status of the formal Root Cause Analysis (RCA) investigation for this downtime event. Not_required for minor or routine events. Pending indicates RCA has been triggered but not yet commenced. In_progress indicates investigation is underway. Completed indicates findings have been documented and corrective actions assigned. Mandatory for critical and high-priority events per site maintenance governance standards.
reference: Infor EAM — RCA Workflow; ISO 14224; ICMM Maintenance Excellence Framework

description: Classification of the shift during which the downtime event commenced (day, afternoon, or night). Stored directly on the event to support shift-level reporting without requiring a join to the shift master when shift_id is unavailable.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR — Shift Classification; Caterpillar MineStar

description: The precise date and time (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) at which the equipment became unavailable or the downtime event commenced. This is the principal real-world event time used for Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculations. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar or OSIsoft PI System historian.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar — Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System; ISO 22400 (MTBF/MTTR)

description: The date and time at which this downtime event record was most recently modified in the source system or silver layer. Used for change data capture, incremental load processing, and audit trail compliance.
reference: Infor EAM — Record Audit; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 55000

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each equipment inspection record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this data product. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — a discrete, lifecycle-managed business event (pre-start or periodic inspection) with regulatory standing.
reference: Infor EAM; IsoMetrix; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Reference to the equipment asset (haul truck, drill, shovel, conveyor, processing plant unit, etc.) that was inspected. Links to the equipment master register sourced from Infor EAM and Caterpillar MineStar. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE / MASTER_RESOURCE category.

Foreign key linking to hse.audit. Business justification: Equipment inspections are frequently scheduled as part of formal HSE audits (ISO 45001, regulatory compliance audits). Linking inspections to the parent audit enables audit finding aggregation, correc

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Inspection costs (labor, materials) are allocated to cost centres for maintenance budget tracking and compliance cost reporting in mining operations.

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Statutory and regulatory inspections in mining are tied to functional locations for compliance reporting (e.g., inspect crusher FL-PROC-001). inspection.location_description is a denormalized text fie

Foreign key linking to hse.hazard. Business justification: Pre-start and statutory equipment inspections in mining identify specific hazards from the hazard register. Linking inspection findings directly to hazard records enables hazard trend analysis, bow-ti

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Incident ID
description: Reference to an associated HSE incident record in IsoMetrix if this inspection was triggered by or linked to a safety incident (e.g., post-incident inspection). Null for routine pre-start and periodic inspections.
Reference to the mine site or operational location where the inspection was conducted. Supports site-level compliance reporting and regulatory submissions per jurisdiction.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; IsoMetrix; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.pm_schedule. Business justification: Inspections are generated from PM schedules in mining EAM systems. PM compliance reporting requires tracing each inspection back to the originating PM schedule without traversing work_order. Regulator
Self-referencing FK on inspection (previous_inspection_id)

Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Equipment inspections are frequently mandated by specific tenement regulatory conditions (regulatory_condition.inspection_required_flag). Compliance teams must demonstrate that each inspection satisfi

Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Equipment inspections identify hazards (worn components, safety system failures, structural defects) that feed into risk assessments. Inspection findings directly inform residual risk ratings and cont

Foreign key linking to maintenance.standard_job. Business justification: Inspection checklists in mining EAM are defined as standard job plans. Linking inspection to standard_job enables checklist compliance tracking, revision control (knowing which job plan version was us

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Equipment inspections mandated by tenement regulatory conditions must be traceable to the tenement licence area. Regulatory compliance audits cross-reference inspection records against tenement licenc

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Equipment inspections performed at a TSF (decant infrastructure, pumps, instrumentation) must reference the specific TSF for regulatory dam safety audit trails and facility-level compliance reporting.

Reference to the corrective maintenance work order generated in Infor EAM as a result of defect findings identified during this inspection. Null if no corrective action was required or work order has not yet been raised.
Date and time when the supervising officer approved the inspection record. Null if not yet approved or approval not required. Supports regulatory audit trails for statutory inspections.

Number of checklist items that were assessed and recorded during this inspection. A value less than checklist_items_total may indicate an incomplete inspection, which is a compliance risk for pre-start and statutory inspections.
reference: IsoMetrix; Infor EAM; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001

description: Total number of checklist items included in the inspection for this equipment class and inspection type. Used to calculate completion rate and to validate that all mandatory items were assessed.
reference: IsoMetrix; Infor EAM; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Indicates whether one or more defect findings from this inspection require a corrective action to be raised. True triggers work order generation in Infor EAM and/or a corrective action task in IsoMetrix. Critical for HSE compliance workflow integration.
reference: IsoMetrix; Infor EAM; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001

description: Timestamp when this inspection record was first created in the source system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.

Number of checklist items rated as critical severity during this inspection. Critical defects require immediate equipment shutdown and corrective action before operation can resume. A value greater than zero should result in a fail overall result.

Number of checklist items rated as major severity during this inspection. Major defects require corrective action within a defined timeframe but may allow conditional operation under specified controls.
Number of checklist items rated as minor severity during this inspection. Minor defects are recorded for monitoring and scheduled maintenance but do not prevent equipment operation.

Date and time when the inspector completed and submitted the inspection. Used to calculate inspection duration and to confirm the inspection was completed before equipment operation commenced (pre-start compliance).

Classification of the equipment type being inspected. Determines which checklist template applies and which regulatory requirements govern the inspection. Sourced from the equipment master register in Infor EAM and Caterpillar MineStar. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: haul_truck|drill|shovel|excavator|dozer|grader|conveyor|crusher|processing_plant|light_vehicle — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM; Caterpillar MineStar; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Indicates whether the equipment was taken out of service (grounded) as a result of this inspection. True means the equipment must not be operated until corrective action is completed and a return-to-service inspection is passed. Directly impacts fleet availability and OEE calculations.
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001; Caterpillar MineStar; Infor EAM

description: Engine or operating hours recorded on the equipment at the time of inspection. Used to determine whether periodic maintenance-based inspections are due and to track equipment utilisation context at the time of the inspection. Sourced from Caterpillar MineStar telematics.
reference: Infor EAM; Caterpillar MineStar; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Human-readable fleet number or asset tag of the equipment being inspected (e.g., HT-042, DR-007). Denormalized from the equipment master for operational convenience and to support inspection records even when the equipment master FK is temporarily unavailable.

Method by which the inspection was captured and submitted. Mobile app refers to field-based digital capture. Paper form indicates a manual record subsequently digitised. Desktop portal is a web-based entry. Integrated OBC (on-board computer) refers to direct capture from Caterpillar MineStar on-board systems.

Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the inspection record as assigned by the source system (IsoMetrix or Infor EAM). Used for cross-system reference, regulatory submissions, and audit trails. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: Infor EAM; IsoMetrix; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001

description: Current workflow state of the inspection record. Draft indicates the inspection is in progress or not yet submitted. Submitted means the inspector has completed and lodged the record. Approved indicates supervisor sign-off. Rejected means the record was returned for correction. Cancelled applies to inspections that were voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.

Classification of the inspection by its regulatory or operational trigger. Pre-start inspections are legally mandated before equipment operation in most mining jurisdictions. Periodic inspections occur at scheduled intervals. Post-incident inspections follow a safety event. Statutory inspections are required by specific mining regulations. Return-to-service inspections occur after maintenance or repair.
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001; WHS Act (Australia); IsoMetrix

description: Free-text narrative comments provided by the inspector to describe defect findings, operational context, or observations not captured by the structured checklist items. Supports incident investigation and root cause analysis.

Competency or certification code held by the inspector that authorises them to conduct this class of inspection (e.g., operator licence number, statutory inspector certificate). Required for regulatory compliance verification. Sourced from SAP HR competency records.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001; WHS Act (Australia)

description: Role of the person conducting the inspection. Operators conduct pre-start checks. Maintainers conduct post-maintenance checks. Supervisors conduct periodic and statutory inspections. Statutory inspectors are government-appointed or certified inspectors. Third-party inspectors are external contractors.

Odometer reading in kilometres recorded at the time of inspection, applicable to mobile equipment such as haul trucks and light vehicles. Null for fixed plant and equipment without odometers. Supports distance-based maintenance scheduling.

Aggregate pass/fail outcome of the inspection. Pass indicates all checklist items met the required standard. Fail indicates one or more critical or major defects were found that prevent safe operation. Conditional pass indicates minor defects noted but equipment may operate under specified conditions pending corrective action.
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001; IsoMetrix; Infor EAM

description: Number of photographic evidence items attached to this inspection record to document defect findings. Supports regulatory audit requirements and insurance claims. Photos are stored in the document management system with references captured at the checklist item level.

The specific mining safety regulation or standard under which this inspection is mandated or conducted. Enables jurisdiction-specific compliance reporting and regulatory submissions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: MSHA_30CFR56|MSHA_30CFR57|WHS_ACT|DMIRS_WA|MINES_ACT_QLD|ISO_45001 — promote to reference product]
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57; ISO 45001; WHS Act (Australia); ICMM

description: The shift during which the inspection was conducted. Pre-start inspections are typically conducted at the commencement of each shift. Used for shift-level compliance reporting and to correlate inspection outcomes with shift patterns.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR; IsoMetrix; MSHA 30 CFR Part 56/57

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this inspection record was ingested into the lakehouse silver layer. Supports data lineage, reconciliation, and troubleshooting of ETL pipelines.

The native primary key or record identifier of this inspection in the originating source system (IsoMetrix, Infor EAM, or mobile app). Enables back-referencing to the source system for reconciliation and audit purposes.

Date and time when the inspector commenced the inspection. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category. Critical for regulatory compliance — pre-start inspections must be completed before equipment operation commences.

Timestamp when this inspection record was last modified in the source system. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category. Tracks amendments, supervisor approvals, and corrective action updates.
Unique surrogate identifier for each OEE composite metric record. Primary key for the oee_record data product in the equipment domain.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Performance Module; Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking

description: Reference to the mining fleet or fixed plant asset (e.g., haul truck, drill, shovel, crusher, conveyor) for which this OEE record is computed. Sourced from Infor EAM asset register and Caterpillar MineStar equipment master.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Module; Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management

description: Reference to the SAP cost centre responsible for this asset during the OEE period. Used for OPEX allocation and budget vs actual reporting against fleet performance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Reference to the mine site or operational location where the asset is deployed. Enables site-level OEE benchmarking and multi-site fleet performance comparison.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: OEE is measured against production schedule targets (throughput_target_t on oee_record derives from the schedule). Schedule vs actual OEE variance reporting is a core mine planning KPI. A mine planner

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: OEE targets and performance vary by product line in multi-product mining operations (iron ore lump vs fines circuits, different coal products). Production reporting and continuous improvement initiati

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Equipment OEE records for processing plant assets must be reconciled against the processing shift production run for the same period. This cross-domain reconciliation — matching equipment availability

Foreign key linking to mine.shift_report. Business justification: OEE records are generated per shift period and feed directly into shift report KPIs (equipment_utilisation_pct, equipment_operating_hours on shift_report). Mine supervisors reconcile OEE data against

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: OEE quality_rate is measured against a product specification in processing plants. Without this FK, quality performance reporting cannot be tied to contractual or customer-specific specs. Mining plant

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: OEE metrics are tracked by tenement for performance reporting and expenditure commitment validation. Real mining operations report productivity by licence area to demonstrate compliance with minimum e

The measured average time per production unit (e.g., haul cycle, crusher pass) achieved during the period. Compared against ideal_cycle_time_sec to derive Performance Rate. Sourced from MineStar dispatch and OSIsoft PI process historian.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; OSIsoft PI System

description: Actual hours the asset was in productive operation during the period, after deducting unplanned downtime and breakdowns. Core input to OEE Availability Rate calculation. Sourced from MineStar equipment health and OSIsoft PI historian.
reference: ISO 22400-2; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; OSIsoft PI System

description: Username or employee ID of the production supervisor or engineer who approved this OEE record in the reconciliation workflow. Supports audit trail requirements for OPEX reporting and regulatory compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM; Infor EAM; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: The ratio of actual operating time to planned operating time, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9250 = 92.50%). First component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as actual_operating_time_hrs / planned_operating_time_hrs. Stored as a raw rate for precision; percentage display handled in reporting layer.
reference: ISO 22400-2; Caterpillar MineStar OEE Module; ICMM Equipment Performance

description: Free-text field for production supervisors or engineers to record contextual notes about this OEE record, such as reasons for low availability, abnormal operating conditions, or data quality caveats.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Notes; Caterpillar MineStar Shift Reports

description: Date and time when this OEE record was first created in the data platform, either by automated ingestion from MineStar/OSIsoft PI or by manual entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
reference: ISO 55001 Asset Management; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Identifies the primary operational system from which this OEE record was sourced or reconciled. Supports data lineage tracing and audit of production reconciliation processes.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar; OSIsoft PI System; Infor EAM; SAP S/4HANA

description: Total distance travelled by mobile fleet assets (e.g., haul trucks, dozers) during the period, in kilometres. Used for tyre wear analysis, fuel efficiency benchmarking, and haul road condition assessment. Not applicable to fixed plant assets.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Fleet Management System (FMS)

description: Total engine running hours accumulated by the asset during the period. Used for maintenance scheduling, MTBF calculation, and asset lifecycle management in Infor EAM. Sourced from MineStar equipment health telematics.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Total diesel fuel consumed by the asset during the period, measured in litres. Used for fuel efficiency analysis, OPEX cost allocation, and carbon emissions reporting. Sourced from MineStar fuel management or SAP MM fuel issue records.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health; SAP S/4HANA MM; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Ratio of actual throughput in tonnes to fuel consumed in litres during the period. Key OPEX efficiency metric for haul trucks and mobile fleet. Enables benchmarking across fleet and identification of underperforming units.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; ICMM Sustainability Reporting; ISO 14001

description: The theoretical minimum time required to produce one unit (e.g., one haul cycle, one crusher pass) under optimal conditions. Used as the benchmark denominator in OEE Performance Rate calculation. Sourced from engineering design specifications or MineStar baseline models.
reference: ISO 22400-2; Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Mine Engineering Standards

description: Indicates whether this OEE record has been through the production reconciliation process and validated against shift reports and source system data. Unreconciled records are provisional and should not be used for OPEX reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; SAP S/4HANA PS Production Reconciliation

description: Total number of discrete load cycles (e.g., truck loads, shovel passes) completed by the asset during the period. Used for cycle time analysis and Performance Rate validation. Sourced from MineStar dispatch system.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Dispatch; Fleet Management System (FMS)

description: The composite OEE metric expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.7500 = 75.00%), calculated as the multiplicative product of availability_rate × performance_rate × quality_rate. This is the primary KPI for fleet performance benchmarking, bottleneck identification, and OPEX reporting. Stored as a pre-reconciled rate from the production reconciliation system.
reference: ISO 22400-2; Caterpillar MineStar OEE Module; ICMM Equipment Performance; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: The budgeted or contractually agreed OEE target rate for this asset type and period, expressed as a decimal. Used for variance analysis between actual OEE and planned OEE in OPEX reporting and fleet performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Budgeting; ICMM Equipment Performance; Mine Planning Standards

description: Average payload per load cycle in dry metric tonnes during the period. Used to assess truck loading efficiency against rated payload capacity. Sourced from MineStar payload monitoring system (VIMS/PLM).
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Fleet Management System (FMS)

description: The ratio of actual production speed to ideal production speed during operating time, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8750 = 87.50%). Second component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as (ideal_cycle_time_sec × total_units_produced) / actual_operating_time_hrs. Stored as raw rate.
reference: ISO 22400-2; Caterpillar MineStar OEE Module; ICMM Equipment Performance

description: The exact date and time marking the end of the production period for which OEE is calculated. Combined with period_start_timestamp to derive total period duration.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; OSIsoft PI System

description: The exact date and time marking the beginning of the production period (shift or sub-shift interval) for which OEE is calculated. Principal business event timestamp for this record. Sourced from MineStar shift dispatch or OSIsoft PI historian.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; OSIsoft PI System

description: Total hours the asset was taken out of service for scheduled preventive maintenance, inspections, or planned shutdowns during the period. Excluded from planned operating time in OEE Availability calculation.

Total scheduled hours the asset was planned to be in productive operation during the period, excluding planned maintenance windows and scheduled downtime. Core input to OEE Availability Rate calculation. Sourced from MineStar shift schedule.
reference: ISO 22400-2 KPIs for Manufacturing Operations Management; Caterpillar MineStar

description: Calendar date associated with this OEE record, representing the production day. Used for daily and monthly OEE trend reporting and OPEX period alignment.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS Production Scheduling; ICMM Reporting

description: The ratio of good units produced to total units produced, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9900 = 99.00%). Third component of the OEE composite metric. Calculated as (total_units_produced - reject_unit_count) / total_units_produced. For mobile fleet assets where quality is not applicable, this defaults to 1.0000.
reference: ISO 22400-2; LabWare LIMS; ICMM Equipment Performance

description: Date and time when this OEE record was formally reconciled and approved in the production reconciliation system. Null if is_reconciled is false.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Externally-known business identifier for this OEE record, formatted as OEE-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-system referencing between MineStar, OSIsoft PI, and SAP PM reporting.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Infor EAM

description: Current lifecycle status of this OEE record within the production reconciliation workflow. Draft records are provisional; approved records are used for OPEX reporting and fleet benchmarking.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; Infor EAM

description: Number of production units that did not meet quality specification during the period (e.g., crusher throughput below grade spec, off-spec concentrate batches). Core input to OEE Quality Rate calculation. Applicable primarily to fixed plant assets; set to zero for mobile fleet where quality is not directly measured.
reference: LabWare LIMS Quality Control; OSIsoft PI System; ISO 22400-2

description: Average travel speed of the mobile asset during the period, in km/h. Used for haul road performance analysis, speed compliance monitoring, and Performance Rate benchmarking. Sourced from MineStar telematics.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Fleet Management; Mine Safety and Health Administration (MSHA) Speed Compliance

description: Actual mass of material (ore or waste) processed or moved by the asset during the period, measured in dry metric tonnes. Primary quantitative production outcome for the period. Sourced from MineStar payload monitoring or OSIsoft PI belt scale historian.
reference: Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Tracking; OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code

description: Planned or budgeted throughput in dry metric tonnes for this asset during the period. Used for production variance analysis and OEE Performance Rate benchmarking against mine plan.

Code representing the type and intensity of hydrothermal or weathering alteration observed in the sample. Important for understanding mineralization controls.

Identifier for the batch or shipment in which this sample was sent to the laboratory. Used for tracking, logistics, and grouping samples for analysis.
Date when the sample was dispatched from the field or core facility to the analytical laboratory. Part of chain of custody documentation.
Easting coordinate of the sample collection point in meters, typically in a projected coordinate system (e.g., UTM). Used for spatial analysis and resource modeling.

Flag indicating whether this sample is a composite created by combining multiple individual samples. Composites are used for resource estimation and grade control.

description: Specific instructions for laboratory sample preparation including crushing, grinding, splitting, and sub-sampling protocols. May reference standard preparation codes or custom procedures.

reference: JORC Code 2012 Section 1 - Quality of Assay Data and Laboratory Tests

description: Percentage of core or sample material recovered relative to the drilled or sampled interval. Low recovery may indicate sample quality issues or geological conditions such as voids or fracturing.

Externally-known unique sample identifier assigned during collection. Used for laboratory tracking, chain of custody, and cross-referencing with assay results. Typically follows site-specific numbering conventions.

description: Unique identifier for the geological log record. Primary key for the geological log entity. Inferred role: EVENT_LOG (append-only geological observations per drill hole interval).
Unique identifier for the drill hole from which this geological log was recorded. Links to the drill hole collar and survey data. Represents the EVENT_SOURCE_REFERENCE for this log entry.

Foreign key linking to hse.hazard. Business justification: Geological logs identify hazardous lithologies (asbestos-bearing formations, acid-generating rock, naturally occurring radioactive material). Mining HSE and environmental regulations require geologist
Qualitative assessment of the intensity or degree of alteration observed: none, weak, moderate, strong, or pervasive. Used in conjunction with alteration_type for resource modeling.

Type of hydrothermal or weathering alteration observed in the interval (e.g., silicification, chloritisation, sericitisation, hematisation). Critical for understanding mineralization processes and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Alteration Classification Standards

description: Primary colour of the rock observed in the interval (e.g., grey, red, green, black, white). May indicate oxidation state, alteration, or lithology.
Free-text comments and observations recorded by the geologist during logging. May include additional details not captured in structured fields.
Overall condition of the drill core: excellent, good, fair, poor, or very_poor. Reflects core integrity and quality for geological interpretation.
Timestamp when this geological log record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and JORC compliance.
Source of the geological log data: field_logging (original logging at drill site), re_logging (subsequent re-interpretation), or historical_import (legacy data migration).
Starting depth of the logged interval in meters below surface or collar. Used to define the from-to interval for geological observations.
Ending depth of the logged interval in meters below surface or collar. Used to define the from-to interval for geological observations.
Classification of the grain size of the rock: very_fine, fine, medium, coarse, or very_coarse. Relevant for sedimentary and some igneous rocks.
Qualitative assessment of rock hardness: very_soft, soft, medium, hard, or very_hard. Relevant for drilling performance and geotechnical assessment.
Length of the logged interval in meters, calculated as depth_to minus depth_from. Represents the sample or observation interval.

Standardized code representing the primary rock type observed in this interval (e.g., BIF for Banded Iron Formation, GRN for Granite, SHL for Shale). Follows site-specific or regional lithology coding standards.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Site Geological Standards

description: Detailed textual description of the rock type, including texture, grain size, composition, and any distinguishing features observed by the geologist.
Date when the geological logging was performed. Represents the EVENT_TIMESTAMP for this observation. Critical for data lineage and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.
Method used to perform the geological logging: visual (traditional manual logging), digital (tablet-based logging), or photographic (image-based logging with subsequent interpretation).
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Internal Logging Standards

description: Qualitative assessment of the intensity or abundance of visible mineralization: none, trace, minor, moderate, or strong. Used for preliminary grade estimation and targeting.

Description of the style and form of mineralization observed (e.g., disseminated, vein-hosted, massive sulfide, replacement, stockwork). Critical for understanding ore body geometry and JORC reporting.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; NI 43-101; SAMREC

description: Timestamp when this geological log record was last modified. Part of audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.
Oxidation state of the mineralization: oxide (fully oxidized), transitional (partially oxidized), or fresh_sulfide (unoxidized). Critical for metallurgical processing and resource classification.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Metallurgical Classification Standards

description: Quality control status of the geological log record: pending (awaiting review), approved (validated and accepted), rejected (failed QC checks), or under_review (currently being reviewed). Critical for JORC Table 1 Section 1 data quality reporting.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Internal QA/QC Standards

description: Percentage of core recovered from the drilled interval, calculated as recovered core length divided by drilled interval length. Critical quality indicator for drill hole data and JORC Table 1 Section 1 reporting.
High-level classification of the rock type into major geological categories: igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic, or unconsolidated material.

Rock Quality Designation expressed as a percentage, calculated as the sum of core pieces greater than 10cm divided by the interval length. Standard geotechnical measure of rock mass quality ranging from 0 to 100 percent.
reference: ISRM Standards; JORC Code Table 1 Section 1

description: Boolean flag indicating whether a physical sample was collected from this interval for assay analysis. True if sample was collected, False otherwise.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Laboratory Sample Management

description: Orientation of structural features recorded as dip direction and dip angle (e.g., 045/60 meaning dipping 60 degrees towards 045 azimuth). Critical for structural modeling and geotechnical assessment.
Type of structural feature observed in the interval (e.g., fault, shear zone, joint, fracture, foliation, bedding). Important for understanding ore controls and geotechnical properties.
reference: JORC Code Table 1 Section 1; Structural Geology Standards

description: Description of the rock texture (e.g., massive, foliated, banded, brecciated, porphyritic). Provides information on rock formation and deformation history.
Description of vein mineralogy and characteristics (e.g., quartz vein, calcite vein, sulfide vein). Critical for understanding mineralization style and ore genesis.
Estimated percentage of the interval occupied by veins. Important for understanding mineralization distribution and structural controls.

Degree of weathering observed in the rock: fresh, slightly_weathered, moderately_weathered, highly_weathered, or completely_weathered. Important for understanding near-surface ore characteristics and geotechnical properties.
reference: Weathering Classification Standards; JORC Code Table 1 Section 1

description: Primary key for the drill_target association

Foreign key linking this drill target to the survey whose anomaly or result generated this target.

Identifier or label for the specific anomaly within the survey that generated this drill target (e.g., anomaly ID, grid reference, or named anomaly). Belongs to the relationship because a single survey may contain many anomalies, only some of which are linked to a given program.

Number of drill targets prioritized from this survey for inclusion in future drilling programs. Subset of total anomalies that pass economic and technical filters. [Moved from survey: drill_targets_generated on survey is a derived count of how many drill targets were generated from that survey. With the drill_target association table in place, this count is derivable via COUNT(drill_target_id) WHERE survey_id = X. Storing it as a denormalised integer on the survey creates a maintenance burden and risks inconsistency. It should be removed from survey and computed from the association.]

Type of drilling follow-up recommended for this target based on the survey result (e.g., scout, infill, step-out, resource_definition). Characterises the nature of the drilling response to the anomaly in the context of this program.

Ranked priority of this drill target within the drill program (e.g., 1 = highest priority). Priority is assigned in the context of a specific program and cannot reside on either the survey or the drill program alone.

Date on which this drill target was formally created from the survey interpretation and added to the targeting register. Records the point in time when the exploration team recognised the anomaly as a drill-worthy target.

The monetary value of the expenditure or budget allocation in the specified currency.
Date on which the budget or expenditure was formally approved by the designated authority.
Version identifier for the budget, enabling tracking of multiple budget revisions throughout the financial year (e.g., V1, V2, V3).
The minimum statutory expenditure commitment amount required for the licence or programme in the specified financial period, used for compliance tracking.
Timestamp when this expenditure record was first created in the system, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the expenditure amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).
Identifier of the source system from which this expenditure record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Pronto Xi, manual entry), supporting data lineage and reconciliation.
The SAP financial document number (e.g., invoice number, journal entry number) associated with this expenditure, enabling traceability to source documents.

The date on which the actual expenditure was incurred or the budget was allocated, serving as the principal business event timestamp for this record.
Free-text description providing additional context about the nature of the expenditure or budget allocation, including specific activities or deliverables.
The financial period (month) within the financial year, formatted as P01 through P12, enabling monthly budget vs actual tracking.
The financial year to which this expenditure or budget applies, formatted as FY followed by the four-digit year (e.g., FY2024).
Timestamp when this expenditure record was last modified, enabling change tracking and audit compliance.
Additional notes or comments related to the expenditure, including explanations for variances, special circumstances, or audit trail information.

Discriminator indicating whether this record represents an original budget allocation, a revised budget, or actual expenditure incurred.
The SAP internal order, WBS element, or cost centre code used as the cost object for this expenditure in the ERP system.
Boolean indicator (True/False) denoting whether this expenditure contributes toward satisfying minimum statutory expenditure commitments required under the exploration licence conditions.
Calculated variance between budgeted and actual expenditure, enabling direct budget-vs-actual analysis. Positive values indicate under-spend, negative values indicate over-spend.

Reference to the underlying Measured or Indicated Mineral Resource estimate from which this ore reserve is derived.

Date on which the ore reserve statement was formally approved by the Competent Person and authorized for public disclosure.
Weighted average grade of the valuable mineral or metal in the ore reserve, calculated after application of dilution and mining recovery factors.
Unit of measure for the average grade, typically percentage (%) for bulk commodities or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals.
Date on which the ore reserve statement was formally endorsed and signed off by the company board of directors for regulatory reporting and public disclosure.
reference: Corporate Governance Standards, ASX Listing Rules, SEC Regulations

description: Total quantity of contained metal or mineral in the ore reserve, calculated as reserve tonnage multiplied by average grade.
Unit of measure for the contained metal, typically tonnes, kilograms, or ounces depending on the commodity.
Timestamp when this ore reserve record was first created in the system.
The minimum economic grade threshold applied to define ore from waste. Material below this grade is classified as waste and excluded from the ore reserve tonnage.
Unit of measure for the cut-off grade, typically percentage (%) for bulk commodities or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals.
Percentage or factor representing the expected dilution of ore grade due to unplanned inclusion of waste material during mining operations.

description: Date on which the ore reserve statement was publicly disclosed to the market and regulatory authorities.
reference: ASX Listing Rules, SEC Regulations, JORC Code 2012

description: Summary of key economic assumptions used in the ore reserve calculation, including commodity prices, exchange rates, operating costs, and capital costs.
The date as of which the ore reserve estimate is current and valid, representing the cut-off for all data and assumptions used in the reserve calculation.
Timestamp when this ore reserve record was last updated or modified in the system.
Estimated life of mine in years based on the ore reserve tonnage and planned annual production rate.
Percentage or factor representing the expected proportion of contained metal that will be recovered during mineral processing and beneficiation.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Clause 39, Metallurgical Test Work Standards

description: The planned mining method to be applied for extraction of the ore reserve, which influences dilution, recovery, and economic viability. description: Percentage or factor representing the expected proportion of in-situ ore that will be successfully extracted during mining operations.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Clause 39, Mining Engineering Standards

description: Description of the modifying factors applied to convert the Mineral Resource to an Ore Reserve, including mining, metallurgical, economic, marketing, legal, environmental, social, and governmental considerations.
Additional notes, comments, or qualifications related to the ore reserve estimate, including material risks, uncertainties, or assumptions.
The international mineral resource and reserve reporting standard under which this ore reserve statement is prepared and disclosed.
reference: JORC Code 2012, NI 43-101, SAMREC Code, SEC S-K 1300, CIM Definition Standards

description: Classification category of the ore reserve: Proved Reserve (highest confidence, derived from Measured Resource) or Probable Reserve (derived from Indicated Resource).
reference: JORC Code 2012 Clause 38-40, NI 43-101, SAMREC Code

description: Unique business code or identifier for the ore reserve used in reporting and planning systems.
Business name or designation of the ore reserve, typically referencing the deposit or mining area.
Current lifecycle status of the ore reserve statement, indicating whether it is in draft, approved for use, publicly disclosed, superseded by a newer estimate, or archived.
Total tonnage of ore in the reserve after application of modifying factors including dilution, mining recovery, and cut-off grade.
Unit of measure for the ore reserve tonnage.
Ratio of waste material (overburden) to ore that must be removed to access the ore reserve, applicable to open pit mining operations.

description: The date on which this resource estimate is effective and represents the state of geological knowledge and data.

Business name or title of the resource estimate, typically referencing the deposit, prospect, or project name.

description: Total number of assay samples used in the estimation process.
The date on which the Competent Person formally signed off and approved this resource estimate.
Total estimated tonnage of mineralized material in the resource category, typically expressed in million tonnes (Mt) or thousand tonnes (kt).
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1, NI 43-101, SAMREC Code

description: Unit of measure for tonnage: tonnes (t), thousand tonnes (kt), million tonnes (Mt), or billion tonnes (Gt).
Total metres of drilling completed and used as the basis for this resource estimate.
Timestamp when this resource estimate record was last modified in the system.

business_glossary_term: Resource Estimation Input - Resource Estimation Input Id
description: Primary key for the resource_estimation_input association

business_glossary_term: Resource Estimation Input - Drill Hole Id
description: Foreign key linking to the drill hole whose assay data is being included or excluded from the resource estimate.

business_glossary_term: Resource Estimation Input - Resource Estimate Id
description: Foreign key linking to the mineral resource estimate that this drill hole input record belongs to.

Indicates whether the assay samples from this drill hole were composited (e.g., regularised to 2m composites) before being used in the estimation. Compositing decisions are per-hole per-estimate and belong to this association.

The statistical weight assigned to this drill hole within the resource estimation algorithm (e.g., kriging weight, IDW distance-based weight). Belongs to the association because the same hole may carry different weights in different estimates.
Timestamp when this drill hole inclusion record was first created, supporting audit trail requirements under JORC 2012 and NI 43-101 for resource estimation dataset provenance.

The mineralisation domain or estimation domain within which this drill hole's data was used. A single hole may intersect multiple domains; this attribute records which domain context applies for this estimate inclusion record.

Total number of drill holes used as input data for this resource estimate. [Moved from resource_estimate: This INT aggregate on resource_estimate is derivable by COUNT(*) WHERE included_flag = TRUE on this association table. Storing it as a denormalised count on the estimate creates a maintenance burden and risks inconsistency. It should be removed from resource_estimate and computed from this association product.]

Free-text reason why this drill hole was excluded from the resource estimate (e.g., insufficient depth, poor core recovery, outside domain boundary, superseded by closer hole). Only populated when included_flag is FALSE. Required for JORC Table 1 audit compliance.

Indicates whether this drill hole was included (TRUE) or explicitly excluded (FALSE) from the resource estimate. Excluded holes are still recorded to provide a complete audit trail of the estimation dataset decision.

The number of assay intervals (downhole sample intervals) from this drill hole that were used as input data in the resource estimate. A hole may have 200 intervals but only 150 within the mineralised domain used for estimation.
Timestamp when this drill hole inclusion record was last modified, supporting change tracking for regulatory compliance and estimate revision history.
Unique identifier for the geotechnical log record. Primary key for downhole geotechnical and physical property measurements from diamond drillhole core.
Unique identifier of the diamond drillhole from which this geotechnical core sample was extracted. Links to the drill hole master record in the exploration domain.

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_unit. Business justification: Geotechnical logs capture physical properties for specific lithologies. Currently geotechnical_log stores lithology_code as a STRING; this should be normalized to FK to geological_unit.geological_unit
Identifier of the laboratory facility that performed the geotechnical testing and density measurements. Used for QAQC traceability and inter-laboratory comparison.

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Geotechnical log entries (UCS, point load, density) are measured on physical core samples submitted to a laboratory. The geotechnical_log already links to laboratory_id but not the specific sample. A

Foreign key linking to geology.production_drillhole. Business justification: Geotechnical logs (RQD, UCS, RMR, fracture frequency, density) are captured from diamond drillhole core and belong to a specific production drillhole. The existing drill_hole_id links to the cross-dom
Free-text field for geotechnical observations, notes on core condition, structural features, alteration, or any other relevant geomechanical information not captured in structured fields.
Percentage of core recovered from the drilled interval, calculated as (recovered core length / drilled interval length) × 100. Critical indicator of rock quality and drilling conditions.
Source system from which this geotechnical log record was extracted. Typically Geovia GEMS for geological logging or LabWare LIMS for laboratory density and strength testing results.
Laboratory method used to determine density and specific gravity. Water immersion is standard for competent core; wax-coated method used for porous or friable samples; pycnometer for fine material.
Starting depth in meters downhole from collar for this geotechnical measurement interval. Defines the top of the logged interval.
Ending depth in meters downhole from collar for this geotechnical measurement interval. Defines the bottom of the logged interval.
Dry bulk density of the core sample in tonnes per cubic meter, measured after oven-drying to constant mass. Primary density value for resource tonnage calculations.
reference: JORC Code 2012 - Section 3 Estimation and Reporting

description: Number of natural fractures, joints, or discontinuities per meter of core. Key parameter for rock mass characterization and ground support design.

Geomechanical domain classification code representing zones of similar rock mass behavior. Used to group rock masses with consistent geotechnical properties for slope stability and stope design modeling.
Length of the geotechnical logging interval in meters, calculated as depth_to minus depth_from. Represents the core run length for this measurement.
Date when the geotechnical core logging was performed in the field or core shed. Distinct from laboratory test date.
reference: JORC Code 2012 - Section 1 Sampling and Sub-sampling

description: Moisture content of the sample at time of measurement, calculated as ((wet mass - dry mass) / dry mass) × 100. Used to convert between wet and dry density values.
reference: ASTM D2216 - Laboratory Determination of Water Content

description: Degree of oxidation and weathering of the rock mass per ISRM weathering classification. Critical for density correction and geotechnical property correlation.
Point Load Strength Index corrected to 50mm diameter equivalent (Is50) in megapascals. Field-based rock strength indicator used to estimate UCS when core testing is not available.
reference: ISRM Suggested Methods - Point Load Test

description: Porosity of the rock sample expressed as percentage of void space to total volume. Influences density, strength, and fluid flow characteristics.

Tunneling Quality Index (Q-system) per Barton et al. (1974). Logarithmic rock mass classification calculated from RQD, joint set number, joint roughness, joint alteration, water reduction factor, and stress reduction factor. Used for underground excavation support design.
Free-text quality control flag or comment describing any data quality issues, anomalies, or validation failures identified during QAQC review. Empty if no issues detected.
Quality assurance and quality control validation status for this geotechnical record. Indicates whether the data has passed internal validation checks for completeness, consistency, and accuracy.
Timestamp when this geotechnical log record was first created in the source system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.
Timestamp when this geotechnical log record was last modified in the source system. Used for change tracking and incremental data loading.

Rock Mass Rating classification value (0-100 scale) per Bieniawski (1989). Composite geomechanical classification integrating rock strength, RQD, discontinuity spacing, condition, groundwater, and orientation. Used for ground support design.

Rock Quality Designation percentage, calculated as the sum of intact core pieces greater than 100mm divided by total interval length × 100. Standard geotechnical index for rock mass quality per Deere (1964).
Unique laboratory sample number assigned to the core interval for geotechnical testing. Links to sample chain-of-custody and laboratory information management system.
Specific gravity of the rock sample, representing the ratio of sample density to water density. Measured using water immersion, wax-coated, or pycnometer methods.
reference: ASTM D854 - Specific Gravity of Soil Solids

description: Date when the geotechnical laboratory testing was performed. Critical for temporal QAQC analysis and data vintage tracking.
Unconfined Compressive Strength of intact rock measured in megapascals (MPa). Laboratory-determined strength of rock under uniaxial compression, critical for pit slope and stope design.
reference: ISRM Suggested Methods - Uniaxial Compressive Strength

description: Wet bulk density of the core sample in tonnes per cubic meter, measured at natural moisture content. Used for in-situ tonnage estimation and comparison with dry density.
reference: JORC Code 2012 - Section 3 Estimation and Reporting

description: Unique identifier for the lithological logging record. Primary key for the lithology log entity.
Reference to the drill hole in which this lithological observation was recorded. Links this log entry to the parent drill hole entity.

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Lithology log intervals are assigned to geological domains for resource estimation. Currently lithology_log stores domain_code as a STRING; this should be normalized to FK to geological_domain.geologi

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_unit. Business justification: Lithology logs reference standardized geological units from the geological_unit reference catalog. Currently lithology_log stores geological_unit as a STRING; this should be normalized to FK to geolog

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory_sample. Business justification: Lithology log intervals are physically sampled for assay during resource and grade control drilling. Tracing a logged interval to its laboratory sample is required for JORC Table 1 data verification a

Foreign key linking to geology.production_drillhole. Business justification: Lithology log intervals are captured from drillhole core and belong to a specific production drillhole in the geology domain. The existing drill_hole_id links to the cross-domain exploration.drill_hol

Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Lithology logs are recorded during drilling campaigns governed by a sample program that defines logging protocols, sampling requirements, and QAQC standards. A resource geologist expects to trace lith
Degree or intensity of alteration affecting the rock unit. Ranges from none to pervasive and influences rock competence and processing behavior.
Type of hydrothermal or weathering alteration affecting the rock unit (e.g., sericitic, chloritic, argillic, propylitic). Important for understanding ore genesis and processing characteristics.
Observed colour of the rock unit. Provides visual identification characteristics and may correlate with mineralogy or alteration.
Additional free-text comments or observations recorded by the logging geologist. Captures contextual information that does not fit structured fields.
Timestamp when this lithology log record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data governance and regulatory compliance.
Source system or database from which this lithological log record originated (e.g., Geovia GEMS, field logging tablet). Provides data lineage for integration and reconciliation.
Average grain size of the rock matrix, classified according to standard grain size scales. Influences rock strength and processing characteristics.
Relative hardness of the rock unit assessed by field tests. Provides indication of rock competence and drilling characteristics.
Starting depth of the logged interval measured in metres from the drill hole collar. Defines the top boundary of the lithological unit being described.
reference: JORC Code 2012 - Section 1 Sampling Techniques and Data

description: Length of the logged interval in metres, calculated as the difference between to_depth and from_depth. Represents the thickness of the lithological unit.
Ending depth of the logged interval measured in metres from the drill hole collar. Defines the bottom boundary of the lithological unit being described.
reference: JORC Code 2012 - Section 1 Sampling Techniques and Data

description: Detailed lithological classification code representing the specific lithological unit or formation. More granular than rock_type_code and aligned with the geological model nomenclature.

Comprehensive free-text description of the lithological characteristics including texture, structure, mineralogy, alteration, weathering, and any other relevant geological observations made by the logging geologist.
Date on which the lithological logging was performed. Important for data provenance and temporal tracking of geological interpretation evolution.
Method used to perform the lithological logging (e.g., visual inspection, digital logging, photographic logging). Affects data quality and consistency.
Style or morphology of mineralisation observed in the interval. Critical for understanding ore body geometry and grade distribution.
Timestamp when this lithology log record was last modified. Tracks data currency and supports change management processes.
Oxidation state of the rock unit indicating the degree of supergene alteration. Critical for metallurgical processing route selection and resource classification.
Dominant mineral species observed in the interval. Represents the most abundant mineral constituent of the rock unit.
Comments or notes from the quality control review process. Documents any issues, corrections, or validation decisions made during QAQC review.

Quality control status of the lithological log entry indicating whether it has been validated, requires review, or has been rejected. Critical for data governance and compliance with JORC reporting standards.
Percentage of core recovered from the drilled interval. High recovery indicates competent rock; low recovery may indicate fractured, weathered, or weak zones. Critical quality control metric.
Standardized code representing the primary rock type observed in this interval (e.g., GRAN for granite, BAS for basalt, SEDS for sedimentary). Used for geological classification and modeling.
Detailed textual description of the rock type including lithological characteristics, composition, and geological interpretation. Provides full context beyond the standardized code.
Rock Quality Designation expressed as percentage of intact core pieces greater than 10cm in length. Standard geotechnical parameter for rock mass quality assessment.
Structural features observed in the interval including bedding, foliation, jointing, veining, or other geological structures. Important for geotechnical and structural geological interpretation.
Visual estimate of sulphide mineral content as percentage of the interval. Important for ore grade estimation and metallurgical characterisation.
Textural characteristics of the rock including fabric, grain relationships, and structural features (e.g., porphyritic, aphanitic, foliated, massive).
Description of vein characteristics including composition, orientation, and morphology (e.g., quartz vein, calcite vein, tension gash). Relevant for structural and mineralisation interpretation.
Estimated percentage of the interval occupied by veins or veinlets. Important for understanding structural controls on mineralisation and rock mass characteristics.
Extent of weathering affecting the rock unit, classified according to standard weathering scales. Critical for geotechnical assessment and mine planning.
Unique identifier for the production drillhole record. Primary key for the production drillhole master register.

Planned azimuth (bearing) of the drillhole at collar, measured in degrees from 0 to 360, where 0/360 is north, 90 is east, 180 is south, and 270 is west. Used to define hole trajectory.

description: Easting coordinate of the drillhole collar (surface location) in the site coordinate reference system, typically in meters. Used for spatial positioning in GIS and geological modeling systems.

description: Northing coordinate of the drillhole collar (surface location) in the site coordinate reference system, typically in meters. Used for spatial positioning in GIS and geological modeling systems.
reference: GIS Coordinate Standard, JORC Code 2012 Table 1 Section 1

description: Reduced Level (elevation) of the drillhole collar relative to site datum, typically in meters above mean sea level. Critical for accurate 3D geological modeling and resource estimation.

description: Free-text field for recording operational notes, drilling issues, geological observations, or other relevant information about the drillhole. Used for quality control and historical reference.
Date when drilling operations were completed or abandoned. Used for program scheduling, performance tracking, and cost allocation.

description: Name or identifier of the geologist or modeller who last modified the wireframe. Supports change tracking and accountability.

Result of automated quality assurance checks on the wireframe geometry (e.g., checks for self-intersecting triangles, inverted normals, gaps, overlaps). Passed indicates the wireframe meets technical quality standards.

description: Specific version number or release identifier of the modelling software used. Required for technical reproducibility and compliance with JORC competent person requirements.

Total surface area of the wireframe in square metres. For solid wireframes, represents the external surface area. For surface wireframes, represents the area of the surface itself. Used for volume calculations and spatial analysis.

Number of vertices (points) that define the wireframe mesh. Higher vertex counts indicate finer resolution and more detailed geometry.

Unique identifier for the analytical method record. Primary key.
Unique accreditation certificate or registration number issued by the accrediting body for this specific method. Used for audit trails and regulatory reporting.

The formal accreditation standard under which this method is certified (e.g., NATA for National Association of Testing Authorities in Australia, ISO 17025 for international laboratory accreditation, A2LA for American Association for Laboratory Accreditation). Critical for resource estimation and regulatory compliance.

Method accuracy expressed as percentage bias from certified reference material (CRM) true values. Positive values indicate over-reporting; negative values indicate under-reporting. Acceptable bias is typically within ±5% for resource estimation methods.
Typical time required to complete the analytical procedure for a single sample, measured in minutes. Used for laboratory capacity planning and turnaround time estimation.

Comma-separated list of chemical elements or compounds that this method can detect and quantify (e.g., Au, Ag, Cu, Fe, S, C for multi-element methods; Au only for single-element fire assay). Defines the scope of the method's analytical capability.

Boolean flag indicating whether this method is approved for real-time or near-real-time process control and optimization in mineral processing plants. True if suitable for operational decision-making; false if restricted to formal assay reporting.

Boolean flag indicating whether this method meets the quality and accreditation standards required for JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC compliant mineral resource and ore reserve estimation. True if approved; false if restricted to process control or non-compliant applications.

Standard cost to analyze one sample using this method, including labor, consumables, reagents, and instrument time. Used for laboratory budgeting and exploration/production cost estimation. Expressed in the organization's reporting currency.
Timestamp when this analytical method record was first created in the LIMS or laboratory management system. Used for audit trails and data lineage.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Record Management, Data Governance Standards

description: Minimum concentration or grade that the method can reliably detect and quantify, expressed in the method's reporting unit. Values below this threshold are reported as below detection limit (BDL).
Date from which this method version became active and approved for use in the laboratory. Used for historical data traceability and audit compliance.
Date on which this method version was superseded or retired. Null for currently active methods. Used for historical data traceability and method lifecycle management.

Type or model family of analytical instrument required to perform this method (e.g., Varian AA240 Atomic Absorption Spectrometer, Bruker S8 Tiger XRF, Agilent 7900 ICP-MS, LECO CS844 Carbon/Sulphur Analyzer). Used for instrument scheduling and capacity planning.

Documentation of known chemical or spectral interferences that may affect method accuracy (e.g., high iron content interfering with copper XRF analysis, arsenic interference in gold fire assay). Used to guide method selection and result interpretation.
Timestamp when this analytical method record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
reference: ISO/IEC 17025 Record Management, Data Governance Standards

description: Date of the most recent method validation or re-validation exercise, including precision and accuracy testing against certified reference materials. ISO 17025 requires periodic re-validation.

Description of the sample matrix types for which this method is validated and approved (e.g., oxide ore, sulphide ore, concentrate, tailings, soil, water, rock). Critical for ensuring method suitability for different geological and process materials.

Business classification of the method's primary application domain (geochemical for ore assays, metallurgical for concentrate and recovery testing, environmental for tailings and water analysis, quality_control for QA/QC samples, process_control for real-time plant monitoring).

Unique alphanumeric code identifying the analytical method (e.g., FA-AAS-01, XRF-PP-02, ICP-MS-03). Used as the business identifier for the method across laboratory systems and resource estimation workflows.

Reference to the detailed method documentation, standard operating procedure (SOP), or published standard (e.g., SOP-LAB-FA-001, ASTM E1915, ISO 11426). Used to access full procedural details and compliance evidence.
Full descriptive name of the analytical method (e.g., Fire Assay with Atomic Absorption Spectroscopy for Gold, X-Ray Fluorescence Pressed Pellet for Multi-Element Analysis).
Name or identifier of the laboratory manager or technical specialist responsible for maintaining and validating this method. Used for accountability and technical queries.

Current lifecycle status of the analytical method. Active methods are approved for use; superseded methods have been replaced by newer versions; pending_accreditation methods are awaiting formal approval.

Classification of the analytical technique family (e.g., fire_assay for FA-AAS/FA-OES, xrf for X-Ray Fluorescence, icp_ms for Inductively Coupled Plasma Mass Spectrometry, leco for LECO carbon/sulphur analyzers). description: Version number of the analytical method (e.g., 1.0, 2.1, 3.0.1). Incremented when method parameters, detection limits, or procedures are updated.
Scheduled date for the next method validation or re-validation exercise. Used for laboratory quality management and accreditation maintenance planning.

Method precision expressed as relative standard deviation (RSD) percentage, derived from replicate analysis of certified reference materials (CRMs). Lower values indicate higher precision. Typical acceptable range is 1-10% depending on analyte and concentration.

Comma-separated list of chemical reagents required for the analytical procedure (e.g., lithium borate flux, hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, lead oxide, silver nitrate). Used for laboratory inventory management and safety planning.

Specification of certified reference materials (CRMs) or standard reference materials (SRMs) that must be analyzed alongside samples for quality control (e.g., OREAS 101a, AMIS 0318, NIST 2709a). Critical for QA/QC protocol compliance.

Standard unit of measure in which analytical results are reported (e.g., ppm for parts per million, g_per_t for grams per tonne, percent for percentage concentration).
