description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this pit design within the mine planning system (e.g., PIT-NW-001-R3). Used as the business-facing reference across engineering, scheduling, and LOM planning documents.
Name or employee identifier of the mine planning or geotechnical engineer responsible for authoring this pit design revision. Used for accountability and design review traceability.

File path or document management system reference to the source design file (e.g., DXF, DWG, or MinePlan project file) containing the pit shell geometry. Enables engineers to retrieve the authoritative spatial data.
Human-readable descriptive name of the pit design (e.g., 'North-West Pit Stage 3 Pushback'). Used in reports, LOM schedules, and stakeholder communications.
Free-text field for engineering notes, assumptions, constraints, or change rationale associated with this pit design revision. Captures context not represented in structured fields.

Current lifecycle status of the pit design record. 'draft' indicates work-in-progress; 'under_review' means submitted for engineering or geotechnical review; 'approved' is the authorised design for mine planning; 'superseded' means replaced by a newer revision; 'archived' is retained for historical reference only.

Classification of the pit design indicating its engineering purpose. 'ultimate_pit' is the final planned pit extent; 'pushback' or 'cutback' represents an incremental mining stage; 'stage' is a scheduled mining increment; 'interim' is a temporary design used for short-term planning. Date from which this pit design is considered the active and authoritative design for mine planning purposes. Supports temporal versioning of pit designs across LOM schedule periods.
Date on which this pit design ceases to be the active authoritative design, typically when superseded by a new revision. Null for the currently active design.
The bench face angle in degrees, representing the angle of the individual bench face from horizontal. Determined by rock mass strength and blasting parameters. Typically 65°–80° for competent rock.

The geotechnical domain or zone classification applied to this pit design, reflecting the rock mass characterisation used to determine slope angle parameters. Sourced from the geotechnical model underpinning the design.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Geovia GEMS; JORC Code 2012

description: Indicates whether hydrogeological conditions (groundwater, dewatering requirements) were explicitly incorporated into the pit slope design parameters. True = hydrogeology modelled; False = not considered or not applicable.

The inter-ramp slope angle in degrees, measured between catch berms. Represents the slope angle of the pit wall between successive ramp access levels. Used in geotechnical stability analysis and bench design.
The open-cut mining method applied in this pit design. Determines the geometric and operational parameters used in the design (e.g., bench height, slope angles, equipment selection).

Name of the mine planning software used to generate the pit shell optimisation (e.g., Hexagon MinePlan, Deswik, Whittle). Provides provenance for the design methodology and enables reproducibility of results.

Total estimated ore tonnes within the pit design shell above the applied cut-off grade. Derived from the resource block model intersected with the pit shell geometry. Used in LOM scheduling and reserve reporting.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Geovia GEMS; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards; SEC S-K 1300

description: The overall inter-ramp or overall pit slope angle in degrees, representing the angle from horizontal of the pit wall from crest to toe. A critical geotechnical parameter governing pit stability and strip ratio. Typically ranges from 35° to 55° depending on rock mass conditions.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; JORC Code 2012; ISO 14001

description: The elevation of the pit floor at its lowest point, expressed as a Reduced Level (RL) in metres above mean sea level (AHD or equivalent datum). Defines the maximum depth of the pit design.
The elevation of the pit crest (top of the uppermost bench) in metres above mean sea level (AHD or equivalent datum). Together with pit_bottom_rl_m defines the total vertical depth of the pit.
Total volume of material (ore and waste) within the pit design shell, expressed in Bank Cubic Metres (BCM). Used for LOM scheduling, equipment fleet sizing, and capital cost estimation.
The maximum gradient of haul road ramps within the pit expressed as a percentage (rise over run × 100). Governs truck cycle times and fuel consumption. Typically 8–12% for large haul trucks.

The design width of haul road ramps within the pit in metres. Determines the minimum turning radius and equipment access for the selected truck fleet. Typically 3–4 times the width of the largest haul truck.
Date on which this revision of the pit design was formally issued or updated. Used to track the currency of the design relative to the LOM schedule and resource model.
Sequential integer revision number for this pit design, incremented each time the design is formally updated. Enables version control and audit trail of design changes over the LOM planning cycle.

The ratio of waste tonnes to ore tonnes within the pit design, expressed as tonnes of waste per tonne of ore (t:t). A fundamental economic parameter for open-cut mining — higher strip ratios increase operating costs (OPEX). Calculated as waste_tonnes / ore_tonnes.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards

description: The total vertical depth of the pit design in metres, calculated as the difference between pit crest RL and pit bottom RL. A key design parameter for geotechnical stability assessment and LOM planning.
Timestamp when this pit design record was last modified in the lakehouse data platform. Used to detect changes and support incremental data processing in the silver layer.
Total estimated waste rock tonnes within the pit design shell, including overburden and internal waste. Used to calculate strip ratio and plan waste dump capacity.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Geovia GEMS; JORC Code 2012

reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; MSHA 30 CFR Part 57; ISO 45001

description: Timestamp when this stope design record was first created in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage requirements.

Minimum economic grade threshold applied to define the stope boundary and determine ore versus waste classification within the design. Expressed in the same unit as design_grade_primary. Directly impacts designed ore tonnes and LOM economics.
reference: JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; CIM Definition Standards; SAMREC Code

description: Date on which the current revision of the stope design was formally completed and issued by the mine design engineer.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Estimated in-situ grade of the primary commodity within the designed stope boundary, expressed in the commodity-appropriate unit (g/t for gold/silver, % for base metals). Derived from the block model and used for mill feed planning and revenue forecasting.
reference: Geovia GEMS Resource Estimation; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; SAMREC Code

description: Sequential revision number of the stope design, incremented each time the design is formally updated. Revision 0 represents the initial issue; subsequent revisions capture engineering changes, geotechnical updates, or grade re-interpretations.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; ISO 19650 Information Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the stope design record, tracking progression from initial draft through engineering review, approval, and construction issue. Superseded status indicates a newer revision has replaced this design.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; ISO 19650 Information Management

description: Name or employee identifier of the mine design engineer responsible for authoring this stope design revision.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; SAP S/4HANA HR

description: Planned dilution percentage representing the proportion of waste rock expected to be extracted with the ore due to stope geometry and mining method. Expressed as a percentage of total ROM tonnes. Key input to mill feed grade forecasting.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; JORC Code 2012; CIM Definition Standards

description: Estimated in-situ ore tonnage within the designed stope boundary, calculated from the block model grade-tonnage curve. Represents the planned ore extraction quantity before dilution and recovery adjustments.
reference: Geovia GEMS Resource Estimation; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; CIM Definition Standards

description: Planned ore recovery percentage representing the proportion of in-situ ore tonnes expected to be extracted from the stope. Accounts for ore loss to pillars, hangingwall slough, and extraction method limitations.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; JORC Code 2012; CIM Definition Standards

description: Total designed void volume of the stope in cubic metres as calculated from the 3D stope solid in Deswik or Geovia GEMS. Used for backfill volume estimation, blast design, and ventilation requirements.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Native stope identifier from the Deswik mine planning and stope optimisation system. Enables reconciliation between the silver layer data product and the Deswik source system for scheduling and optimisation workflows.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Deswik Mine Planning and Scheduling

description: Underground mining extraction method applied to this stope design. Determines drill pattern, blast design, support requirements, and backfill strategy. Key driver of dilution, recovery rate, and OPEX. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: longhole_open|longhole_retreat|cut_and_fill|sublevel_caving|sublevel_stoping|bench_and_fill|room_and_pillar|shrinkage — promote to reference product]
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; JORC Code 2012; CIM Definition Standards

description: Planned extraction sequence number of this stope within its mining block or production area. Governs the order of stope firing to manage geotechnical stability, backfill curing time, and ventilation circuit integrity.
reference: Deswik Mine Planning and Scheduling; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Native stope object identifier from the Geovia GEMS geological modelling and mine design system. Used for traceability and data lineage between the silver layer data product and the source system of record.
reference: Geovia GEMS Mine Design; SAP S/4HANA PS

description: Unit of measure for the primary commodity grade value (e.g., g/t for gold, % for copper or iron ore, ppm for trace elements). Ensures correct interpretation of design_grade_primary across different commodity types.

Geotechnical ground support classification assigned to the stope design, typically based on rock mass rating (RMR) or Q-system. Drives the specification of rock bolts, cable bolts, mesh, and shotcrete requirements. Class A = competent rock; Class E = very poor ground.
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 57; ISO 45001; Deswik Stope Optimization

description: Elevation in metres above mean sea level (mAMSL) of the primary extraction level or ore drive floor for this stope design. Used for ventilation circuit design, dewatering planning, and LOM scheduling.
reference: Geovia GEMS Mine Design; Deswik Mine Planning; GIS Coordinate Reference Systems

description: Life of Mine (LOM) planning period or fiscal year-quarter in which this stope is scheduled for extraction (e.g., 'FY2026-Q2'). Links the stope design to the LOM production schedule in Deswik for NPV and IRR modelling.
reference: Deswik Mine Planning and Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS 6 Exploration

description: Number of ore drives (extraction drives) designed within the stope footprint. Determines development metres required, drill rig access, and mucking logistics for the stope.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Designed height of each ore drive (extraction drive) within the stope in metres. Determines equipment clearance and development cost per metre.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Designed width of each ore drive (extraction drive) within the stope in metres. Determines equipment clearance, drill pattern geometry, and development cost per metre.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Type of geotechnical pillar incorporated into the stope design to maintain ground stability. Crown pillars separate stopes vertically; rib pillars separate stopes horizontally; sill pillars provide floor support; barrier pillars protect major infrastructure. None indicates pillarless design.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; MSHA 30 CFR Part 57; ISO 45001

description: Designed width of the geotechnical pillar in metres. Null if no pillar is specified (pillar_type = none). Critical geotechnical parameter determining pillar stability factor and ore loss to pillars.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; MSHA 30 CFR Part 57; ISO 45001

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the stope design within the mine planning system. Used as the primary business reference across Deswik, Geovia GEMS, and SAP PS work breakdown structures.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design; SAP PS Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)

description: Designed vertical height of the stope from floor to back (crown) in metres. Critical input for geotechnical stability assessment, cable bolt design, and blast energy calculations.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design; MSHA 30 CFR Part 57

description: Designed horizontal length of the stope along the strike direction in metres. Primary dimension used for volume calculation, drill pattern design, and production scheduling.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Human-readable name or label assigned to the stope design, typically incorporating level, drive, and sequence identifiers (e.g., 'L15-OD-001'). Used in engineering drawings, P&IDs, and operational communications.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Designed horizontal width of the stope across the orebody in metres. Determines minimum mining width, dilution exposure, and equipment access requirements.
reference: Deswik Stope Optimization; Geovia GEMS Mine Design

description: Vertical distance in metres between sublevels within the stope design, applicable to sublevel caving (SLC) and sublevel stoping extraction methods. Drives drill ring burden, blast design, and ore recovery modelling.

Timestamp when this stope design record was most recently modified in the data platform, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks design revision history and supports change management auditing.

business_glossary_term: Ventilation Airflow Requirement (cubic metres per second)
description: Minimum required fresh air supply to the stope in cubic metres per second (m³/s) during active extraction, as specified in the stope design. Determined by diesel equipment fleet, blast fume clearance time, and regulatory minimum airflow standards.
reference: MSHA 30 CFR Part 57 Ventilation; ISO 45001; Deswik Mine Planning

Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced from specific feed streams. One batch from one feed stream; one feed stream produces many batches. This links the batch to the feed stream that was processed. The ore_

Foreign key linking to procurement.freight_order. Business justification: Concentrate batches are physically transported via freight orders. Linking concentrate_batch to freight_order enables logistics cost allocation per batch (freight cost per DMT), demurrage tracking, an

Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Concentrate batches record concentrate_grade_pct and head_grade_pct for a specific element. Linking to grade_parameter enables automated bonus/penalty calculation via grade_limit and supports quality

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Concentrate handling (dust generation, moisture control, loading/transport) can trigger environmental incidents (dust emissions) or safety incidents (slips, equipment failures). Batch-level traceabili

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Concentrate samples sent to designated labs (often umpire labs) for final grade determination, moisture content, and penalty element analysis. Critical for sales contract settlement, invoicing, and co

Foreign key linking to customer.letter_of_credit. Business justification: In Mining concentrate sales, individual batches/shipments are covered by specific Letters of Credit issued by the buyer's bank. Finance and commercial teams must link each batch to its governing LC fo
Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this batch production is tracked and reconciled for long-term planning purposes.

Foreign key linking to processing.metallurgical_balance. Business justification: Each concentrate batch is produced within a metallurgical accounting period and contributes to the metallurgical balance reconciliation. Linking concentrate_batch to its metallurgical_balance period e

Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Concentrate batches are traced to source orebody for product provenance, quality control, and customer specifications. Required for sales contract compliance, penalty element tracking, and demonstrati
Reference to the mineral processing plant where this concentrate batch was produced. Links to the plant master record.

Foreign key linking to product.pricing_basis. Business justification: Each concentrate batch is priced according to a specific pricing basis (provisional pricing percentage, quotational period, TC/RC applicability). This is a core commercial process — the pricing_basis

Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Concentrate production must be reconciled against mine production schedules for metal accounting, LOM plan tracking, and mine-to-mill optimization. Critical for integrated production reporting and fin

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Concentrate batches are the primary revenue-generating production output. Profit centre assignment on each batch enables product-level revenue recognition, period-end inventory valuation, and segment

Foreign key linking to sales.quality_certificate. Business justification: A quality certificate is issued per concentrate batch certifying grade and composition for buyer acceptance and invoice settlement. The direct batch-to-certificate link is required for provisional-to-

business_glossary_term: Quality Maintenance Work Order Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Concentrate quality issues (off-spec grade, high impurities) may trace to equipment maintenance needs (screen wear, cyclone apex wear, flotation cell condition). Links product quality problems to main

Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_obligation. Business justification: Mining royalties are calculated on concentrate production volumes and grades per batch. Linking concentrate_batch to royalty_obligation enables automated royalty accrual per production batch — a core

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Concentrate batches are instances of saleable products and must reference the product master for specification compliance, pricing basis, and quality parameters. The current product_type (string) shou

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Concentrate batches are produced during a production shift run. One batch belongs to one shift run; one shift run produces many batches. This links the batch to the processing domain's shift entity.
Reference to the production shift during which this concentrate batch was produced. Used for shift-level production reporting and reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Concentrate batches must be traced to source tenements for royalty payment calculations based on production value, regulatory reporting by tenement, and sales contract compliance requiring origin cert

Foreign key linking to product.product_specification. Business justification: Concentrate batches must meet contractual specifications for grade, moisture, impurities. Critical for quality control, sales contract compliance, and penalty/bonus calculations. Complements existing

Foreign key linking to tailings.waste_rock_dump. Business justification: Some concentrate production processes generate waste rock co-products (e.g., gravity separation rejects) sent to waste rock dumps. Material tracking requirement for inventory reconciliation and waste

Externally-known alphanumeric identifier for the concentrate batch, used across sales, logistics, and laboratory systems. Format: commodity prefix, year, and sequential number (e.g., CU-2024-000123). Sourced from LabWare LIMS batch numbering.

Current lifecycle status of the concentrate batch through the production-to-dispatch workflow: in_progress (being produced), sampled (sample taken for assay), assayed (lab results received), approved (quality approved for sale), dispatched (shipped to customer), rejected (failed quality control). Sourced from LabWare LIMS.
The primary commodity contained in this concentrate batch (e.g., copper, iron ore, lithium, nickel, coal). Drives downstream sales, logistics, and regulatory reporting classification.
reference: JORC Code 2012; CIM Definition Standards; ICMM

description: The grade of the primary commodity in the final concentrate product for this batch, expressed as a percentage (% Cu, % Fe, % Li, % Ni). The key product quality metric used in sales contracts and customer specifications. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ISO 11536 (Iron Ore Analysis)

description: Date and time when this concentrate batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and governance compliance.

The primary transport mode used to dispatch this concentrate batch from the processing plant to the port or customer (e.g., rail, road, ship, pipeline, conveyor). Determines logistics cost allocation and supply chain planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Supply Chain Management Standards

description: Dry weight of the concentrate batch in dry metric tonnes (DMT), calculated after moisture correction. The primary commercial weight measure used for sales contracts, invoicing, and production reporting. Key SSOT field for concentrate production output.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ISO 3087; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Electrical energy consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System. Key OPEX and sustainability metric tracked against LOM plan targets.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 50001 (Energy Management); ICMM

description: Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed into the processing circuit to produce this concentrate batch. Used as the denominator in recovery rate and yield calculations.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Caterpillar MineStar FMS

description: The grade of the primary commodity in the feed ore (Run of Mine (ROM)) entering the processing circuit for this batch, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the input quality before beneficiation. Used for recovery rate calculation and reconciliation.
reference: JORC Code 2012; LabWare LIMS; Geovia GEMS

description: Silica (SiO2) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A key gangue impurity that affects product quality, smelter penalties, and customer acceptance. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.
reference: LabWare LIMS; ISO 11536; Customer Product Specifications

description: Sulfur (S) content in the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%). A critical impurity for smelter operations and environmental compliance. High sulfur levels may attract penalties or rejection. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.
reference: LabWare LIMS; ISO 11536; Environmental Protection Regulations

description: Indicates whether this concentrate batch has passed quality control review and been formally approved for sale or dispatch. True = approved; False = pending or rejected. Set by the metallurgist or quality manager in LabWare LIMS.
Moisture content of the concentrate batch expressed as a percentage (%) of wet weight. Determined by laboratory analysis per ISO 3087. Critical for DMT calculation and product quality compliance.

The P80 particle size of the concentrate batch in micrometres (µm), representing the sieve size through which 80% of the material passes. Critical comminution (size reduction) quality parameter affecting downstream processing efficiency and product specification compliance.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; LabWare LIMS; ISO 4701 (Size Distribution)

description: The mineral processing route used to produce this concentrate batch (e.g., flotation, leaching, Dense Media Separation (DMS), Solvent Extraction Electrowinning (SX-EW), High Pressure Grinding Rolls (HPGR), Semi-Autogenous Grinding (SAG)). Determines applicable quality standards and reagent profiles. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flotation|leaching|DMS|SX-EW|HPGR|SAG|gravity|magnetic_separation — promote to reference product]
Calendar date on which this concentrate batch was produced at the processing plant. Used for daily production reporting and reconciliation against the production schedule.
Precise date and time when the concentrate batch production was completed and the batch was sealed/finalised at the processing plant. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian.

The provisional sale price per dry metric tonne (DMT) in US dollars (USD) agreed at the time of dispatch, subject to final price adjustment based on quotation period commodity prices. Used for initial revenue recognition under IFRS 15.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS 15; LME (London Metal Exchange)

description: Date and time when the concentrate batch was formally approved for sale following quality control review. Represents a key lifecycle milestone in the batch workflow. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.

The quality classification assigned to this concentrate batch based on assay results and product specification compliance. Grade A = premium specification met; Grade B = standard specification met; Grade C = marginal specification; off_spec = outside specification but saleable; rejected = failed quality control and cannot be sold.
reference: LabWare LIMS; Customer Product Specifications; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Total reagent consumption per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in kilograms per tonne (kg/t). Aggregates all flotation, leaching, or other process reagents. Used for operating expenditure (OPEX) tracking and process efficiency analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA CO; ICMM

description: The percentage of the valuable mineral in the feed ore that is successfully recovered into the concentrate product for this batch. Calculated as (concentrate grade × DMT) / (head grade × feed tonnes) × 100. Key processing efficiency KPI tracked against LOM plan targets.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework; OSIsoft PI System

description: The date on which the representative sample was taken from this concentrate batch for laboratory assay analysis. Used for sample chain-of-custody tracking and quality control compliance.

The density of the concentrate slurry (water and ore mixture) in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³) at the point of batch finalisation. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System SCADA. Used for process control and dewatering circuit optimisation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SCADA; Internal Process Standards

description: The average ore processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) achieved during the production of this concentrate batch. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System SCADA historian. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) and capacity utilisation analysis.
Date and time when this concentrate batch record was last modified. Audit trail field used for change tracking, data lineage, and Silver Layer incremental processing in the Databricks Lakehouse.

Process water consumed per tonne of ore processed for this concentrate batch, expressed in cubic metres per tonne (m³/t). Critical environmental compliance metric reported under ISO 14001 and GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 14001; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Total wet weight of the concentrate batch in metric tonnes (t) as measured at the processing plant prior to moisture correction. Used as the basis for moisture content calculation.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ISO 3087 (Iron Ore Moisture Determination)

description: The mass yield of concentrate produced as a percentage of the total ore feed for this batch (DMT concentrate / feed tonnes × 100). Indicates the ratio of product output to ore input and is used for process efficiency benchmarking.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each concentrate stockpile inventory record in the processing domain. Primary key for this entity.

Foreign key linking to laboratory.assay_result. Business justification: Concentrate stockpile inventory management requires certified assay results for grade tracking, blending decisions, and shipment nomination. The last_assay_date plain attribute signals this dependency

Foreign key linking to product.blend. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are built to achieve a specific blend specification before shipment. The blend product defines target grade and component ratios for the stockpile. blend_target_grade is a denor

Foreign key linking to sales.cargo_shipment. Business justification: Cargo shipments draw concentrate from stockpiles for vessel loading. Inventory reconciliation, demurrage management, and loading rate optimization require tracking which stockpile feeds each shipment.

Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles receive product from specific processing circuits (e.g., flotation circuit, SX-EW circuit). Linking concentrate_stockpile to circuit enables circuit-level inventory tracking and

Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Stockpiles hold specific commodities (iron ore concentrate, copper concentrate). Fundamental for inventory management, blending decisions, and production reconciliation. Replaces denormalized commodit

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Stockpile inventory is valued and tracked against cost centres for balance sheet reporting (IAS 2 inventory), NRV testing, and working capital management. Links physical inventory to financial asset a
Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan against which this stockpile inventory is tracked for production target reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to sales.offtake_schedule. Business justification: Stockpile readiness management is a named mining logistics process: concentrate stockpiles are built and managed against specific offtake schedule liftings. Port and logistics teams need to know which
Reference to the processing plant or facility that produced or holds this concentrate stockpile. Links to the plant master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; OSIsoft PI System

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles track inventory against scheduled production for shipment planning, metal accounting, and working capital management. Critical for sales contract fulfillment and financial repor

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles represent finished goods inventory on the balance sheet. Profit centre assignment is required for period-end inventory valuation, IFRS inventory reporting, and segment asset dis

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Stockpiles contain specific saleable products awaiting dispatch. Essential for sales order fulfillment, inventory valuation, and linking physical inventory to sales contracts. Standard in mining logis

Foreign key linking to sales.shipment_nomination. Business justification: Shipment nominations allocate specific stockpile inventory to customer orders and vessel schedules. Logistics planning, inventory management, and customer delivery commitments depend on linking nomina

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Royalty accrual and regulatory reporting require concentrate stockpile inventory to be attributed to the source tenement. Royalty obligations are calculated per tenement; stockpile records without ten

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are managed to meet a specific product specification before shipment nomination. Blending and inventory decisions are driven by specification limits. min_grade_spec and max_mois

Foreign key linking to procurement.warehouse_location. Business justification: Concentrate stockpiles are physical storage locations requiring warehouse management for inventory reconciliation, site access control, environmental compliance, and security. Links processing output

Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate added to this stockpile from production since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents inflows from the processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW). Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; OSIsoft PI System; ICMM Reporting

description: Mineralogical classification of the concentrate product in this stockpile. Determines applicable processing routes, smelter penalty elements, and sales contract terms. E.g., sulphide concentrate from flotation vs oxide concentrate from leaching/SX-EW.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards

description: Timestamp when this concentrate stockpile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage.

Indicates whether dust suppression measures (e.g., water sprays, chemical binders, covers) are currently applied to this stockpile. Relevant for environmental compliance, HSE reporting, and community impact management.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE; ISO 14001; MSHA Dust Control Standards

description: Chemical element or compound symbol for which the product_grade is reported (e.g., 'Cu' for copper, 'Fe' for iron, 'Li2O' for lithium oxide, 'Ni' for nickel). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of the grade value across commodities.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards

description: Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as dry metric tonnes (DMT), adjusted for moisture content. This is the primary inventory quantity used for sales nominations, shipping calculations, and inventory reconciliation. Updated after each survey event.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ICMM Reporting; FOB/CIF contract terms

description: Current physical inventory of concentrate in the stockpile expressed as wet metric tonnes (WMT), inclusive of moisture. Used for physical handling, transport weight calculations, and logistics planning. Distinct from DMT which is the commercial quantity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Pronto Xi Inventory

description: Indicates whether this stockpile inventory has been nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule. True = nominated for an upcoming shipment; False = available for future nomination. Supports logistics scheduling and sales operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Pronto Xi; FOB/CIF Contract Terms

description: Indicates whether this stockpile is subject to an environmental hold that restricts movement or shipment of material (e.g., pending EIS review, acid rock drainage concern, or regulatory compliance investigation). True = hold in place; False = no environmental restriction.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE; ISO 14001; EIS Regulatory Requirements

description: Date of the most recent laboratory assay used to determine the current product grade and moisture content of the stockpile. Used to assess grade data currency for sales nominations and blend optimisation.

Date of the most recent physical survey or measurement used to determine the current stockpile inventory volume and tonnage. Critical for assessing inventory data currency and triggering re-survey workflows when data is stale.
reference: Internal Survey SOP; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Target concentrate inventory level in dry metric tonnes as specified in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan for the current period. Used to assess inventory performance against plan and trigger production rate adjustments.

Engineered maximum storage capacity of the stockpile expressed in dry metric tonnes (DMT). Used for capacity planning, logistics scheduling, and triggering production rate adjustments when inventory approaches capacity limits.
reference: Mine Planning SOP; SAP S/4HANA MM; Hexagon MinePlan

description: Current moisture content of the concentrate stockpile expressed as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert between WMT and DMT, assess handleability, and ensure compliance with shipping moisture limits (e.g., transportable moisture limit for bulk cargo under IMSBC Code).
reference: LabWare LIMS; IMSBC Code (IMO); ISO 3087 (Coal Moisture)

description: Quantity of concentrate in dry metric tonnes nominated against an active shipment or vessel loading schedule from this stockpile. Enables available-to-nominate calculation (inventory_dmt minus nominated_shipment_dmt) for logistics planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Pronto Xi; FOB/CIF Contract Terms

description: Dry metric tonne inventory at the start of the current reconciliation period. Together with additions_dmt and removals_dmt, enables closing inventory calculation and mass balance closure verification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; ICMM Reporting; IFRS 6

description: Comma-separated list of penalty elements detected in the concentrate above contractual threshold levels (e.g., 'As,Pb,Bi'). Penalty elements attract price deductions in smelter contracts and may trigger quarantine or blending requirements. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results.
reference: LabWare LIMS; Sales Contract Terms; ICMM Reporting

description: Current assay-derived grade of the valuable mineral in the concentrate stockpile, expressed as a percentage (%). Represents the weighted average grade of all material currently in the stockpile based on the most recent survey or assay. Critical for sales nomination and blend optimisation.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ISO 11536 (Iron Ore Assay)

description: End date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Nullable if the period is still open.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; ICMM Reporting; IFRS 6

description: Start date of the current inventory reconciliation period for which additions_dmt and removals_dmt are accumulated. Aligns with metallurgical balance accounting periods for mass balance closure.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; ICMM Reporting; IFRS 6

description: Cumulative dry metric tonnes of concentrate removed from this stockpile for shipment, blending, or transfer since the last inventory reconciliation period. Represents outflows to logistics, port, or downstream processing. Used in mass balance and inventory reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Pronto Xi; ICMM Reporting

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this stockpile location, used in logistics scheduling, sales nominations, and inventory reconciliation reports. Sourced from SAP MM material document or plant layout register.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Pronto Xi Inventory

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the concentrate stockpile (e.g., 'Plant Pad A — Copper Concentrate', 'Port Shed 3 — Iron Ore Fines'). Used in operational dashboards and logistics communications.

Free-text operational notes recorded by the metallurgist or inventory controller regarding this stockpile, such as quality observations, blending instructions, survey anomalies, or logistics constraints. Supports operational decision-making and audit trail.

Current operational lifecycle status of the stockpile. 'active' = receiving or dispatching material; 'inactive' = temporarily not in use; 'full' = at maximum capacity; 'quarantine' = material held pending quality resolution; 'closed' = permanently decommissioned.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM; Internal Inventory Management SOP

description: Method used to measure the current stockpile volume and derive inventory tonnage. Determines accuracy and frequency of inventory updates. Values: drone_lidar (aerial LiDAR scan), drone_photogrammetry (aerial photogrammetric survey), manual_survey (ground-based manual measurement), belt_scale (continuous belt weigher), weightometer (in-line weightometer), laser_scan (fixed laser scanner).
reference: Internal Survey SOP; ISO 12745 (Copper Concentrate Sampling)

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this concentrate stockpile record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each metallurgical balance record in the processing domain. Primary key for the metallurgical_balance data product.

Foreign key linking to geology.block_model. Business justification: Mine-to-mill reconciliation compares metallurgical_balance feed_grade and feed_tonnes_dry against block model grade and tonnage predictions. This is a mandatory reconciliation process for JORC reserve
Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit) within the plant for which this balance applies.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are commodity-specific for mass/metal accounting. Essential for JORC compliance, resource reconciliation, and ore reserve reporting. Replaces denormalized commodity text field.
Reference to the qualified competent person responsible for signing off this metallurgical balance, as required under JORC, NI 43-101, or SAMREC reporting standards.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Competent Person requirements; NI 43-101; SAMREC Code 2016

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Metallurgical accounting periods reconcile metal production to cost centres for financial reporting, variance analysis, and AISC calculation. Core mining finance process linking technical metallurgica

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Metallurgical balance reconciliation benchmarks measured_recovery_pct and theoretical_recovery_pct against geological domain predictions. Mine-to-mill reconciliation reports are structured by geologic

Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Metallurgical balance records feed_grade and concentrate_grade for a specific element/parameter. Linking to grade_parameter ensures the balance references the correct parameter definition and unit for
Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this metallurgical balance is being reconciled. Enables tracking of actual performance versus long-term plan targets.
reference: JORC Code 2012; Deswik LOM Plan; Hexagon MinePlan Scheduling

description: Foreign key linking to mine.mining_block. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile processing performance against mining block model predictions for resource model updates, F1/F2/F3 factor calculation, and geological domain recovery characterization.

Foreign key linking to exploration.ore_reserve. Business justification: Mine-to-mill reconciliation compares actual metallurgical balance outcomes against ore reserve predictions. This is a mandatory practice for reserve confidence reporting and investor disclosure. The b

Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are calculated per orebody to track recovery performance by ore source. Essential for resource-to-reserve conversion, mine planning optimization, and validating geological model
Reference to the mineral processing plant or circuit where this metallurgical balance was recorded. Links to the plant master in the processing domain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Plant Master; OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile processing performance against scheduled mine production for period-based metal accounting, recovery variance analysis, and integrated mine-mill performance reporting

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Metallurgical balance is the authoritative period-end production reconciliation document used for financial reporting, royalty calculations, and segment disclosure. Profit centre linkage enables the b

Foreign key linking to exploration.resource_estimate. Business justification: Metallurgical balances validate resource estimate recovery assumptions. Required for reserve conversion, competent person sign-off, and JORC/NI 43-101 compliance. Real business need: resource-to-reser

Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Metallurgical balances are routinely prepared per ROM stockpile source to quantify grade contribution and recovery by ore source. This is a standard mine-to-mill accounting requirement — metallurgists

Foreign key linking to finance.royalty_obligation. Business justification: Metallurgical balance provides the reconciled production figures (tonnes, grade, metal units) used as the basis for period royalty calculations submitted to government. Direct link to royalty_obligati

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Metallurgical balance periods are reconciled per saleable product for financial reporting and production accounting. commodity_id alone is insufficient — a plant producing both copper cathode and copp

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Metallurgical balances reconcile production against tenement reserves and are required for statutory reporting by tenement to regulatory authorities. Essential for reserve depletion tracking and compl

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Metallurgical balance periods must reconcile tailings_tonnes_dry sent to specific TSF. Fundamental accounting control for mass balance closure, regulatory reporting, and TSF capacity verification. Lin
End date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record. Together with accounting_period_start defines the reconciliation window.
reference: JORC Code 2012; IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources

description: Start date of the metallurgical accounting period covered by this balance record (daily, weekly, or monthly). Aligns with the financial reporting calendar.
reference: JORC Code 2012; IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources

description: Name or employee identifier of the competent person or authorised metallurgist who approved the adjustment or restatement of this balance record. Required for JORC-compliant audit trail and regulatory sign-off.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Competent Person requirements; SEC S-K 1300; CIM Definition Standards 2014

description: Free-text description of the reason for any adjustment or restatement applied to this version of the metallurgical balance (e.g., assay rerun, sampling error correction, instrument calibration update, stockpile reweigh). Required for audit trail and competent person sign-off.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Competent Person requirements; SEC S-K 1300; ICMM Reporting

description: Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was formally approved and signed off by the competent person. Marks the transition to approved status in the balance workflow.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Competent Person requirements; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Externally-known business identifier for the metallurgical balance record, used in reports, sign-off documentation, and regulatory submissions. Format: METBAL-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Current workflow status of the metallurgical balance record: draft (in preparation), submitted (awaiting review), under_review (being assessed by competent person), approved (signed off), or rejected (returned for correction).
reference: JORC Code 2012 Competent Person requirements; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Assayed grade of the concentrate product expressed in the same unit as feed_grade_unit. Determines the commercial value and smelter payability of the concentrate.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; LabWare LIMS Assay Module; CIM Definition Standards 2014

description: Total dry metric tonnes of concentrate product produced during the accounting period. Represents the valuable mineral product output from the processing circuit.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; SAP S/4HANA CO Production Order; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Date and time when this metallurgical balance record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
reference: ICMM Reporting Framework; SAP S/4HANA Audit Trail

description: Estimated percentage of waste rock (gangue) dilution in the feed material, representing the proportion of non-ore material included during mining and haulage. Impacts feed grade and recovery.
reference: JORC Code 2012; Hexagon MinePlan Grade Control; Geovia GEMS

description: Total electrical energy consumed by the processing circuit during the accounting period in kilowatt hours. Key OPEX component and sustainability metric reported under GRI and ICMM frameworks.
reference: OSIsoft PI System SCADA; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ICMM Reporting

description: Assayed head grade of the feed material expressed as percentage (%) for base metals or grams per tonne (g/t) for precious metals. Determined by LabWare LIMS assay results on feed samples.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; LabWare LIMS Assay Module; SAMREC Code 2016

description: Unit of measure for the feed head grade value: pct (percentage), g_per_t (grams per tonne), kg_per_t (kilograms per tonne), or ppm (parts per million). Varies by commodity.
Moisture content of the feed material as a percentage of wet weight. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting purposes.
reference: LabWare LIMS Sample Management; JORC Code 2012

description: Total dry metric tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing circuit during the accounting period. Dry weight basis is the standard for metallurgical accounting.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; ICMM Reporting Framework; SAP S/4HANA CO Production Order

description: Indicates whether this record is the current (latest approved) version of the metallurgical balance for the given period and circuit. True = current version; False = superseded version retained for audit trail.
Mass balance closure expressed as a percentage: (concentrate_tonnes_dry + tailings_tonnes_dry) / feed_tonnes_dry × 100. Values outside 98–102% typically trigger investigation and adjustment.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Actual metallurgical recovery percentage calculated from assayed feed, concentrate, and tailings grades and tonnes using the standard two-product formula. Represents the proportion of valuable mineral recovered into concentrate.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; SAMREC Code 2016; CIM Definition Standards 2014

description: Total contained metal units in the feed stream for the period, calculated as feed_tonnes_dry × feed_grade. Expressed in tonnes of metal or troy ounces depending on commodity. Key input to JORC resource-to-reserve reconciliation.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; SEC S-K 1300; CIM Definition Standards 2014

description: Total contained metal units recovered into concentrate for the period, calculated as concentrate_tonnes_dry × concentrate_grade. Used in LOM plan tracking and sales reconciliation.
reference: JORC Code 2012 Table 1; SEC S-K 1300; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Granularity of the metallurgical accounting period: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annual. Determines the reporting frequency and aggregation level for LOM plan tracking.

description: Theoretical or planned recovery percentage for the period based on LOM plan targets, ore type characterisation, and process design parameters. Used as the benchmark for variance analysis.
