Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.
reference: ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: The specific stage of the flotation circuit captured in this event record. Rougher is the primary separation stage; scavenger recovers minerals from rougher tailings; cleaner upgrades rougher concentrate grade; recleaner further upgrades cleaner concentrate. Critical for stage-by-stage metallurgical accounting.
reference: ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Dosage rate of the collector reagent (e.g., xanthate, dithiophosphate) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Collector promotes hydrophobicity of target mineral surfaces to enable bubble attachment. Key reagent optimisation variable.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Pronto Xi Inventory; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Grade of the valuable mineral in the flotation concentrate product, expressed as a percentage (%). Measures the quality of the separation achieved. Higher concentrate grade reduces downstream smelting costs and improves payable metal. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: The planned or contractually required concentrate grade for the valuable mineral, expressed as a percentage (%). Used to assess whether the flotation circuit is meeting product quality specifications for offtake agreements and smelter contracts.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD; Hexagon MinePlan; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: Total dry mass of concentrate produced by the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in metallurgical accounting mass balance and payable metal calculations.
reference: ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: Timestamp when this flotation event record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.

Dosage rate of the depressant reagent (e.g., sodium cyanide, zinc sulphate, CMC) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Depressants selectively prevent flotation of gangue minerals or penalty elements, improving concentrate grade and selectivity.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Pronto Xi Inventory; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Descriptive reason for any unplanned or planned downtime experienced by the flotation circuit during this event period (e.g., pump failure, cell maintenance, reagent outage, power interruption). Null if no downtime occurred. Used for reliability analysis and maintenance planning in Infor EAM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pump_failure|cell_maintenance|reagent_outage|power_interruption|planned_shutdown|instrumentation_fault — promote to reference product]

The real-world timestamp marking the end of the flotation measurement period (typically shift end). Combined with start timestamp to define the measurement window for throughput and reagent consumption calculations.

Externally-known business identifier for this flotation event record, formatted as FLT-YYYY-MM-DD-NNNN. Used for cross-system reference, metallurgical accounting reconciliation, and audit trail. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA CO-PA

description: The real-world timestamp marking the start of the flotation measurement period (typically shift start). Used for time-series alignment with OSIsoft PI historian data and SCADA records. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category.

Current workflow state of the flotation event record. Draft indicates data entry in progress; confirmed indicates shift data locked; reviewed indicates metallurgist sign-off; approved indicates inclusion in metallurgical balance; voided indicates record invalidated. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: Head grade of the valuable mineral (e.g., copper, lithium, nickel) in the flotation feed, expressed as a percentage (%). Derived from assay results and used as the denominator in circuit recovery calculations. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay data.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: The 80th percentile passing particle size (P80) of the flotation feed, measured in micrometres (µm). Particle size is a critical determinant of flotation performance — coarse particles are difficult to float while ultra-fine particles suffer from poor bubble attachment. Sourced from LabWare LIMS particle size analysis.
reference: LabWare LIMS; SME Mineral Processing Handbook; JORC Code 2012

description: Mass flow rate of ore slurry fed into the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/h). Key throughput metric for metallurgical accounting and LOM plan performance tracking.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: Depth of the froth layer maintained in the flotation cell during the event period, measured in millimetres (mm). Froth depth is a critical control parameter influencing concentrate grade and recovery trade-off. Deeper froth generally improves grade at the expense of recovery.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Dosage rate of the frother reagent (e.g., MIBC, pine oil, polyglycol) added to the flotation circuit, expressed in grams per dry metric tonne of feed (g/t). Frother stabilises the froth layer and controls bubble size distribution. Critical for froth stability and concentrate transport.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Pronto Xi Inventory; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Average rotational speed of the flotation cell impellers during the event period, measured in revolutions per minute (RPM). Impeller speed controls slurry agitation, air dispersion, and bubble-particle contact. Monitored via OSIsoft PI SCADA.

Dosage rate of lime (calcium hydroxide or calcium oxide) added to the flotation circuit for pH control, expressed in kilograms per dry metric tonne of feed (kg/t). Lime is typically the highest-volume reagent in sulphide flotation circuits and a significant OPEX driver.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Pronto Xi Inventory; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: The ratio of concentrate mass to feed mass expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates the proportion of total feed mass reporting to the concentrate. High mass pull may indicate over-flotation and gangue entrainment; low mass pull may indicate under-recovery. Key flotation control parameter.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SME Mineral Processing Handbook; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: The primary valuable mineral being recovered in this flotation circuit event (e.g., copper, nickel, lithium). Determines the applicable reagent suite, recovery benchmarks, and product specifications. Essential for multi-commodity processing plants. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: copper|nickel|lithium|zinc|lead|molybdenum|gold|silver — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: JORC Code 2012; LabWare LIMS; Geovia GEMS

description: Classification of the ore type being processed in the flotation circuit during this event (e.g., sulphide, oxide, mixed, transitional). Ore type significantly influences reagent selection, dosage rates, and achievable recovery. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sulphide|oxide|mixed|transitional|primary|secondary — promote to reference product]

Solids content of the flotation feed slurry expressed as a percentage by mass (% solids). Pulp density affects reagent effectiveness, bubble-particle collision rates, and overall flotation kinetics. Monitored continuously via OSIsoft PI.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: pH of the flotation pulp during the event period. pH is a critical chemical environment parameter that governs mineral surface chemistry, collector adsorption, and selectivity between valuable minerals and gangue. Typically controlled with lime addition.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: The planned or budgeted circuit recovery percentage for this flotation stage as defined in the LOM plan or metallurgical design criteria. Enables variance analysis between actual and target recovery for performance management and LOM plan tracking.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines

description: The LabWare LIMS sample identifier for the assay samples collected during this flotation event (feed, concentrate, and tailings). Enables traceability between the flotation performance record and the underlying laboratory assay results supporting grade data.

Temperature of the flotation feed slurry during the event period, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Slurry temperature affects reagent solubility, reaction kinetics, and froth stability. Particularly relevant in cold-climate operations and heated leach-flotation circuits.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Grade of the valuable mineral remaining in the flotation tailings stream, expressed as a percentage (%). Indicates mineral losses to the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF). Used to calculate circuit recovery and identify optimisation opportunities. Sourced from LabWare LIMS.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Total dry mass of tailings discharged from the flotation circuit during the event period, measured in dry metric tonnes (t). Used in mass balance reconciliation and TSF volumetric planning.
reference: ICMM Metallurgical Accounting Guidelines; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Timestamp when this flotation event record was last modified. Tracks data corrections, metallurgist adjustments, and re-assay updates. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.

business_glossary_term: Water Addition Rate (Cubic Metres Per Hour)
description: Rate of process water added to the flotation circuit to maintain target pulp density, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Water consumption is a key environmental and OPEX metric, particularly in water-stressed mining regions subject to ISO 14001 reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each leaching operation event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the leach_event data product.

Foreign key linking to equipment.asset. Business justification: Leach events occur in specific leach tanks or heap leach pad assets. Hydromet operations track extraction efficiency and reagent consumption per tank asset to schedule maintenance, manage asset life,
Reference to the leach circuit or processing unit (heap leach pad, CIL/CIP tank, vat leach vessel) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Leaching extracts specific commodities (copper, gold, nickel, uranium). Fundamental process-commodity link for hydrometallurgical operations. Replaces denormalized commodity text field. Essential for

Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: Leach events process specific feed streams. One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the leach event to the feed stream being processed.

Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: Leach events record feed_grade_pct and residue_grade_pct for a specific element. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition for extraction efficiency reporting, regulatory complia

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Leaching uses hazardous chemicals (cyanide for gold, sulfuric acid for copper). Incidents during leach operations (solution spills, gas releases, exposure events) require direct traceability to leach

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Leach solution samples (PLS, raffinate, residue) analyzed at specific labs for extraction efficiency monitoring and reagent optimization. Standard hydrometallurgical process control requirement for co

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Leach tank/pad equipment failures (agitators, pumps, solution distribution) during leach events generate maintenance work orders. Links hydrometallurgical process issues to maintenance response for ex
Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the leach event occurred. Supports multi-site reporting and plant-level performance benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location; OSIsoft PI Asset Framework

description: Identifier of the representative leach solution or residue sample submitted to the laboratory for assay analysis during this event. Links to LabWare LIMS sample management records for quality control and mass balance verification.

Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Leach events must reference production schedules for heap leach ore tracking, metal recovery forecasting, and ore type extraction performance analysis. Critical for heap leach operations and long-term

Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Leach performance is ore-source dependent. Essential for heap leach/tank leach optimization, resource model metallurgical assumptions validation. Real business need: extraction efficiency tracking by

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Leach events occur during a production shift run. One leach event belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many leach events. This links the event to the processing domain's shift entity.
Reference to the operational shift during which this leach event was recorded. Enables shift-based production reporting and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR/PP Module; Mine Shift Schedule

description: Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Leach residues/tailings deposited to specific TSF. Required for geochemical risk management (ARD assessment), TSF design compliance, and environmental monitoring. Tracks ore_feed_mass_t minus recovere

Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: leach_event is a specialized subtype of unit_operation_event for heap/tank/vat leaching operations. Linking leach_event to its parent unit_operation_event enables the supertype/subtype pattern for uni

Operational batch or lot number assigned to the ore charge or solution batch processed in this leach event. Used for traceability from ROM feed through to final concentrate or pregnant leach solution (PLS). Sourced from LabWare LIMS or SCADA batch management.
reference: LabWare LIMS; OSIsoft PI System Batch Framework

description: Free-text operational comments or notes recorded by the process operator or metallurgist for this leach event. May include observations on solution colour, foam, equipment behaviour, or other qualitative process indicators not captured by structured fields.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Event Frames; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification

description: Timestamp when this leach event record was first captured and persisted in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and silver layer ingestion tracking.

Identifies the originating operational system from which this leach event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between automated sensor data (OSIsoft PI, SCADA) and manually entered or LIMS-derived records.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; LabWare LIMS; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage

description: Concentration of the target dissolved metal in the pregnant leach solution (PLS) or leachate, measured in parts per million (ppm). Primary indicator of leaching effectiveness and solution grade. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results or inline OSIsoft PI analyser tags.
reference: LabWare LIMS; OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code 2012

description: Concentration of dissolved oxygen in the leach solution, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Dissolved oxygen is a critical parameter for cyanide leaching of gold and silver, directly influencing metal dissolution rates. Monitored via OSIsoft PI DO analysers.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 5814 (Water Quality - Dissolved Oxygen)

description: The actual date and time when the leaching operation concluded for this event or batch. Used in conjunction with event_start_timestamp to derive leach cycle duration.

Externally-known business identifier for the leach event, typically sourced from the OSIsoft PI System or SCADA batch/event log. Used for cross-system traceability and operational reporting (e.g., LE-2024-00123).
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA PM Module

description: The actual date and time when the leaching operation commenced for this event or batch. Principal real-world event time used for duration calculations, shift allocation, and production scheduling alignment.

Current lifecycle status of the leach event. Indicates whether the leaching operation is actively running, has completed normally, was aborted due to process upset, is on hold, or is pending quality review before finalisation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA MES Integration

description: Percentage of the target metal extracted from the ore feed into solution during this leach event, calculated as (metal dissolved / metal in feed) × 100. Key performance indicator for process optimisation, LOM plan reconciliation, and recovery rate reporting. Sourced from LabWare LIMS mass balance.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Mineral Processing Reporting; LabWare LIMS

description: Head grade of the ore feed entering the leach circuit, expressed as a percentage (%) for base metals or g/t equivalent converted to percentage for precious metals. Sourced from assay results in LabWare LIMS. Critical input for extraction efficiency and recovery rate calculations.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards

description: Total elapsed time of the leaching operation for this event, measured in hours. Represents the actual leach time from solution application/charge to drain/discharge. Used for cycle time optimisation, throughput modelling, and LOM plan variance analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Hexagon MinePlan Scheduling

description: Classification of the leaching method applied in this event. Distinguishes between heap leach (copper, gold), tank leach (CIL/CIP for gold), vat leach, and agitation leach configurations. Drives process parameter benchmarking and recovery rate analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: heap_leach|tank_leach|vat_leach|cil|cip|agitation_leach — promote to reference product]
reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Mineral Processing Standards

description: Total dry mass of ore charged to the leach circuit for this event, measured in metric tonnes. Represents the principal quantitative input for recovery rate calculation and throughput reporting against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets.
reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Mineral Processing Reporting

description: Mineralogical classification of the ore processed in this leach event. Ore type significantly influences leaching chemistry, reagent selection, and achievable recovery rates. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control assay data.
reference: Geovia GEMS; JORC Code 2012; CIM Standards

description: Oxidation-reduction potential (ORP) of the leach solution measured in millivolts (mV). Critical electrochemical parameter for monitoring leaching conditions, particularly in cyanide leach (gold) and SX-EW (copper) circuits. Sourced from OSIsoft PI inline analysers.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SCADA Integration; ICMM Cyanide Management Code

description: The OSIsoft PI System tag name or event frame reference associated with this leach event. Enables direct traceability back to the process historian for raw time-series data retrieval, audit, and re-processing. Applicable when data_source is pi_system or scada.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework; SCADA Integration

description: Indicates whether a process upset or abnormal operating condition occurred during this leach event (True = upset occurred, False = normal operation). Process upsets may include pH excursions, reagent dosing failures, pump trips, or temperature deviations. Triggers investigation and corrective action workflows in IsoMetrix.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE Module; ISO 45001; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification

description: Concentration of the primary leaching reagent (e.g., cyanide for gold CIL/CIP, sulphuric acid for copper heap leach, caustic soda for lithium leach) in the leach solution, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Key process control parameter for optimising extraction efficiency and reagent cost (OPEX).
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ICMM Cyanide Management Code; ISO 14001

description: Total mass of primary leaching reagent consumed during this leach event, measured in kilograms. Used for OPEX tracking, reagent cost allocation, and environmental reporting of chemical usage. Sourced from Pronto Xi inventory consumption records and SCADA flow meters.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Pronto Xi; ICMM Cyanide Management Code

description: Type of primary leaching reagent used in this event. Determines applicable safety, environmental, and regulatory requirements. Examples include sodium cyanide (gold CIL/CIP), sulphuric acid (copper heap leach), and caustic soda (lithium leach circuits). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cyanide|sulphuric_acid|caustic_soda|ammonia|chlorine|hydrochloric_acid — promote to reference product]
reference: ICMM Cyanide Management Code; ISO 14001; IsoMetrix HSE Module

description: Grade of the target metal remaining in the leach residue (tailings) after the leaching event, expressed as a percentage. Used to calculate actual metal recovery and identify process losses. Sourced from LabWare LIMS assay results on residue samples.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Density of the ore-solution slurry (water and ore mixture) in the leach circuit, measured in grams per cubic centimetre (g/cm³). Applicable to tank leach and CIL/CIP circuits. Monitored via OSIsoft PI density instruments. Affects mass transfer and reagent efficiency.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ICMM Mineral Processing Standards

business_glossary_term: Solution Flow Rate (Cubic Metres per Hour)
description: Volumetric flow rate of leach solution through the circuit during this event, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Monitored via OSIsoft PI flow instrumentation. Influences reagent distribution, contact time, and overall leaching kinetics.

Measured pH of the leach solution at the time of this event or at the representative sampling point within the event window. Critical process control parameter for reagent efficiency, metal dissolution kinetics, and environmental compliance (acid mine drainage prevention).
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 10523 (Water Quality - pH Determination); ISO 14001

description: Temperature of the leach solution in degrees Celsius (°C) measured during this event. Temperature directly influences leaching kinetics, reagent consumption rates, and metal dissolution efficiency. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System instrumentation.

Total volume of leach solution applied or circulated during this event, measured in cubic metres (m³). Used for water balance reporting, reagent dosing calculations, and environmental water management compliance.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Operational identifier of the specific leach tank, leach pad cell, or vat vessel used in this event (e.g., TANK-03, PAD-CELL-7B). Sourced from the OSIsoft PI asset hierarchy or SCADA tag configuration. Enables equipment-level performance tracking.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework; SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location

description: Planned or budgeted extraction efficiency percentage for this leach event type and ore type, as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or metallurgical test work. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned recovery performance.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik LOM Planning; JORC Code 2012

description: Timestamp when this leach event record was last modified or updated in the data platform. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance.

Free-text description of the process upset or abnormal condition that occurred during this leach event, if applicable. Populated when process_upset_flag is True. Captures the nature of the deviation for root cause analysis and corrective action tracking.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE Module; SAP S/4HANA PM Notification; ISO 45001

description: Primary key for operational_exception
Identifier of the primary piece of equipment directly involved in or causing the operational exception. Links to the equipment asset register.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Register; SAP PM Equipment Master

description: Foreign key linking to mine.blast_execution. Business justification: Processing upsets (crusher blockages, mill overloads, poor fragmentation) are frequently caused by blast quality issues. Linking operational exceptions to the originating blast enables blast-to-plant
Identifier of the specific processing circuit affected by the exception (e.g., SAG mill circuit, flotation circuit, leach circuit, SX-EW circuit, DMS circuit). Links to the circuit master.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework; SAP PM Functional Location Hierarchy

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Operational exceptions (unplanned downtime, process upsets) cause production losses that must be costed and reported against cost centres for variance analysis, insurance claims, and OEE cost impact r

Foreign key linking to equipment.downtime_event. Business justification: Processing operational exceptions (circuit trips, process upsets) are directly caused by or cause equipment downtime events. Linking them supports cross-domain root cause analysis, OEE loss categorisa

Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_report. Business justification: Processing operational exceptions (mill trips, pump failures, process upsets) directly generate maintenance failure reports for the same event. Mining operations use this cross-domain link for integra

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Operational exceptions (downtime, process upsets) occur at specific functional locations. Links exceptions to maintenance functional location hierarchy for root cause analysis, maintenance planning, a

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: Processing plant downtime events that trigger HSE incidents must reference the formal incident record for compliance reporting and root cause analysis. The current hse_incident_reference (string) shou
Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which production loss from this exception is measured. Enables variance reporting between actual downtime impact and LOM plan targets.
reference: Deswik LOM Schedule; Hexagon MinePlan; JORC Code Resource and Reserve Reporting

description: Identifier of the mineral processing plant where the operational exception occurred. Links to the plant master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Functional Location; OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework

description: Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Process upsets and environmental exceedances at a plant may constitute breaches of tenement regulatory conditions, triggering mandatory regulatory reporting. Regulators require that each notifiable op

Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Operational exceptions (process upsets, control deviations) are reviewed against the governing risk assessment to determine if critical controls were breached. Mining process safety management require

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Operational exceptions occur during a production shift run. One exception belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many exceptions. This links the exception to the processing domain's shift entity.
Identifier of the shift report during which this operational exception was recorded. Links to the mine or plant shift report.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM; Caterpillar MineStar Shift Report

description: Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Processing plant operational exceptions are frequently caused by TSF constraints — tailings line blockages, TSF freeboard limits forcing throughput curtailment, or TSF pump failures. Linking exception

Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: Operational exceptions (unplanned downtime, process upsets, alarm events) occur during specific unit operation events. Linking operational_exception to unit_operation_event provides direct traceabilit
Identifier of the maintenance work order raised in response to this operational exception. Enables linkage to the maintenance domain for corrective or emergency work orders.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order; SAP PM Work Order (PM Order)

description: The measured actual value of the process parameter at the time of the deviation or alarm event. Compared against setpoint_value to determine deviation magnitude and against alarm limits for threshold breach analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Real-Time Data; SCADA Historian

description: The configured high alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the high alarm boundary and to support alarm rationalisation analysis.
reference: ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems; OSIsoft PI System Alarm Configuration

description: The configured low alarm threshold for the process parameter at the time of the event. Used to contextualise whether the actual_value breached the low alarm boundary.
reference: ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems; OSIsoft PI System Alarm Configuration

description: Description of the corrective action taken by the operator or maintenance team to resolve the operational exception and restore normal plant operations. Feeds maintenance knowledge base and SOP review processes.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Resolution; SAP PM Notification Resolution Text; ISO 45001 Corrective Action

description: Timestamp when this operational exception record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Standard; IFRS Data Audit Requirements

description: Method by which the operational exception was first detected: scada_alarm (automated SCADA/PLC alarm), operator_observation (manual observation by plant operator), dcs_alert (Distributed Control System alert), lab_result (assay or process sample result), inspection (physical inspection finding), predictive_alert (predictive maintenance or process analytics alert).
reference: OSIsoft PI System Alarm Management; ISA-18.2; Caterpillar MineStar Equipment Health

description: Absolute difference between actual_value and setpoint_value at the time of the exception. Stored as a business field to support process control quality analysis and trend reporting without requiring arithmetic on raw values.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Performance Monitoring; ISA-18.2 Alarm Management

description: Total elapsed duration of the operational exception in minutes, calculated from event_start_timestamp to event_end_timestamp. Stored as a business field to support OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) availability loss reporting without requiring real-time timestamp arithmetic.
reference: ISO 22400 OEE KPI Standard; Caterpillar MineStar Downtime Reporting

description: Operational category of the exception cause: mechanical (equipment failure), electrical (power or instrumentation fault), process (reagent, slurry, or chemistry issue), planned_maintenance (scheduled shutdown), feed_shortage (insufficient ROM feed), or parameter_exceedance (process variable breach). Drives OEE loss categorisation and maintenance domain routing.
reference: ISO 14224 Failure Classification; SAP PM Notification Category

description: Date and time when the operational exception event was resolved and normal operations resumed. Null if the event is still active. Used with event_start_timestamp to calculate event duration.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Event Frames; ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations Management

description: Human-readable business identifier for the operational exception event, used for cross-referencing with maintenance work orders, shift reports, and process control logs. Format: OE-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Reference; SAP PM Notification Number

description: Date and time when the operational exception event commenced, as recorded by the SCADA/PLC system or operator. This is the principal real-world event time used for duration calculation and OEE loss attribution.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Event Frames; ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations Management

description: Current lifecycle status of the operational exception record: open (event active, investigation pending), in_progress (corrective action underway), resolved (root cause identified and corrective action completed), closed (verified and signed off), cancelled (duplicate or incorrectly raised).
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Status; SAP PM Notification Status

description: Classification of the operational exception by its fundamental nature: downtime (equipment or circuit stoppage), parameter_deviation (process variable outside set-point tolerance), alarm (SCADA/PLC alarm triggered), near_miss (safety or process near-miss), or process_upset (uncontrolled process condition). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: downtime|parameter_deviation|alarm|near_miss|process_upset — promote to reference product if additional types are required]
reference: OSIsoft PI System Alarm Management; ISA-18.2 Management of Alarm Systems

business_glossary_term: Health Safety and Environment (HSE) Incident Raised Indicator
description: Indicates whether this operational exception also triggered a formal HSE incident report in the IsoMetrix system (True) or not (False). Enables cross-domain linkage between process exceptions and HSE incident management.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE Incident Management; ISO 45001; MSHA Incident Reporting

description: Description of the immediate containment action taken at the time of the exception to prevent further production loss, equipment damage, or safety risk, prior to full corrective action. Distinct from corrective_action which addresses root cause.
reference: IsoMetrix Incident Management; ISO 45001 Emergency Response; SAP PM Notification

description: Indicates whether the downtime or exception event was planned (True) or unplanned (False). Planned events include scheduled maintenance shutdowns and planned feed stoppages. Used to segregate planned vs unplanned downtime in OEE calculations.
reference: ISO 22400 OEE KPI Standard; ICMM Operational Performance Reporting

description: OEE loss category to which this exception is attributed for OEE calculation purposes: availability (equipment or circuit not running), performance (running below rated throughput), or quality (product not meeting grade specification). Aligns with ISO 22400 OEE decomposition.
reference: ISO 22400-2 OEE KPI Standard; ICMM Operational Performance Reporting

description: Human-readable description of the process parameter that deviated, providing context beyond the SCADA tag code. Examples: SAG Mill Feed Rate (tph), Flotation Cell Level (%), Leach Tank pH.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; P&ID (Piping and Instrumentation Diagram) Documentation

description: The OSIsoft PI System or SCADA tag identifier for the process parameter that deviated from its set-point. Applicable only for event_type = parameter_deviation or alarm. Examples: SAG_MILL_FEED_TPH, FLOTATION_CELL_LEVEL_PCT, LEACH_TANK_PH.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Tag Naming Convention; ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols

description: Engineering unit of measure for the process parameter set-point and actual values. Examples: tph (tonnes per hour), %, pH, m3/h, kPa, degC, g/L. Ensures correct interpretation of setpoint_value and actual_value.
reference: ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; OSIsoft PI System Engineering Units

description: Estimated ore or concentrate production lost in dry metric tonnes as a direct consequence of this operational exception. Used for OEE production loss attribution, LOM plan variance reporting, and OPEX impact quantification.
reference: JORC Code Production Reporting; ISO 22400 OEE KPI Standard; ICMM Operational Performance

description: Standardised code classifying the root cause of the exception per the plant's failure taxonomy (e.g., ISO 14224 cause codes). Enables structured root cause analysis and trend reporting across events.
reference: ISO 14224 Equipment Reliability and Maintenance Data; SAP PM Cause Code Catalogue

description: Detailed narrative description of the identified root cause of the operational exception, as determined through investigation. Supports root cause analysis (RCA), recurring failure identification, and process improvement initiatives.
reference: ISO 14224 Root Cause Classification; Infor EAM Failure Analysis; SAP PM Cause Code

description: The target or set-point value for the process parameter at the time of the deviation event. Used in conjunction with actual_value to quantify the magnitude of the deviation. Applicable for event_type = parameter_deviation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Set-Point Management; ISA-88 Batch Control Standard

description: Severity rating of the operational exception indicating its impact on plant throughput, safety, and production targets. Critical: full circuit or plant stoppage; High: significant throughput reduction; Medium: partial degradation; Low: minor deviation with negligible production impact.
reference: IsoMetrix Risk Matrix; ISO 45001 Risk Assessment; ICMM Critical Control Management

description: Calendar date of the shift during which the operational exception commenced. Used for daily and shift-level production reporting and exception trend analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA Production Reporting; ISA-95 Manufacturing Operations

description: Type of shift during which the operational exception occurred: day, night, or afternoon. Supports shift-level performance analysis and identification of shift-specific exception patterns.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR Shift Management; ISA-95 Personnel and Equipment Scheduling

description: Estimated reduction in processing throughput rate in tonnes per hour (tph) attributable to this exception event. Supports OEE rate loss analysis and process bottleneck identification.
reference: ISO 22400 OEE KPI Standard; Caterpillar MineStar Productivity Reporting

description: Timestamp when this operational exception record was most recently modified, such as when root cause, corrective action, or resolution details were updated. Supports audit trail and data currency tracking.
reference: ISO 8601 Date and Time Standard; IFRS Data Audit Requirements

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each reagent consumption record in the mineral processing circuit. Primary key for this transactional entity.

Reference to the mineral processing circuit (e.g., flotation, leaching, SX-EW, DMS) where the reagent was applied. Enables circuit-level OPEX analysis.

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Reagent costs (cyanide, lime, collectors) are posted to specific GL accounts for AISC C1 cost reporting. reagent_consumption already has cost_centre but lacks GL account reference needed for automated

Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_issue. Business justification: In mining ERP, reagent consumption triggers a goods issue (SAP movement type 201/261) from warehouse stock. Linking reagent_consumption to goods_issue enables material document reconciliation, invento

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Reagent quality samples (collectors, frothers, flocculants) analyzed at specific labs for specification compliance and batch acceptance testing. Supply chain quality control requirement to ensure reag
Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the reagent was consumed. Supports multi-site OPEX reporting and benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Centre; Internal Asset Hierarchy

description: Foreign key linking to hse.risk_assessment. Business justification: Reagent handling activities (cyanide dosing, acid addition, xanthate use) require specific task-level risk assessments beyond the chemical register entry. Mining HSE systems link reagent consumption r

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Reagent consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the consumption to the processing domain's shift
Reference to the operational shift during which the reagent was consumed. Links to the shift master for date, crew, and time-of-day context.
Reference to the reagent master record identifying the specific chemical reagent (collector, frother, flocculant, lime, cyanide, acid, solvent, etc.) consumed.
reference: Pronto Xi Inventory Master; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: Reagent consumption occurs during specific unit operations (flotation, leaching, etc.). Linking reagent_consumption to unit_operation_event provides granular traceability of reagent use to the specifi

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Reagent consumption during maintenance activities (pump repairs, system flushes, reagent line maintenance) is costed against the work order for AISC and opex allocation. Mining cost accountants requir
Supplier batch or lot number of the reagent consumed. Enables traceability for quality incidents, reagent performance investigations, and environmental compliance audits.
reference: Pronto Xi Inventory Batch Management; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; LabWare LIMS

description: Externally-visible business identifier for this consumption record, formatted as RC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN. Used for cross-referencing with SAP material documents, LIMS records, and environmental compliance reports.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document; Internal Numbering Standard

description: Current lifecycle status of the reagent consumption record. Draft records are pending operator confirmation; confirmed records are locked for OPEX reporting; adjusted records have been corrected post-shift; cancelled records are voided.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Movement Status; Internal Process Control Standard

description: The principal real-world date and time at which the reagent consumption event occurred or was recorded by the process control system (OSIsoft PI / SCADA). Distinct from record audit timestamps.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Process Historian; ISO 8601 Timestamp Standard

description: Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost and total cost fields (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Required for multi-currency OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: Origin of the reagent consumption data: SCADA/OSIsoft PI automated reading, manual operator entry, LabWare LIMS import, SAP ERP goods issue, or estimated value. Critical for data quality assessment and audit trail.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; LabWare LIMS; SAP S/4HANA MM; Internal Data Quality Standard

description: Rate at which the reagent was dosed into the circuit, expressed in grams per tonne (g/t) of ore processed or kilograms per hour (kg/hr) depending on the reagent type and circuit. Core metric for reagent optimisation and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.
reference: ICMM Mineral Processing Best Practice; Internal Process Control Standard; OSIsoft PI System

description: Unit of measure for the reagent dosage rate field. Common units include grams per tonne (g/t) for solid reagents dosed relative to ore throughput, and kilograms per hour (kg/hr) for liquid reagents dosed by flow rate.
reference: ICMM Mineral Processing Best Practice; Internal Process Control Standard

description: Indicates whether this reagent consumption event requires mandatory environmental compliance reporting (e.g., cyanide usage under ICMC, acid consumption under EIS conditions). Drives automated compliance workflow in IsoMetrix.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; IsoMetrix Environmental Monitoring; ICMM Cyanide Management Code

description: Head grade (percentage) of the valuable mineral in the ore feed to the circuit during the shift. Contextualises reagent dosage decisions — higher feed grades may require adjusted reagent rates for optimal recovery.
reference: LabWare LIMS Assay Results; JORC Code; Internal Grade Control Standard

description: Indicates whether the reagent is classified as hazardous under applicable environmental and safety regulations (e.g., cyanide, sulphuric acid). Triggers mandatory environmental compliance reporting and PPE requirements per ISO 14001 and ISO 45001.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; IsoMetrix HSE; MSHA Regulations

description: Free-text field for operator or supervisor notes regarding the reagent consumption event, such as reasons for deviation from planned dosage, equipment issues affecting dosing, or reagent quality observations.
Total dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the shift. Used as the denominator for calculating specific reagent consumption (g/t) and benchmarking against LOM plan targets.
reference: Internal Mineral Processing KPI Standard; JORC Code; OSIsoft PI System

description: Life of Mine (LOM) plan target dosage rate for this reagent in this circuit, in the same unit as dosage_rate. Enables variance analysis between actual and planned reagent consumption for process optimisation.
reference: Deswik Mine Planning; Hexagon MinePlan; Internal LOM Plan Standard

description: Life of Mine (LOM) plan target quantity of reagent for this shift and circuit in kilograms. Used for budget variance reporting and OPEX performance management against the approved mine plan.
reference: Deswik Mine Planning; SAP S/4HANA CO Budget; Internal LOM Plan Standard

description: SAP or Pronto Xi purchase order number under which the reagent batch was procured. Enables three-way matching (PO, goods receipt, invoice) and links consumption to procurement contracts for OPEX tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order; Pronto Xi Procurement; IFRS IAS 2

description: Total mass of reagent consumed during the shift in kilograms. Primary quantity field for OPEX cost calculation, reagent inventory reconciliation, and environmental compliance reporting of chemical usage volumes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Goods Issue; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Unit of measure for the quantity consumed field. Defaults to kilograms (kg) for solid reagents; litres (L) for liquid reagents. Enables multi-reagent reporting with consistent unit handling.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; Internal Inventory Standard

description: Percentage of the valuable mineral recovered from the feed ore during the shift in this circuit. Key process performance indicator correlated with reagent dosage to assess reagent effectiveness and optimise consumption.
reference: ICMM Mineral Processing Best Practice; Internal Processing KPI Standard; OSIsoft PI System

description: Calendar date of the shift during which the reagent was consumed. Used for daily and monthly OPEX roll-up reporting and LOM plan variance analysis.
Classification of the operational shift (day, night, or afternoon) during which the reagent was consumed. Supports shift-level performance analysis and reagent usage pattern identification.

Density of the slurry in grams per cubic centimetre at the time of reagent consumption. Influences reagent dispersion and effectiveness; monitored via OSIsoft PI System for process control and optimisation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System SCADA; Internal Process Control Standard

description: pH level of the slurry (water and ore mixture) in the circuit at the time of reagent consumption. Critical process parameter for reagent effectiveness — lime and acid dosage directly controls pH for flotation and leaching circuits.
reference: OSIsoft PI System SCADA; Internal Process Control Standard; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Reagent consumption normalised per dry metric tonne of ore processed (grams per tonne). Calculated as quantity_consumed_kg × 1000 / ore_throughput_t. Key OPEX optimisation metric and LOM plan variance indicator.
reference: ICMM Mineral Processing Best Practice; Internal OPEX Reporting Standard

description: Name of the supplier who provided the reagent batch consumed. Used for supplier performance management, procurement analysis, and reagent quality investigations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master; Pronto Xi Procurement

description: Total Operating Expenditure (OPEX) cost of reagent consumed during the shift, calculated as quantity_consumed_kg multiplied by unit_cost. Primary financial metric for reagent OPEX tracking, budget variance, and All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Centre Accounting; IFRS IAS 2; World Gold Council AISC Standard

description: Cost per kilogram (or applicable unit) of the reagent at the time of consumption, sourced from the SAP purchase order or standard cost. Used for shift-level OPEX calculation and reagent cost variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Standard Cost; SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: Timestamp when this reagent consumption record was last modified (e.g., post-shift adjustment, supervisor confirmation). Supports change tracking and data quality monitoring in the Databricks Silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for each SX-EW operational event record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for this transactional entity. Role: TRANSACTION_HEADER.

Reference to the specific SX-EW processing circuit (tankhouse or extraction circuit) where this event occurred. Links to the processing circuit master resource.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Asset Framework; Infor EAM Asset Registry

description: Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: SX-EW produces specific commodities (copper cathode). Direct production link essential for cathode quality tracking, production reporting, and linking hydrometallurgical output to saleable products. S

Foreign key linking to processing.feed_stream. Business justification: SX-EW events process specific feed streams (PLS feed). One event processes one feed stream; one feed stream has many events. This links the SX-EW event to the feed stream being processed.

Foreign key linking to product.grade_parameter. Business justification: SX-EW events record cathode_grade_pct and aqueous_feed_grade_gl for specific elements. Linking to grade_parameter ensures correct parameter definition for cathode quality reporting, LME grade complian

Foreign key linking to hse.incident. Business justification: SXEW involves high-voltage electrical systems (electrowinning cells) and corrosive solutions (sulfuric acid electrolyte). Incidents during SXEW operations (electrical faults, acid spills, cathode hand

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Electrolyte and cathode samples from SX-EW circuits sent to designated labs for copper grade, impurity analysis, and cathode quality verification. Essential for product quality control and sales contr

Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: SX-EW circuit equipment issues (rectifier failures, cell short circuits, organic pumps) during production events trigger maintenance work orders. Links cathode production problems to maintenance inter
Reference to the mineral processing plant or hydrometallurgical facility where the SX-EW circuit operates. Enables multi-site reporting and plant-level performance aggregation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM – Functional Location; Infor EAM

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: SX-EW events track cathode production against scheduled mine production for integrated copper production planning, metal accounting, and sales contract fulfillment. Essential for copper operations wit
Reference to the operational shift during which this SX-EW event was recorded. Each SX-EW event is captured per shift as the primary reporting cadence.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP – Shift Definition; OSIsoft PI System

description: Foreign key linking to processing.unit_operation_event. Business justification: sxew_event is a specialized subtype of unit_operation_event for Solvent Extraction Electrowinning operations. Linking sxew_event to its parent unit_operation_event enables unified hydrometallurgical p

Sulphuric acid concentration in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Acid balance is critical for cathode quality, current efficiency, and prevention of anode corrosion in the EW tankhouse.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS; P&ID Reference

description: Copper concentration in the aqueous feed (pregnant leach solution, PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Determines the extraction circuit loading and directly influences cathode production output.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS; ICMM Operational Guidance

description: Purity of the copper cathode produced, expressed as a percentage of copper content. LME Grade A copper cathode requires ≥99.99% Cu. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and LME registration eligibility.
reference: London Metal Exchange (LME) Grade A Copper Standard; ASTM B115; LabWare LIMS

description: Total mass of copper cathode produced during the shift, measured in kilograms. The primary production output metric for the SX-EW circuit, used for daily production reporting, LOM plan reconciliation, and sales volume tracking.
reference: JORC Code; SEC S-K 1300; SAP S/4HANA CO – Production Orders; ICMM

description: Quality classification of copper cathode produced during the shift. 'LME_Grade_A' indicates product meets London Metal Exchange Grade A specification (≥99.99% Cu); 'off_spec' indicates product below LME standard but saleable; 'rejected' indicates product not suitable for sale.
reference: London Metal Exchange (LME) Grade A Copper Standard; ASTM B115; LabWare LIMS

description: Number of individual copper cathode sheets harvested (stripped) from the electrowinning cells during the shift. Used for tankhouse productivity tracking, cathode cycle time management, and inventory reconciliation.
reference: Internal Tankhouse Operations Standard; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Average operating voltage across the electrowinning cells during the shift, measured in volts (V). Used to monitor energy efficiency, detect anode/cathode fouling, and calculate specific energy consumption per tonne of copper produced.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – SCADA Integration; IEC 60038

description: Number of electrowinning cells in active operation during the shift. Used to calculate effective circuit capacity, monitor cell availability, and assess impact of cell outages on production.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Asset Framework; Infor EAM – Asset Availability

description: Total number of electrowinning cells installed in the circuit (nameplate capacity). Used as the denominator for cell availability calculations and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) reporting.
reference: Infor EAM – Asset Registry; Internal Plant Design Specification

description: Total unplanned and planned downtime for the SX-EW circuit during the shift, measured in minutes. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) calculation, maintenance planning via Infor EAM, and production loss reporting.
reference: Infor EAM – Work Order Management; SAP S/4HANA PM; ICMM OEE Framework

description: Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and silver layer ingestion tracking. Populated automatically at record insertion.

Electrical current density applied across the electrowinning cathode surface, measured in amperes per square metre (A/m²). A primary electrowinning process control parameter governing copper deposition rate, cathode quality, and energy consumption.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – SCADA Integration; IEC 60038; ICMM Operational Guidance

description: Ratio of actual copper deposited to the theoretical copper that should have been deposited based on Faraday's Law, expressed as a percentage. A key electrowinning performance indicator; values below target indicate short-circuiting, impurity effects, or electrolyte imbalance.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; ICMM Operational Guidance; Faraday's Law of Electrolysis

description: Primary reason code for circuit downtime recorded during the shift. Used for root cause analysis, maintenance prioritisation, and production loss categorisation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned_maintenance|unplanned_breakdown|process_upset|reagent_shortage|power_outage|other — promote to reference product if additional codes required]
reference: Infor EAM – Failure Codes; SAP S/4HANA PM – Notification Types; ICMM

description: Operating temperature of the electrowinning electrolyte during the shift, measured in degrees Celsius (°C). Temperature affects copper ion mobility, current efficiency, and cathode surface quality. Monitored via OSIsoft PI System.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; SCADA Integration

description: Human-readable business identifier for the SX-EW shift event, formatted as SXEW-{YYYY}-{NNNNNN}. Used for cross-referencing with OSIsoft PI System historian records, lab assay reports, and production reconciliation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Event Frame; Internal Naming Convention

description: Lifecycle status of the SX-EW event record. 'draft' indicates data entry in progress; 'confirmed' indicates shift data locked; 'reviewed' indicates metallurgist review complete; 'approved' indicates management sign-off; 'voided' indicates record invalidated.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; JORC Code

description: Percentage of copper extracted from the aqueous feed (PLS) into the organic phase during the solvent extraction stage, calculated as (PLS grade – raffinate grade) / PLS grade × 100. A key metallurgical recovery indicator for the SX circuit.
reference: ICMM Operational Guidance; LabWare LIMS; Internal Metallurgical KPI Standard

description: Concentration of iron (Fe) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Elevated iron levels reduce current efficiency and degrade cathode surface quality. Monitored via LabWare LIMS shift samples.
reference: LabWare LIMS – Assay Results; LME Grade A Copper Standard; ASTM B115

description: Concentration of manganese (Mn) impurity in the electrowinning electrolyte, measured in milligrams per litre (mg/L). Manganese causes anode passivation and cathode surface defects. Tracked via LabWare LIMS for electrolyte bleed management.
reference: LabWare LIMS – Assay Results; LME Grade A Copper Standard

business_glossary_term: Life of Mine (LOM) Plan Cathode Target (kg/shift)
description: Planned cathode copper production target for the shift as defined in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan or short-term production schedule. Used for variance analysis between actual and planned production, and for LOM plan tracking.
reference: Deswik – Mine Scheduling; Hexagon MinePlan – Production Schedule; JORC Code

description: Concentration of copper in the organic (solvent) phase of the solvent extraction circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). A key process control parameter indicating the loading efficiency of the organic extractant (e.g., LIX reagent). Sourced from OSIsoft PI System online analysers or LabWare LIMS shift samples.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS; ICMM Operational Guidance

description: Volumetric flow rate of the organic solvent phase circulating through the solvent extraction mixer-settlers, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Used to control the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and optimise copper extraction efficiency.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Flow Historian; P&ID Reference

description: Volumetric flow rate of the pregnant leach solution (PLS) entering the solvent extraction circuit, measured in cubic metres per hour (m³/h). Controls the aqueous-to-organic (A:O) ratio and extraction circuit residence time.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Flow Historian; P&ID Reference

description: Calendar date on which the SX-EW production event occurred. Used for daily production reporting, reconciliation against Life of Mine (LOM) plan targets, and regulatory disclosure.

Copper concentration in the raffinate (barren aqueous solution) exiting the solvent extraction circuit after copper has been extracted, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Used to calculate extraction efficiency and monitor copper losses to raffinate.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS

description: Volume of organic extractant reagent (e.g., LIX 984N or equivalent) consumed per cubic metre of pregnant leach solution processed, measured in litres per cubic metre (L/m³). Used for reagent cost tracking, OPEX reporting, and supply chain planning via Pronto Xi.
reference: Pronto Xi – Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM – Material Consumption; ICMM

description: The actual date and time the operational shift ended for this SX-EW event. Used to calculate shift duration and align with production reporting periods.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Event Frame End Time; ISO 8601

description: The actual date and time the operational shift commenced for this SX-EW event. Principal business event timestamp used for time-series analysis, production scheduling alignment, and OSIsoft PI historian correlation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Event Frame Start Time; ISO 8601

description: Classification of the shift period: day, afternoon, or night. Used for shift-pattern analysis, fatigue risk management, and production performance benchmarking across shift rotations.
reference: ISO 45001 – Occupational Health and Safety; Internal Shift Schedule

description: Originating operational system from which this SX-EW event record was sourced. Supports data lineage, reconciliation between systems, and data quality root cause analysis in the lakehouse silver layer.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; Databricks Lakehouse Architecture

description: Electrical energy consumed per tonne of copper cathode produced, measured in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t Cu). A key OPEX and sustainability metric for the electrowinning circuit, used for energy cost allocation and carbon footprint reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Energy Historian; SAP S/4HANA CO – Cost Centre; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; ISO 14001

description: Copper concentration in the spent electrolyte returning from the electrowinning cells to the stripping circuit, measured in grams per litre (g/L). The difference between strip solution grade and spent electrolyte grade represents the copper deposited per pass.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS

description: Copper concentration in the electrolyte strip solution (advance electrolyte) fed to the electrowinning tankhouse, measured in grams per litre (g/L). Directly controls cathode deposition rate and current efficiency in the EW circuit.
reference: OSIsoft PI System – Process Historian; LabWare LIMS; ICMM Operational Guidance

description: Timestamp when this SX-EW event record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), audit trail, and data quality monitoring in the Databricks silver layer.
Unique surrogate identifier for a processing circuit record within the mineral processing plant. Primary key for the processing_circuit data product.

Foreign key linking to product.commodity. Business justification: Circuits are engineered for specific commodities with tailored flowsheets and reagent schemes. Critical for metallurgical accounting, recovery optimization, and circuit performance reporting. Replaces

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Individual processing circuits (rougher, cleaner, leach) are discrete cost objects in mining operations. Circuit-level cost centre assignment enables granular AISC/C1 cost reporting and variance analy

Foreign key linking to hse.emergency_response_plan. Business justification: Specific circuits (cyanide leach, acid circuits) require dedicated emergency response plans under ICMC and national chemical safety regulations, distinct from the plant-level plan. Mining HSE managers

Foreign key linking to maintenance.functional_location. Business justification: Circuits are maintainable functional locations within plant hierarchy. Maintenance strategies, PM schedules, and work orders are assigned to circuit-level functional locations for granular maintenance

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Processing circuits are designed and configured for specific geological domains — oxide circuits differ fundamentally from sulphide circuits in reagent_scheme_code, design_ph_target, and target_recove

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Each processing circuit is assigned a primary laboratory for routine process control assays (head grade, concentrate grade, tailings grade). Metallurgical accounting and circuit performance reporting
Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan under which this circuit's design parameters and recovery targets were established. Enables traceability of circuit design basis to the governing mine plan.
reference: JORC Code; CIM Standards; NI 43-101; Deswik

description: Reference to the mineral processing plant in which this circuit operates. Links circuit-level data to plant-level operational context.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA PM

description: Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Each processing circuit is configured to produce a specific saleable product. Circuit performance reporting, reagent schemes, and design parameters are set based on the saleable product requirements.

Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Each processing circuit operates under a defined sample program specifying sampling points, frequency, and analytical suite for metallurgical accounting and process control. This is a fundamental oper

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each circuit is designed to produce output meeting a specific product specification. Circuit design parameters (target_product_grade_pct, target_recovery_rate_pct) are derived from the specification.

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Processing circuit design specifies which TSF receives its tailings output — a fundamental environmental permit and water balance requirement. Metallurgical engineers and environmental teams must know
Self-referencing FK on circuit (upstream_circuit_id)

Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the processing circuit within the plant flowsheet (e.g., PC-SAG-01, FL-RGH-02). Used in P&ID documentation, SCADA tags, and operational reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; P&ID Standards; ISO 14001

description: Free-text description of the processing circuit including its role in the plant flowsheet, key equipment, and any notable design features or operational constraints. Supports engineering documentation and onboarding.
Human-readable descriptive name of the processing circuit (e.g., Primary SAG Mill Circuit, Rougher Flotation Circuit, Leach Circuit 1). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.
Current operational lifecycle status of the processing circuit. Drives scheduling, maintenance planning, and production reporting inclusion/exclusion.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP S/4HANA PM; ISO 14001

description: Category of mineral processing technology employed in this circuit. Drives applicable KPIs, reagent profiles, and recovery benchmarks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: comminution|flotation|leaching|gravity_separation|dense_media_separation|sx_ew|thickening|filtration|classification — promote to reference product]
reference: ICMM Guidance; SME Mineral Processing Handbook

description: Date on which the processing circuit was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset age calculations, warranty tracking, and depreciation under IFRS.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; Infor EAM; IFRS 16

description: Date on which the processing circuit was or is planned to be permanently decommissioned. Nullable for active circuits. Used for mine closure planning, asset write-off scheduling, and rehabilitation cost provisioning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS 6; ISO 14001; Mine Closure Plans

description: Design basis mechanical availability of the circuit expressed as a percentage of scheduled operating hours. Used as the denominator in OEE calculations and production planning. Derived from MTBF and MTTR estimates at design stage.
reference: ICMM Guidance; Infor EAM; OEE Standards

description: Design basis P80 particle size of the feed material entering this circuit in millimetres. P80 represents the 80th percentile passing size. Critical for comminution circuit design and mill sizing.
reference: SME Mineral Processing Handbook; ICMM Guidance

description: Design basis target pH value for the circuit process stream. Applicable to flotation (pH control for selectivity), leaching (acid/alkaline conditions), and SX-EW circuits. Drives lime/acid reagent consumption.
reference: SME Mineral Processing Handbook; OSIsoft PI System

description: Design basis P80 particle size of the product leaving this circuit in millimetres. Defines the target grind size for comminution circuits and classification efficiency for downstream separation circuits.
reference: SME Mineral Processing Handbook; ICMM Guidance

description: Design basis residence time of material within the circuit expressed in minutes. Determines tank/vessel sizing and is a key parameter for leach kinetics, flotation recovery, and thickener design.
reference: SME Mineral Processing Handbook; ICMM Guidance

description: Design basis slurry density expressed as percentage solids by weight for circuits handling slurry streams. Critical for pump sizing, pipe design, and process control setpoints.
reference: SME Mineral Processing Handbook; OSIsoft PI System

description: Annualised nameplate design throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Used for LOM plan alignment, capital project justification, and annual production reporting.
reference: JORC Code; SEC S-K 1300; LOM Plan Standards

description: Nameplate design throughput capacity of the circuit expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the engineering design basis used for LOM plan benchmarking and OEE calculations.

Design basis utilisation rate of the circuit expressed as a percentage of available hours actually used for production. Combined with availability to derive OEE. Accounts for planned feed interruptions and scheduled downtime.

business_glossary_term: Design Water Consumption (Cubic Metres Per Tonne)

description: Date from which the current circuit configuration, design parameters, and targets are effective. Supports slowly changing dimension (SCD) management and historical performance benchmarking against the correct design basis.

description: Date until which the current circuit configuration and design parameters are effective. Null for the current active version. Enables historical tracking of circuit design changes and target revisions over the LOM.

description: Reference number of the environmental operating permit or licence governing this circuit's operations (e.g., water use licence, air emission permit). Required for regulatory compliance reporting under ISO 14001 and EIS obligations.
reference: ISO 14001; EIS Requirements; GRI Mining and Metals Supplement; IsoMetrix

description: Classification of the primary feed material entering this circuit (e.g., ROM Ore for primary comminution, slurry for flotation). description: Indicates whether this circuit is the primary (first-pass) processing circuit in the plant flowsheet as opposed to a secondary, scavenger, or retreatment circuit. Used for production reporting hierarchy and mass balance prioritisation.

description: Count of parallel processing units (e.g., mills, flotation cells, leach tanks) operating within this circuit. Used for availability calculations, maintenance scheduling, and throughput modelling.

description: Code identifying the standard reagent addition scheme applied in this circuit (e.g., collector, frother, pH modifier for flotation; acid type for leaching). Links to reagent consumption planning and OPEX budgeting.

description: Name or employee identifier of the process engineer accountable for the design, optimisation, and performance of this circuit. Used for accountability tracking and escalation routing.

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each ore feed stream record entering the processing plant. Primary key for the feed_stream data product.

description: Reference identifier for the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill circuit, DMS circuit, flotation circuit) that this feed stream feeds into. Enables circuit-level mass balance and throughput analysis.

Reference identifier for the LOM (Life of Mine) plan version that defines the target feed parameters for this stream. Enables variance analysis between planned and actual feed characteristics for production reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to exploration.prospect. Business justification: Feed streams originate from specific prospects/orebodies. Critical for mine-to-mill optimization, ore tracking, metallurgical reconciliation, and LOM planning. Real business need: tracking ore source
Reference identifier for the specific physical source location (ROM pad, stockpile, crusher station) from which this feed stream originates. Links to the plant location or stockpile master.

Foreign key linking to laboratory.sample_program. Business justification: Feed streams are sampled under a defined sample program governing sampling protocol, frequency, and analytical suite for metallurgical accounting. Knowing which sample program applies to each feed str

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Feed streams originate from ore extracted from specific tenements. Essential for royalty calculations, grade reconciliation against tenement reserves, and compliance with tenement-specific production

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Feed streams are configured to meet a target product specification (grade, particle size, moisture). Process engineers reference the specification when setting feed stream design parameters. Metallurg

Bond Abrasion Index (Ai) of the ore in the feed stream, a dimensionless measure of ore abrasiveness. Drives liner and media wear rate predictions for SAG mills, ball mills, and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls), informing maintenance scheduling and OPEX forecasting.
reference: LabWare LIMS; Infor EAM; JORC Code 2012

description: Bond Ball Mill Work Index (BWi) of the ore expressed in kilowatt-hours per tonne (kWh/t). Measures ore hardness and grindability; used to predict mill power requirements, throughput, and energy consumption in comminution circuit design and optimisation.

description: Bulk density of the ore material in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre (t/m³). Used to convert volumetric flow measurements to mass flow for mass balance calculations and stockpile volume reconciliation.

description: The date on which this feed stream was formally commissioned and accepted into operational service. Used for asset lifecycle tracking, warranty management, and CAPEX (Capital Expenditure) project closure reporting.

description: The timestamp when this feed stream record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for data lineage and record provenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 14001; IFRS 6; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: The minimum economic grade threshold (cut-off grade) applied to material entering this feed stream. Material below cut-off grade is classified as waste and diverted to the waste dump. Defined per LOM plan and commodity economics.

Maximum rated throughput capacity of the feed stream infrastructure (conveyor, feeder, chute) in dry metric tonnes per hour. Used for bottleneck analysis and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculations.

Percentage of waste material mixed with ore in the feed stream due to mining dilution. Dilution reduces the effective head grade and is tracked for reconciliation between geological resource model grades and actual plant feed grades.
reference: JORC Code 2012; Hexagon MinePlan; Geovia GEMS

description: The date from which this feed stream definition and its associated parameters (nominal rate, target grade, processing route) became operationally effective. Supports temporal versioning of feed stream configurations aligned to LOM plan revisions.
