description: The date on which this feed stream definition ceases to be operationally effective. Null indicates the stream is currently active with no planned end date. Supports LOM plan stage transitions and plant reconfiguration tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA; Hexagon MinePlan; JORC Code 2012

description: Temperature of the ore or slurry in the feed stream measured in degrees Celsius. Relevant for leaching circuits (SX-EW), autoclave operations, and cold-climate operations where freezing affects conveyor and crusher performance.

Percentage of waste rock (gangue) minerals in the feed stream material. High gangue content increases dilution, reduces effective head grade, and impacts reagent consumption in flotation and leaching circuits.

The assay-determined grade of the primary valuable mineral in the feed stream expressed as a percentage (e.g., % Fe for iron ore, % Cu for copper). Head grade is the key input for recovery rate calculations and production reconciliation against the LOM plan.
reference: JORC Code 2012; LabWare LIMS; Geovia GEMS

description: Moisture content of the ore in the feed stream expressed as a percentage of total wet mass. Moisture affects conveyor performance, crusher throughput, mill power draw, and dry tonne calculations for mass balance and invoicing.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ISO 3087 (Coal Moisture)

description: The design or nameplate feed rate for this stream expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (tph). Represents the target throughput capacity used in LOM (Life of Mine) plan scheduling and plant design. Distinct from actual measured feed rate.

Geological ore type classification of the material in this feed stream (e.g., oxide, sulphide, transitional, fresh, weathered). Ore type drives processing route selection, reagent dosing, and expected recovery rates. reference: JORC Code 2012; Geovia GEMS; Hexagon MinePlan

The 80th percentile passing particle size of the feed stream material expressed in millimetres (mm). P80 is the industry-standard metric for characterising particle size distribution (PSD) in comminution circuits. Critical input for SAG mill and HPGR (High Pressure Grinding Rolls) performance modelling.
reference: JORC Code 2012; OSIsoft PI System; LabWare LIMS

The designated mineral processing route for material in this feed stream (e.g., SAG/Ball Mill comminution, DMS (Dense Media Separation), flotation, leaching, SX-EW (Solvent Extraction Electrowinning)). Determines downstream circuit routing and reagent requirements. description: Density of the ore-water slurry mixture in the feed stream expressed in tonnes per cubic metre. Applicable to wet processing circuits (SAG mill discharge, flotation feed). Monitored via OSIsoft PI System for real-time process control.

Percentage of solids by mass in the slurry feed stream (percent solids). A key process control parameter for wet grinding and flotation circuits; directly affects mill power draw, pump performance, and flotation cell residence time.

Classification of the physical origin point from which ore material enters this feed stream. ROM (Run of Mine) pad indicates direct truck tip; stockpile indicates reclaimed material; crusher indicates post-comminution feed. Descriptive human-readable name of the feed stream (e.g., 'Primary ROM Feed', 'SAG Mill Discharge', 'Crusher Product Recirculating Load'). Used in operational dashboards and reporting.

Current lifecycle status of the feed stream definition within the processing plant. Active streams are currently receiving material; suspended streams are temporarily offline; decommissioned streams are permanently retired from the circuit.

Classification of the feed stream by its position and role in the comminution circuit. The planned or scheduled feed rate for this stream in dry metric tonnes per hour as defined in the current production schedule or LOM plan. Distinct from nominal (design) feed rate; used for shift-level production planning and variance reporting.

The planned P80 particle size target for this feed stream in millimetres as specified in the processing plant design or LOM plan. Used to assess crusher and mill performance against design specifications.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; OSIsoft PI System

description: Maximum lump size of material in the feed stream expressed in millimetres. Defines the upper bound of the particle size distribution and is critical for crusher gap settings, conveyor design, and mill liner protection.
The timestamp when this feed stream record was most recently modified. Supports incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail requirements in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: ISO 14001; IFRS 6; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Nameplate annual throughput capacity expressed in million dry metric tonnes per annum (Mtpa). Derived from design_capacity_tph and planned operating hours but stored as a master data attribute for LOM plan alignment and regulatory resource reporting.

reference: JORC Code 2012; ICMM Technical Guidelines; Infor EAM

description: Elevation of the processing plant site in metres above sea level (mASL). Relevant for water balance modelling, slurry pipeline hydraulics design, and environmental impact assessments.

Design ROM feed head grade (percentage of valuable mineral in the feed ore) for which the plant was engineered. Used as the baseline for metallurgical balance calculations, cut-off grade assessments, and LOM plan modelling.

description: Geographic latitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS (Geographic Information System) mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.

business_glossary_term: Laboratory Information Management System (LIMS) Plant Code
description: The plant identifier used in the LabWare LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) for sample registration, assay result linkage, and metallurgical accounting. Enables traceability from plant feed to product assay.
Geographic longitude of the processing plant centroid in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used for GIS mapping, spatial analysis, and environmental impact zone delineation.

Design-rated metallurgical recovery rate expressed as a percentage (0–100). Represents the percentage of the target valuable mineral in the feed that is expected to report to the concentrate or product stream under nameplate conditions. Key input to LOM plan financial modelling and JORC/NI 43-101 resource estimation.
reference: JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; SAMREC; CIM Standards

description: Total count of parallel processing lines (circuits) within the plant. Each line represents an independent processing train capable of treating ore from feed to product. Drives capacity planning, maintenance scheduling, and redundancy analysis.

Current lifecycle state of the processing plant. 'active' = fully operational; 'care-and-maintenance' = temporarily idle but preserved; 'decommissioned' = permanently closed; 'commissioning' = in start-up phase; 'suspended' = temporarily halted due to regulatory or operational reasons.
reference: ICMM Mine Closure Framework; ISO 14001; Infor EAM

description: Comma-separated list of ore/commodity types this plant is designed and certified to process (e.g., 'iron ore, copper, gold, lithium, nickel, coal, diamonds'). Drives feed stream routing and LOM plan alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron ore|copper|gold|lithium|nickel|coal|diamonds|zinc|lead|manganese — promote to reference product]

Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the processing plant, used across ERP, SCADA, and FMS systems (e.g., 'PP-PILBARA-01', 'PROC-CU-02'). Unique across the enterprise and referenced in operational reporting.

Free-text narrative description of the processing plant including key design features, processing circuit configuration, historical context, and any notable operational characteristics. Supports regulatory submissions, investor reports, and technical documentation.

description: Full human-readable name of the mineral processing plant facility (e.g., 'Pilbara Iron Ore Processing Plant 1', 'Olympic Dam Copper Concentrator'). Used in reports, dashboards, and regulatory submissions.

High-level classification of the processing plant by metallurgical process family. 'concentrator' = physical separation to produce mineral concentrate; 'hydrometallurgical' = chemical leaching/SX-EW; 'pyrometallurgical' = smelting/roasting; 'beneficiation' = upgrading without full extraction; 'refinery' = final product refining; 'pelletising' = iron ore pellet production.

Nameplate design electrical power consumption in kilowatts (kW) at full throughput. Used for energy cost modelling (OPEX), carbon footprint reporting (GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement), and grid capacity planning.

description: The principal mineral processing and beneficiation method employed by this plant. The timestamp when this processing plant master record was first created in the data platform (Silver Layer). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and regulatory record-keeping obligations.
The timestamp when this processing plant master record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, data quality monitoring, and audit compliance.

The SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) functional location code assigned to this processing plant. Enables linkage to maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and CAPEX/OPEX cost centre reporting in SAP.

The identifier of the SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) system instance or node that monitors and controls this processing plant. Used to link real-time process historian data (OSIsoft PI System) to the plant master record.

description: State, province, or territory in which the processing plant is located (e.g., 'Western Australia', 'British Columbia', 'Antofagasta'). Used for jurisdictional regulatory compliance, environmental permitting, and geographic reporting.

business_glossary_term: Design Water Consumption (Cubic Metres Per Hour)
description: Nameplate design water consumption rate in cubic metres per hour (m³/h) for the plant under full throughput conditions. Critical for water balance management, environmental licence compliance, and EIS (Environmental Impact Statement) reporting.
reference: ISO 14001; EIS Regulatory Requirements; ICMM Water Stewardship

description: Primary key for plant_utility_consumption
Identifier of the specific processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) for which utility consumption is recorded. Enables circuit-level OPEX benchmarking.
reference: OSIsoft PI System Asset Framework; Internal Circuit Register

description: Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Electricity, water, and gas consumption are major opex line items in processing. Cost centre assignment on each utility consumption record is required for GL posting, AISC energy cost component report

Foreign key linking to hse.environmental_permit. Business justification: Water and energy consumption records must be reconciled against environmental permit licence limits for regulatory compliance reporting. The denormalized water_licence_limit_m3 and water_licence_compl

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: Utility costs (power, water, gas) are posted to specific GL accounts for financial reporting. Direct GL account reference on utility consumption records enables automated journal posting, cost element
Reference to the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which utility consumption targets and budgets are benchmarked. Supports variance analysis against LOM plan targets.
reference: Deswik LOM Planning; Internal LOM Plan Register

description: Foreign key linking to processing.metallurgical_balance. Business justification: Plant utility consumption (electricity, water, compressed air) is recorded per circuit per shift and feeds into the metallurgical balance for energy and water intensity calculations. Linking plant_uti
Identifier of the mineral processing plant where utility consumption was recorded. Links to the plant master record.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Functional Location; Internal Plant Master

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Utility consumption must be tracked against production schedules for cost allocation, energy intensity benchmarking, and carbon accounting. Essential for AISC calculation and Scope 1/2 emissions repor

Foreign key linking to tenement.regulatory_condition. Business justification: Water licence limits and environmental conditions on tenements directly govern plant utility consumption. plant_utility_consumption already tracks water_licence_compliance_flag and water_licence_limit

Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Utility consumption is recorded per shift run. One consumption record belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many consumption records. This links the utility consumption to the processing domain'
Identifier of the production shift during which this utility consumption was recorded. Links to the shift production run record for operational context.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP-PI; Internal Shift Schedule

description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Plant utility consumption (electricity, water, compressed air) during planned shutdowns must be attributed to the shutdown plan for AISC cost reporting and energy benchmarking. Mining finance and oper

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Plant utility consumption records track water drawn from and returned to the TSF. Water licence compliance requires reconciling plant water use (fresh_water_intake_m3, recycled_water_m3) against the T

Foreign key linking to tailings.water_balance. Business justification: Plant water consumption records must be reconciled against the TSF water balance for water licence compliance reporting. A direct FK enables automated reconciliation of plant-side water use (process_w
Date and time when the utility consumption record was approved by the authorised employee. Marks the record as locked for OPEX cost posting and environmental compliance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; Internal Data Governance Standard; ISO 14001

description: Approved carbon budget allocation for this circuit and period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), derived from the LOM plan carbon budget. Used to calculate emissions variance and compliance status.
reference: ICMM Climate Change Position Statement; NGER Act; Internal LOM Plan Carbon Budget

description: Volume of compressed air consumed by the circuit during the period in standard cubic metres (Sm³). Used for flotation cell aeration, instrumentation, and pneumatic equipment. Contributes to energy OPEX.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Internal OPEX Reporting Standard

description: Externally-known business reference number for this utility consumption record, used for cross-referencing with SAP S/4HANA CO cost postings and OPEX reporting. Format: UC-YYYY-MM-NNNNNN.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Module; Internal OPEX Reporting Standard

description: Date and time when this utility consumption record was first created in the system, sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian ingestion or manual entry. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; ISO 14001 Audit Trail

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary cost fields in this record (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Ensures consistent currency context for OPEX reporting and financial consolidation.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS; SAP S/4HANA FI Currency Configuration

description: Origin system or method by which utility consumption data was captured. PI System and SCADA sources are automated and high-confidence; manual_entry and estimated records require additional validation before use in regulatory reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SCADA Integration Standard; Internal Data Quality Framework

description: Total electrical energy consumed by the circuit during the period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), sourced from OSIsoft PI System metering data. Primary input for energy OPEX calculation and carbon footprint reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; GRI 302-1 Energy Consumption; ISO 14001

description: Total monetary cost of electricity consumed during the period in the reporting currency, calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh multiplied by electricity_unit_cost plus applicable demand charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS; Internal OPEX Reporting Standard

description: Contracted or metered unit cost of electricity per kilowatt-hour ($/kWh) applicable to this period. Confidential commercial rate used for OPEX cost allocation and energy cost benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Element; Utility Supply Contract; IFRS

description: Planned electrical energy consumption target for this circuit and period in kilowatt-hours (kWh), derived from the LOM plan energy budget. Used to calculate energy consumption variance for OPEX performance reporting.
reference: Internal LOM Plan; AISC Reporting Standard; ICMM Energy and GHG Protocol

description: Estimated volume of water lost to evaporation from open process vessels, ponds, and the TSF during the period in cubic metres (m³). Required for water balance closure and environmental reporting.
reference: GRI 303-5 Water Consumption; ISO 14001; Water Licence Conditions

description: Volume of fresh water drawn from licensed water sources (rivers, bores, municipal supply) into the processing plant during the period in cubic metres (m³). Reported against water licence limits for regulatory compliance.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management; GRI 303-3 Water Withdrawal; Water Licence Conditions

description: Reference identifier for the physical or virtual meter reading(s) underpinning this consumption record, as recorded in OSIsoft PI System or the utility provider invoice. Supports audit trail and data lineage for regulatory submissions.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Utility Invoice; Internal Metering Standard

description: Volume of natural gas consumed by the circuit during the period in gigajoules (GJ), used for drying, heating, or on-site power generation. Input for Scope 1 greenhouse gas emissions calculation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; GRI 302-1; NGER Act (Australia); ISO 14064

description: Free-text field for recording operational context, anomalies, meter faults, estimation methodology, or adjustment reasons relevant to this utility consumption record. Supports audit review and regulatory submissions.
Maximum instantaneous electrical power demand recorded during the period in kilowatts (kW). Used for utility tariff management, demand charge optimisation, and grid capacity planning.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Utility Tariff Agreement

description: The real-world end date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. Used with period_start_timestamp to calculate duration and validate completeness of the consumption record.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA CO Accounting Period

description: The real-world start date and time of the utility consumption measurement period. For shift records this aligns with shift start; for monthly records this is the first instant of the accounting month.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SAP S/4HANA CO Accounting Period

description: Granularity of the consumption accounting period. Shift-level records are sourced from OSIsoft PI System historian; monthly records align with SAP S/4HANA CO accounting periods for OPEX reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Controlling; OSIsoft PI System

description: Average power factor (ratio of real power to apparent power, dimensionless 0–1) for the circuit during the period. Low power factor incurs utility penalty charges and indicates reactive power inefficiency.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Utility Tariff Agreement; IEC 60038

description: Total process water consumed within the circuit during the period (fresh intake plus recycled, minus discharge) in cubic metres (m³). Net water balance input for environmental reporting and water licence compliance.
reference: GRI 303-5 Water Consumption; ISO 14001; Water Licence Conditions

description: Current workflow lifecycle status of the utility consumption record. Draft records are pending submission; approved records are locked for OPEX and environmental reporting.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Volume of process water recycled from the Tailings Storage Facility (TSF) and other internal sources back into the processing circuit during the period in cubic metres (m³). Supports water stewardship reporting and licence compliance.
reference: GRI 303-3 Water Withdrawal; ISO 14001; Water Licence Conditions; ICMM Water Stewardship Protocol

description: Direct greenhouse gas emissions from on-site combustion of natural gas or other fuels during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e). Supports carbon budget compliance and NGER/GRI reporting.
reference: GRI 305-1 Direct GHG Emissions; NGER Act (Australia); ISO 14064; ICMM Climate Change Position Statement

description: Indirect greenhouse gas emissions from purchased electricity consumption during the period in tonnes of CO₂ equivalent (tCO₂e), calculated using applicable grid emission factors. Supports carbon budget compliance and GRI/NGER reporting.
reference: GRI 305-2 Indirect GHG Emissions; NGER Act (Australia); ISO 14064; ICMM Climate Change Position Statement

description: Electrical energy consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (kWh/t), calculated as electricity_consumption_kwh divided by tonnes_processed. Key comminution efficiency KPI benchmarked against LOM plan and AISC targets.
reference: ICMM Energy and GHG Protocol; Internal LOM Plan; AISC Reporting Standard

description: Process water consumed per dry metric tonne of ore processed (m³/t). Key water efficiency metric benchmarked against LOM plan targets and water licence conditions.
reference: GRI 303-5; ICMM Water Stewardship Protocol; Internal LOM Plan

description: Dry metric tonnes of ore processed through the circuit during the period. Used as the denominator for calculating specific consumption rates (e.g., kWh/t, m³/t) for benchmarking and LOM plan variance.
reference: JORC Code 2012; Internal Metallurgical Accounting Standard

description: Date and time when this utility consumption record was last modified. Tracks corrections, adjustments, and re-submissions for data lineage and audit compliance.
reference: Internal Data Governance Standard; ISO 14001 Audit Trail

description: Total monetary cost of fresh water intake during the period in the reporting currency, including licence fees and supply charges. Posted to SAP S/4HANA CO cost centre for OPEX tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS; Water Licence Conditions

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each shift production run record in the processing plant. Primary key for this transactional entity. Entity role: TRANSACTION_HEADER — represents a discrete operational shift event with a clear lifecycle in the mineral processing domain.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES (Manufacturing Execution System)

description: Foreign key linking to processing.circuit. Business justification: A shift production run is executed on a specific processing circuit within a plant. The circuit_id FK directly links the shift run to its circuit, enabling circuit-level performance aggregation. The e

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Shift-level production costs (labour, consumables, utilities) are allocated to cost centres for unit cost calculation, AISC reporting, and budget variance analysis. Core mining cost accounting process

Foreign key linking to geology.geological_domain. Business justification: Shift reporting and metallurgical reconciliation require tracking which geological domain material was processed each shift. Shift supervisors record feed_blend_oxide_pct and primary_ore_type against

Foreign key linking to laboratory.laboratory. Business justification: Shift samples are dispatched to specific laboratories for analysis. Essential for tracking sample turnaround times, metallurgical accounting, and reconciling lab results with production data for perfo

Identifier of the Life of Mine (LOM) plan version against which this shift production run is being reconciled. Enables tracking of actual production performance versus the approved LOM plan targets for throughput, grade, and recovery.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; SAMREC

description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.work_order. Business justification: Production runs frequently trigger corrective maintenance work orders when equipment issues or process deviations are detected during shift operations. Links shift performance problems to maintenance

Foreign key linking to processing.metallurgical_balance. Business justification: Each shift production run contributes to a metallurgical accounting period. Linking shift_production_run to its metallurgical_balance period enables direct aggregation of shift-level production data i

Foreign key linking to geology.orebody. Business justification: Shift production runs process ore from specific orebodies. Operational reporting, grade reconciliation, and metallurgical accounting require linking production shifts to source orebody to compare actu

Identifier of the mineral processing plant where this shift production run occurred. Links to the plant master reference. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE category (resource reference to the processing facility). Used for plant-level production aggregation and benchmarking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM; Infor EAM; OSIsoft PI System

description: Foreign key linking to mine.production_schedule. Business justification: Processing shift runs must align with mine production schedules for integrated mine-to-mill optimization, bottleneck analysis, and shift-level production reconciliation. Essential for real-time produc

Foreign key linking to mine.rom_stockpile. Business justification: Processing shift runs track feed source from ROM stockpiles for inventory management, feed scheduling, and stockpile depletion tracking. Essential for real-time feed management and stockpile inventory

Foreign key linking to product.saleable_product. Business justification: Shifts produce specific saleable products (concentrate grades, cathodes). Essential for production reconciliation, inventory management, and linking shift output to sales contracts. Natural business r

Foreign key linking to mine.shift_report. Business justification: Daily mine-plant shift reconciliation requires aligning the mine shift report (ore mined, tonnes moved) with the processing shift production run (ore processed, recovery) for the same shift period. Mi

Foreign key linking to maintenance.shutdown_plan. Business justification: Shift production runs that fall within a planned shutdown window must be attributed to the shutdown plan for production-loss accounting and schedule compliance reporting. Mining operations teams expli

Foreign key linking to tenement.tenement. Business justification: Production runs process ore from specific tenements. Required for royalty payment calculations, tenement-specific statutory production reporting, and reconciliation of tenement reserves against actual

Foreign key linking to product.specification. Business justification: Each shift production run targets a specific product specification. Shift supervisors and metallurgists assess whether the run met grade, moisture, and impurity limits. This drives shift quality repor

Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Every processing shift generates tailings sent to specific TSF. Required for material flow tracking, mass balance reconciliation, TSF capacity planning, and water balance closure. Fundamental operatio

Actual average processing rate achieved during the shift, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph). Calculated as tonnes processed divided by operating hours. Compared against design throughput rate for performance gap analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10)

description: Total number of hours in the shift period as defined by the site calendar (typically 12 hours for a standard shift). Used as the denominator for plant utilisation and OEE calculations. Expressed in decimal hours.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Percentage of calendar hours during which the processing circuit was available for production, calculated as (calendar hours minus planned and unplanned downtime) divided by calendar hours, expressed as a percentage. Key KPI for processing plant performance against LOM plan targets.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Total dry tonnes of mineral concentrate or final processed product produced during the shift. Used for production accounting, inventory management, and sales dispatch planning. Sourced from product weighbridge or belt scale measurements.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES; SAP S/4HANA MM; JORC Code 2012

description: Timestamp when this shift production run record was first created in the Silver layer data product. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and SLA monitoring of data ingestion from OSIsoft PI System and MES. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category.
reference: ISO 8601; IFRS 6 Exploration Assets; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Nameplate or design throughput rate of the processing plant circuit, expressed in dry tonnes per hour (tph), as specified in the LOM plan and plant design documentation. Used as the benchmark for actual throughput performance assessment.
reference: Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik; LOM Plan; JORC Code 2012

description: Percentage of oxide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Part of the feed blend composition tracking used to predict and explain metallurgical recovery variations and circuit performance. Sourced from grade control block data and ROM stockpile management.
reference: Geovia GEMS; Hexagon MinePlan; OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code 2012

description: Percentage of sulphide ore type in the ROM feed blend during the shift. Used alongside oxide percentage to characterise the feed blend composition for metallurgical performance analysis and reagent dosage optimisation.
reference: Geovia GEMS; Hexagon MinePlan; OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code 2012

description: Chemical element symbol for which the feed head grade is reported (e.g., Fe for iron ore, Cu for copper, Li for lithium). Enables multi-commodity processing plants to correctly attribute grade measurements to the target mineral. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Fe|Cu|Au|Ag|Li|Ni|Co|Zn|Pb|Mo — promote to reference product]
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; IUPAC Chemical Nomenclature

description: Assayed head grade of the primary valuable element (e.g., Fe%, Cu%, Li%) in the ROM feed during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS composite sample assay results. Critical input for metallurgical recovery calculations and LOM plan reconciliation.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; SAMREC; ISO 17025 Laboratory Accreditation

description: Moisture content of the ROM feed expressed as a percentage of wet weight, measured by on-belt moisture analyser or laboratory sample. Used to convert wet tonnes to dry tonnes for metallurgical accounting and mass balance calculations.
reference: LabWare LIMS; OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code 2012; ISO 17025

description: Percentage of the valuable element in the ROM feed that is successfully recovered into the final concentrate or product during the shift. Calculated from feed grade, product grade, and mass balance. Core KPI for processing efficiency and LOM plan reconciliation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Overall Equipment Effectiveness expressed as a percentage, combining availability, performance, and quality rate for the processing plant during the shift. Industry-standard KPI for processing plant productivity benchmarking against LOM plan targets. Sourced from OSIsoft PI System.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Actual number of hours the processing plant was in productive operation during the shift, excluding planned and unplanned downtime. Used in OEE calculation and throughput rate derivation. Expressed in decimal hours.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Hours of scheduled downtime during the shift for planned maintenance, inspections, or scheduled shutdowns. Used in circuit availability and OEE calculations. Sourced from Infor EAM work order schedules.
reference: Infor EAM; OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10)

description: Percentage of available hours during which the plant was actively processing ore, calculated as operating hours divided by available hours. Reflects how effectively available plant time is being used for production. Reported in daily production meetings.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10); ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Dominant ore type or lithological classification of the ROM feed processed during the shift (e.g., oxide, sulphide, mixed, transitional, fresh). Determines the applicable processing circuit configuration and expected metallurgical recovery. Sourced from Geovia GEMS geological model and grade control data.
reference: Geovia GEMS; Hexagon MinePlan; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; SAMREC

description: Assayed grade of the primary valuable element in the final concentrate or product produced during the shift, expressed as a percentage. Sourced from LabWare LIMS product sample assay. Used for product quality certification, sales contract compliance, and metallurgical accounting.
reference: LabWare LIMS; JORC Code 2012; NI 43-101; ISO 17025 Laboratory Accreditation

business_glossary_term: Run of Mine (ROM) Feed Tonnes Dry
description: Total dry tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, calculated by applying the measured moisture factor to wet tonnes. Dry tonnes are the standard basis for metallurgical accounting, resource reporting, and LOM plan reconciliation.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework; NI 43-101

business_glossary_term: Run of Mine (ROM) Feed Tonnes Wet
description: Total wet tonnes of Run of Mine (ROM) ore fed to the processing plant during the shift, as measured at the feed conveyor belt scale. Wet weight includes moisture content. Used for mass balance and throughput reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Current lifecycle state of the shift production run record. 'open' indicates the shift is in progress; 'completed' indicates shift has ended and data is captured; 'under_review' indicates supervisor review pending; 'approved' indicates shift supervisor sign-off complete; 'cancelled' indicates the shift was voided. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS category.
reference: MES (Manufacturing Execution System); ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Calendar date on which the production shift commenced. Used as the primary date dimension for daily production reporting, reconciliation against LOM plan targets, and regulatory reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP category (date-level precision).
reference: OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Precise date and time when the production shift concluded, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for shift duration calculation, handover documentation, and OEE reporting. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; IEC 62541 OPC-UA; ISO 8601

description: Free-text operational notes recorded by the shift supervisor covering significant events, process upsets, equipment issues, safety observations, or other contextual information relevant to the shift production run. Supports shift handover communication and exception reporting.

Designates the shift rotation within the calendar day: 'day' (typically 06:00–18:00), 'night' (18:00–06:00), or 'afternoon' (14:00–22:00) depending on site roster configuration. Used for shift-based production reporting and crew scheduling.
reference: MES (Manufacturing Execution System); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Externally-known business identifier for the shift production run, used in daily production reports, shift handover documentation, and reconciliation against LOM plan targets. Format: SPR-YYYY-MM-DD-PLANTCODE-SEQ. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER category.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES (Manufacturing Execution System); JORC Code 2012

description: Precise date and time when the production shift commenced, sourced from the MES or OSIsoft PI System historian. Used for accurate shift duration calculation, OEE computation, and integration with SCADA event logs. ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; IEC 62541 OPC-UA; ISO 8601

description: Date and time when the shift supervisor formally approved and signed off the shift production run record. Marks the transition from 'under_review' to 'approved' status. Required for daily production reporting and audit compliance.

Total dry tonnes of ore that completed the primary processing circuit during the shift. May differ from ROM feed tonnes due to in-circuit inventory changes, spillage, or sampling losses. Used as the primary throughput metric for production reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER QUANTITATIVE_RESULT category.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; MES; JORC Code 2012; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Hours of unscheduled downtime during the shift due to equipment breakdowns, process upsets, or emergency stoppages. Key input for MTBF and MTTR analysis and OEE degradation reporting.
reference: Infor EAM; OSIsoft PI System; OEE Industry Standard (SEMI E10)

description: Timestamp when this shift production run record was last modified, including supervisor sign-off, data corrections, or status transitions. Supports audit trail and change tracking in the Silver layer. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category.
reference: ISO 8601; IFRS 6 Exploration Assets; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: Prevailing weather condition at the mine site during the shift that may have affected processing plant operations (e.g., extreme heat impacting reagent performance, rain increasing feed moisture, dust storms affecting conveyor operations). Used for operational exception reporting and HSE compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|storm|fog|high_wind|dust_storm|snow|extreme_heat — promote to reference product]
reference: IsoMetrix HSE; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; EIS (Environmental Impact Statement)

description: Boolean flag indicating whether adverse weather conditions materially impacted processing plant operations or throughput during the shift. Used to qualify production shortfalls in reporting and distinguish weather-related downtime from equipment or process failures.
reference: IsoMetrix HSE; ISO 14001 Environmental Management; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each slurry measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this time-series observation table. Entity role: EVENT_LOG — append-only stream of immutable slurry parameter observations captured from OSIsoft PI System via OPC-UA at key mineral processing points.

Reference to the physical instrument or sensor asset record in the asset management system (Infor EAM) that generated this measurement. Enables traceability from measurement data to instrument maintenance history, calibration records, and asset lifecycle management. Supports instrument drift detection and predictive maintenance analytics.
reference: Infor EAM; ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Reference to the process measurement point (instrument tag location) where the slurry parameters were captured. Corresponds to the PI tag source location in the OSIsoft PI System, identifying the physical sensor or instrument node in the mineral processing circuit (e.g., SAG mill discharge, flotation feed, leach feed).
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-95 Equipment Hierarchy

description: Reference to the mineral processing plant or facility where the slurry measurement was recorded. Enables multi-site reporting and benchmarking across processing facilities within the mining operation.
reference: ISA-95 Equipment Hierarchy; SAP S/4HANA Plant Master

description: Reference to the specific processing circuit (e.g., comminution, flotation, leaching, SX-EW) within the plant where the measurement was taken. Supports circuit-level mass balance and metallurgical accounting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-88 Batch Control Standard

description: Foreign key linking to processing.shift_production_run. Business justification: Slurry measurements are taken during a production shift run. One measurement belongs to one shift run; one shift run has many measurements. This links the measurement to the processing domain's shift

Reference to the operational shift during which this slurry measurement was recorded. Enables shift-level aggregation and reporting of process performance, throughput, and data quality statistics. Links to the shift schedule maintained in SAP S/4HANA HR or the mine operations shift management system.
reference: SAP S/4HANA HR; Mine Operations Shift Management; ISA-95

description: Foreign key linking to tailings.tsf. Business justification: Slurry measurements at the plant tailings outlet (density, flow rate, pH, solids content) are used for TSF deposition planning and tailings characterisation. Linking measurement points to the receivin

The configured upper alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value exceeds this limit, an automated high alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-18.2 Alarm Management; IEC 62682 Alarm Systems

description: The configured lower alarm threshold for this measurement point in the SCADA/DCS system. When the measured value falls below this limit, an automated low alarm is generated for operator response. Expressed in the same engineering unit as the measurement. Used for automated alarm generation and process safety management.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-18.2 Alarm Management; IEC 62682 Alarm Systems

description: The date of the most recent calibration performed on the instrument or sensor that generated this measurement, in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used to assess measurement reliability, identify instruments due for recalibration, and support data quality investigations. Instruments with expired calibration may have their measurements flagged as 'suspect' in the data quality flag.
reference: Infor EAM; ISO 9001 Quality Management; OIML International Measurement Standards

description: Electrical conductivity of the slurry at the measurement point, expressed in millisiemens per centimetre (mS/cm). Indicator of dissolved ion concentration, relevant for process water quality management, reagent dosing control in flotation, and environmental monitoring of process water circuits.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISO 7888 Water Conductivity; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: OSIsoft PI System data quality status flag for this measurement record, indicating the reliability of the captured value. 'good' = valid measurement within instrument range; 'suspect' = value outside expected range or instrument drift detected; 'bad' = sensor fault or communication failure; 'substituted' = value replaced by manual entry or interpolation. Critical for downstream metallurgical mass balance calculations and automated alarm generation. Aligns with OPC-UA quality codes.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; OPC-UA Standard IEC 62541; ISA-95

description: The arithmetic difference between the actual measured value and the configured process setpoint value (measured_value minus setpoint_value), expressed in the same engineering unit. A non-derived operational field captured directly from the PI System control deviation tag. Positive values indicate measurement above setpoint; negative values indicate below setpoint. Used for process control performance monitoring and alarm root cause analysis.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-95; Process Control Engineering Standards

description: The unit of measurement associated with the primary measurement value for this PI tag (e.g., t/m³, %w/w, m³/hr, pH, °C, cP, mS/cm, µm). Sourced from the PI tag engineering unit configuration. Supports dynamic unit-aware analytics and cross-tag comparisons in the metallurgical historian.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; SI Units (International System of Units); ISA-95

description: Volumetric flow rate of the slurry stream at the measurement point, expressed in cubic metres per hour (m³/hr). Measured by electromagnetic or Coriolis flow meter. Used in metallurgical mass balance, throughput calculations, and pump performance monitoring. Fundamental input for recovery rate and concentrate production calculations.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-5.1 Instrumentation Symbols; Metallurgical Accounting Standards

description: The date and time (UTC) at which this measurement record was ingested into the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer from the OSIsoft PI System source. Distinct from the measurement_timestamp (when the physical measurement occurred). Used for data pipeline latency monitoring, SLA tracking, and identifying late-arriving data in streaming ingestion pipelines.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer; Data Governance Standards

description: Boolean flag indicating whether an active process alarm condition existed at this measurement point at the time of measurement (True = alarm active; False = no alarm). Sourced from the SCADA/DCS alarm management system via OSIsoft PI System event frames. Enables alarm frequency analysis, process stability assessment, and operator response time reporting.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-18.2 Alarm Management; IEC 62682 Alarm Systems

description: Boolean flag indicating whether the measurement value was within the defined process control limits (alarm_low_limit to alarm_high_limit) at the time of measurement (True = in control; False = out of control). Supports Statistical Process Control (SPC) analysis, process stability KPIs, and automated metallurgical mass balance quality gates.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; ISA-95; Statistical Process Control (SPC) Standards; JORC Code

description: Mass flow rate of dry solids in the slurry stream, expressed in dry metric tonnes per hour (t/hr). Calculated from volumetric flow rate and slurry density adjusted for percent solids. Primary throughput metric for metallurgical accounting, LOM (Life of Mine) plan tracking, and production reporting against targets.
reference: OSIsoft PI System; JORC Code; Metallurgical Accounting Standards; LOM Plan

Name or identifier of the person who approved the water balance for operational use or regulatory submission. Typically a senior technical authority or site manager.
Date and time when the water balance calculation was performed. Supports audit trail and version control for reconciliation purposes.
Free-text field for recording observations, assumptions, data quality issues, or operational events that affected the water balance during the period. Supports interpretation and future reference.
End date of the water balance calculation period. Defines the reporting interval for inflows, outflows, and storage changes.
Start date of the water balance calculation period. Typically daily, weekly, or monthly depending on operational requirements and regulatory reporting frequency.

Difference between calculated storage change and measured storage change, measured in cubic meters. Represents the water balance closure error and indicates measurement accuracy and unaccounted losses or gains.

Balance reconciliation variance expressed as a percentage of total inflow. Provides a normalized measure of water balance accuracy. Acceptable variance thresholds are typically defined in operational procedures.

Current status of the water balance record in the approval workflow. Draft indicates initial calculation, preliminary for review, final for operational use, revised for corrections, and approved for regulatory submission.
Elevation of the TSF pond water surface at the end of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate closing storage volume and freeboard.
Volume of water stored in the TSF pond at the end of the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Derived from pond level survey and storage-elevation relationship.
Date and time when the water balance record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Current elevation of the TSF embankment crest at the time of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate freeboard and assess storage capacity.

Volume of water returned from the TSF to the process plant or water storage via decant structures during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Primary mechanism for water recovery and freeboard management.
Minimum required freeboard specified in the TSF design for the current operational stage, measured in meters. Used to assess compliance with design criteria and trigger operational responses.
Volume of water lost from the TSF pond through evaporation during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated from evaporation rate and pond surface area.

Vertical distance between the pond water level and the embankment crest at the end of the balance period, measured in meters. Critical safety parameter for TSF operational management and regulatory compliance.
Volume of groundwater ingress into the TSF pond during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Typically estimated from piezometric monitoring and seepage models.
Date and time when the water balance record was last updated. Supports version control and change tracking for water management data.
Elevation of the TSF pond water surface at the start of the balance period, measured in meters above a defined datum. Used to calculate opening storage volume.
Volume of water stored in the TSF pond at the start of the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Derived from pond level survey and storage-elevation relationship.

Volume of process water discharged to the TSF from mineral processing operations during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Includes tailings slurry water and any additional process water streams.
Volume of direct rainfall onto the TSF pond surface during the balance period, calculated from rainfall depth and pond area, measured in cubic meters.
Name or identifier of the person who reviewed and validated the water balance calculation. Ensures quality control and technical oversight of water management data.

Volume of surface water runoff from the TSF catchment area entering the pond during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Includes runoff from embankment slopes and upstream catchment if not diverted.

Volume of water lost through seepage from the TSF embankment and foundation during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Estimated from piezometric data, seepage collection systems, and geotechnical models.
Volume of water discharged through the TSF spillway during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Typically occurs during extreme rainfall events when pond capacity is exceeded.
Net change in water storage volume during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as closing storage minus opening storage. Should reconcile with total inflow minus total outflow.
Sum of all water inflows to the TSF during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as process water + rainfall + runoff + groundwater + other inflows.
Sum of all water outflows from the TSF during the balance period, measured in cubic meters. Calculated as evaporation + seepage + decant return + spillway discharge + other outflows.
Assessment of whether the water balance period operations complied with water licence conditions including discharge limits, return water quality, and reporting requirements.

Current outstanding receivables balance against this credit limit. Calculated in real-time as the sum of all unpaid invoices and open deliveries. When utilisation approaches or exceeds the limit amount, shipment blocks are triggered.

SAP user ID of the person who created the material document. Provides audit trail for transaction origination.
Outbound delivery document number for stock transport orders. Links the goods issue to the logistics execution document.
Date on the physical document or the date the transaction was initiated. May differ from posting date.
Date when the material document was created in the system. Represents when the record was first captured.
Time when the material document was created in the system (HH:MM:SS format).
Name of the person or organizational unit receiving the material. Used for accountability and tracking.
Free-text notes or comments entered at the material document header level. Provides additional context for the transaction.
Line item number within the material document. Represents the sequence of individual material movements within a single document.
SAP material document number (MBLNR) that uniquely identifies the goods movement transaction in the source system. This is the externally-known business identifier for the goods issue.
Fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Part of the compound key in SAP along with material document number.
Single-character indicator that further classifies the movement (e.g., B for goods movement for purchase order, F for production order, L for delivery).
Current lifecycle status of the goods issue transaction. Indicates whether the movement has been successfully posted, reversed, or is pending.

description: SAP movement type code that classifies the nature of the goods movement. Common values include 201 (goods issue to cost center), 261 (goods issue to work order/production order), 301 (plant-to-plant transfer), 311 (storage location to storage location transfer), 551 (scrapping from storage location), 202/262 (reversal of 201/261).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type Configuration

description: Four-character code identifying the mine site or plant from which the material was issued. Represents the source location for the goods movement.
Date on which the goods issue was posted to inventory and financial accounting. This is the principal business event timestamp for the transaction.
Quantity of material issued in the base unit of measure. Positive values represent outbound movements; negative values represent reversals.
Code indicating the business reason for the goods issue (e.g., planned maintenance, breakdown repair, operational consumption, scrapping due to damage).
Four-character code identifying the destination mine site or plant for inter-plant transfers (movement type 301). Null for consumption issues.
Four-character code identifying the destination storage location for inter-storage-location transfers (movement type 311). Null for consumption issues.
External reference document number (e.g., delivery note, packing slip, transfer note) associated with the goods issue.
Line item number within the reservation document.
Material reservation number that pre-allocated the stock for this issue. Links the goods issue to the planning document.
Flag indicating whether this goods issue has been reversed (true) or is an active transaction (false).
Movement type used for the reversal transaction (e.g., 202 reverses 201, 262 reverses 261). Populated only if the transaction has been reversed.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Movement Type Configuration

description: Single-character code indicating special stock categories (e.g., E for sales order stock, Q for project stock, K for consignment stock).
Unit of measure for the issued quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters, M for meters, TON for tonnes).
Physical location or area within the receiving site where the material is delivered (e.g., workshop bay, crusher station, ROM pad).
Split valuation type for materials managed with multiple stock types (e.g., own stock vs consignment stock).
Maintenance or production work order number to which the material is issued for movement type 261. Links material consumption to specific maintenance activities or production runs.

Reference to the mine site, processing plant, or facility where goods are received. Identifies the receiving location at the organizational level.
Reference to the purchase order against which this goods receipt is posted. Links the goods receipt to the originating procurement document.
Reference to the specific purchase order line item for which goods are being received. Enables three-way match validation (PO-GR-Invoice).

Reference to the material master record for the material or equipment being received. Links to the material catalog.
Reference to the vendor or supplier who delivered the materials. Identifies the counterparty in the goods receipt transaction.
Reference to the specific warehouse, ROM (Run of Mine) pad, or storage area within the plant where materials are physically stored after receipt.

description: Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Capital GRs post to WBS for asset under construction tracking, capex reporting, and project cost control. Essential for capital project accounting in mining.
The batch or lot number assigned to the received materials for traceability and quality control. Critical for tracking ore batches, chemical reagents, and spare parts through the supply chain.
The shipping document number issued by the carrier for the transported goods. Critical for FOB (Free on Board) and CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight) shipments in mining commodity trading.
Reference number of the certificate of analysis provided by the vendor or generated by the laboratory. Documents quality specifications and test results for the received material.

Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the valuation amount (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).
The date printed on the external delivery note or packing slip provided by the vendor. May differ from posting date due to processing delays.

The current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction. Posted indicates successful inventory update; reversed indicates cancellation; pending inspection indicates awaiting quality clearance; completed indicates full processing including invoice verification.

The externally-known unique document number assigned to this goods receipt transaction in the ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) system. Used for audit trail and three-way match in invoice verification.

description: The fiscal year in which the goods receipt document was posted. Used in combination with document number for unique identification in SAP systems.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document Year (MSEG-MJAHR)

description: The line item sequence number within the goods receipt document. Identifies individual material lines when multiple items are received in a single transaction.

User ID of the person who last modified the goods receipt document. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
Date and time when the goods receipt record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to the document.
Sequential line item number within the material document. Identifies individual material movements within a single goods receipt document.
The unique material document number generated by the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA or Pronto Xi) upon posting the goods receipt. This is the business identifier for the goods receipt transaction.
Fiscal year in which the material document was posted. Used in combination with material document number for unique identification in SAP systems.
The date and time when the goods receipt record was last updated. Tracks changes to quantity, status, or other fields after initial creation.
A single-character code indicating the direction of the goods movement (e.g., B for goods receipt, H for reversal). Used for system processing logic.
The SAP movement type code that classifies the type of goods receipt transaction (e.g., 101 for GR against PO, 501 for transfer posting). Determines how inventory and financial accounts are updated.
The weight of the received materials excluding packaging and containers. Used for inventory valuation and yield calculations in mineral processing.

The date on which the goods receipt was posted to inventory in the financial and materials management system. This is the official accounting date for inventory valuation and triggers the three-way match process.

Indicates whether the received materials must undergo quality inspection before being released for unrestricted use. When true, materials are placed in quality inspection stock pending LIMS (Laboratory Information Management System) assay results.
The quantity originally ordered on the purchase order line. Used to calculate delivery variance and over/under delivery tolerance.

The physical quantity of material received and accepted at the storage location. This quantity updates inventory levels and is used in the three-way match against purchase order quantity and invoice quantity.
Condition assessment of the goods upon physical receipt. Indicates whether materials were received in acceptable condition or if there were issues such as damage or quantity discrepancies.
The actual date when the physical goods were received at the mine site, warehouse, or processing plant. Used for delivery performance tracking.
The name of the warehouse or stores personnel who physically accepted and verified the delivered goods. Used for accountability and audit trail.
Free-text field for additional notes or comments about the goods receipt transaction. May include observations about packaging condition, delivery delays, or special handling instructions.
The document number of the reversal transaction if this goods receipt has been cancelled. Links to the offsetting material document.
Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled. When true, the inventory movement has been negated and the original transaction is no longer valid.
The coded reason for reversing the goods receipt (e.g., damaged goods, quantity discrepancy, wrong material delivered). Used for root cause analysis and vendor performance evaluation.
Unique serial number for serialized equipment or high-value assets. Enables individual asset tracking and lifecycle management.

description: The inventory status category of the received goods. Unrestricted stock is available for use; quality inspection stock awaits testing; blocked stock cannot be used; restricted stock has limited usage.
The weight of packaging, containers, or transport equipment. Calculated as gross weight minus net weight.
The unit in which the received quantity is measured (e.g., TON for tonnes, KG for kilograms, EA for each, M3 for cubic meters). Must align with material master and purchase order UOM.
The specific physical location or dock where the delivery vehicle was unloaded (e.g., Dock 3, ROM Pad A, Reagent Bay 2). Used for logistics planning and congestion management.

The valuation category for split-valuation scenarios where the same material is valued differently based on procurement source or quality grade (e.g., domestic vs. imported ore).
The batch or lot number assigned by the vendor to the delivered materials. Used for traceability back to the suppliers production records and quality certificates.
The unit in which weights are measured (e.g., TON for tonnes, KG for kilograms, LB for pounds). Must be consistent across gross, net, and tare weights.

Reference to the plant where the material is stored.
Reference to the material being tracked in inventory.

Quantity of material available for unrestricted use and consumption in operations.
Method used to value the inventory, such as standard cost, moving average price, FIFO, or LIFO.

Monetary value of the variance between system and counted quantities.
