description: Capital expenditure required to sustain current production levels, including equipment replacement, tailings storage facility (TSF) raises, and mine infrastructure maintenance. Included in AISC but excluded from C1 cash cost. Sourced from SAP PS project actuals.
reference: World Gold Council AISC Standard; SAP S/4HANA PS Project System; IFRS IAS 16

description: Aggregate of all cost components (mining OPEX, processing OPEX, G&A, sustaining CAPEX, royalties, refining and transport) less by-product credits for the reporting period, expressed in USD. This is the numerator used to compute the per-unit AISC or C1 cost.
reference: World Gold Council AISC Standard; IFRS IAS 21 Foreign Currency; SAP S/4HANA CO

description: Calculated per-unit AISC or C1 cash cost expressed in USD per production unit of measure (e.g., USD/oz for gold, USD/lb for copper, USD/dmt for iron ore). This is the primary cost disclosure metric for investor reporting and peer benchmarking. Computed as total_cost_usd divided by production_volume.
reference: World Gold Council AISC Standard; Brook Hunt C1 Framework; SEC S-K 1300

description: Date and time when this cost performance report record was last modified. Tracks restatements, corrections, or updates to cost components following period-end close adjustments in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO; IFRS IAS 8 Accounting Policies Changes and Errors; Databricks Delta Lake

description: Percentage variance of actual unit cost result against the budget unit cost for the period. Positive values indicate cost overrun; negative values indicate cost underperformance. Calculated as ((unit_cost_result - budget_unit_cost) / budget_unit_cost) * 100.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Variance Analysis; ICMM Cost Reporting Guidelines

description: Percentage variance of actual unit cost result against the prior period unit cost. Used in quarterly investor reports and management commentary to explain cost movement drivers.
reference: World Gold Council AISC Standard; ASX Listing Rules; SEC S-K 1300

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each OPEX budget record in the silver layer lakehouse. Primary key for the opex_budget data product.

Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_contract. Business justification: Mining OPEX budgets for contracted services (maintenance, reagents, haulage) are executed under standing procurement contracts. This FK enables contract spend vs. budget tracking and supports C1 cash

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: OPEX budgets are allocated by cost centre. The cost_centre_code should be replaced with FK to cost_centre.cost_centre_id to enable joining to cost centre master data including hierarchy, responsible m

Foreign key linking to finance.general_ledger_account. Business justification: OPEX budgets are allocated by GL account. The gl_account_code should be replaced with FK to general_ledger_account.general_ledger_account_id to enable joining to account master data including account

Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: OPEX budgets are prepared at mine site level for site-level cost management and reporting. opex_budget.mine_site_code is a denormalized plain-text reference; replacing with mine_site_id FK enforces re

Foreign key linking to processing.processing_plant. Business justification: Operating budgets are set at plant level for cost control, variance reporting, and AISC forecasting. Links financial planning to operational assets for budget vs actual analysis and cost centre perfor

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: OPEX budgets roll up to profit centres for segment reporting. The profit_centre_code should be replaced with FK to profit_centre.profit_centre_id to enable joining to profit centre master data.

Foreign key linking to finance.project_valuation. Business justification: OPEX budgets for mining operations are informed by project valuations which include opex_unit_usd and production rate assumptions. Linking opex_budget to project_valuation enables comparison of budget

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: OPEX budgets can be project-related and allocated to WBS elements. The wbs_element_code should be replaced with FK to wbs_element.wbs_element_id to enable joining to project master data.
The actual operating expenditure incurred against this budget line for the fiscal period, sourced from SAP FI/CO actuals postings. Used to calculate budget variance and support C1 and AISC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; ICMM AISC Reporting

description: The date on which this OPEX budget record was formally approved by the designated approver. Marks the transition from draft to approved status in the budget lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Audit Standards

description: Identifier for the budget planning cycle (e.g., AOP-2024, MYR-2024-Q2) during which this OPEX budget was prepared and submitted. Supports multi-cycle budget management including Annual Operating Plan (AOP) and mid-year reviews.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; Internal Budget Governance; ICMM

description: Free-text description of the OPEX budget line providing business context, justification, or notes entered by the budget owner. Supports budget review and audit processes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; Internal Budget Governance

description: The end date of the period covered by this OPEX budget record. Defines the temporal boundary for expenditure allocation and variance measurement.

The budgeted quantity of the resource or activity associated with this OPEX line (e.g., tonnes of ore processed, litres of fuel, hours of labour). Used in unit cost calculations for C1 and AISC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; ICMM C1 Cash Cost Reporting

description: Externally-known alphanumeric identifier assigned to the OPEX budget record in SAP S/4HANA CO, used for cross-system traceability and audit referencing. Corresponds to the SAP internal order or cost centre budget document number.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards)

description: The start date of the period covered by this OPEX budget record. May align with the fiscal period start or represent a sub-period budget window for project-based expenditure.

Current workflow status of the OPEX budget record within the budget approval lifecycle. Controls editability and reporting inclusion (e.g., only approved budgets are used in C1 and AISC baseline calculations).

Identifies the version of the budget record, distinguishing the original approved budget from subsequent revisions and forecasts. Supports budget lifecycle tracking and variance analysis against baseline. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: original|revised_1|revised_2|revised_3|forecast|reforecast|final — promote to reference product if more versions are required]
The value of purchase orders and contracts committed against this OPEX budget line but not yet invoiced or posted as actuals. Supports available budget calculation and cash flow forecasting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM-PO; CO-OM Commitment Management; IFRS IAS 37

description: The primary mineral commodity associated with this OPEX budget line. Enables commodity-level cost reporting and supports C1 cash cost per tonne calculations by commodity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|gold|silver|other — promote to reference product]
reference: JORC; ICMM; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: High-level classification of the operating expenditure type. Used to segment OPEX into functional areas for C1 cash cost and AISC reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mining|processing|maintenance|administration|hse|logistics|exploration|rehabilitation — promote to reference product]
reference: ICMM (International Council on Mining and Metals); GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement; AISC reporting standards

description: SAP CO cost element code providing a secondary classification of the expenditure nature (e.g., wages, fuel, reagents, explosives). Bridges the GL account to cost object controlling for granular OPEX analysis.
The timestamp when this OPEX budget record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail entry point for the budget record lifecycle.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; ISO 27001 Audit Logging

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this budget record (e.g., AUD, USD, ZAR). Supports multi-currency mining operations and foreign exchange reporting.
reference: ISO 4217; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates; SAP S/4HANA FI

description: The foreign exchange rate applied to convert budget amounts from the transaction currency to the group reporting currency. Sourced from SAP FI exchange rate tables for the applicable fiscal period.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 21; SEC Mining Disclosure Rules S-K 1300

description: The fiscal period (month number 1–12) within the fiscal year to which this OPEX budget record applies. Supports monthly budget phasing and period-level variance reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI-GL; IFRS IAS 34 Interim Reporting

description: The fiscal year (e.g., 2024) to which this OPEX budget record belongs. Used for annual budget cycle management and year-over-year variance analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; IFRS 6 Exploration

description: The latest forecast of total OPEX spend for the fiscal period, incorporating actuals to date plus projected remaining spend. Used in rolling forecast cycles and AISC guidance updates.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; ICMM AISC Reporting; IFRS IAS 34

description: Indicates whether any portion of this OPEX budget line has been reclassified as capital expenditure (CAPEX) under IFRS 6 or IAS 16. True if capitalised; False if fully expensed as OPEX. Critical for IFRS 6 exploration accounting compliance.
reference: IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources; IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; SAP S/4HANA FI

business_glossary_term: Life of Mine (LOM) Plan Included Flag
description: Indicates whether this OPEX budget line is included in the Life of Mine (LOM) plan financial model. True if included in LOM; False if excluded (e.g., one-off or non-recurring items). Supports NPV and DCF modelling.
reference: JORC (Joint Ore Reserves Committee); CIM; Deswik LOM Planning; ICMM

description: The date on which the most recent budget revision was applied to this record. Tracks the recency of budget amendments for governance and audit purposes.

Classifies the OPEX budget line according to industry cost reporting frameworks. C1 cash cost covers direct mining and processing costs; AISC (All-In Sustaining Cost) includes sustaining capital and corporate overhead. Critical for external cost disclosure to investors and regulators.
reference: ICMM AISC Reporting Guidelines; World Gold Council AISC Standard; SEC S-K 1300; CIM (Canadian Institute of Mining)

description: The initially approved OPEX budget amount in the reporting currency for the specified cost centre, GL account, mine site, and fiscal period. Represents the baseline against which actual spend and revisions are measured.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; ICMM Cost Reporting Guidelines

description: The unit of measure for the budget_quantity field (e.g., t = tonnes, kt = kilotonnes, L = litres, h = hours, kWh = kilowatt-hours). Ensures dimensional consistency in unit cost calculations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: t|kt|Mt|L|kL|h|kWh|MWh|m3|kg — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units; ICMM

description: The most recently approved revised OPEX budget amount after formal budget amendments. Null if no revision has been made. Used in variance analysis to compare against actual spend.

Identifies the operational system of record from which this OPEX budget record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks silver layer and reconciliation between planning and financial systems.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage; SAP S/4HANA; Hexagon MinePlan; Deswik

description: The timestamp of the most recent update to this OPEX budget record. Used for change data capture in the Databricks silver layer and incremental load processing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; Databricks Lakehouse Delta Lake CDC

description: The difference between the revised (or original if no revision) budget amount and the actual spend amount for the fiscal period. Positive value indicates underspend; negative value indicates overspend. Stored as a pre-computed field to support period-end reporting without recalculation overhead.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; IFRS IAS 1; ICMM Cost Reporting; SEC S-K 1300

description: The budget variance expressed as a percentage of the budget amount ((budget - actual) / budget * 100). Enables proportional variance analysis and exception reporting across cost centres and mine sites.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-OM; ICMM Cost Reporting Guidelines; IFRS IAS 1

description: Unique identifier for the profit centre. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: SAP CO profit centres are assigned to company codes (legal entities) for segment reporting under IFRS. The profit_centre table currently stores company_code as a denormalized string. Adding legal_enti
Boolean flag indicating whether this profit centre is included in All-In Sustaining Cost (AISC) reporting for external disclosures.
The SAP business area code used for cross-company code reporting and segment analysis.
Boolean flag indicating whether this profit centre reports C1 cash cost metrics for commodity margin analysis.
The date on which the profit centre was formally closed or decommissioned.

The primary mineral commodity associated with this profit centre (e.g., iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, nickel). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: iron_ore|copper|coal|lithium|nickel|gold|silver|zinc|bauxite|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The SAP Controlling Area to which this profit centre belongs. Controlling areas represent organizational units for cost and profit management.
The cost centre group or hierarchy to which this profit centre's cost centres roll up for cost allocation and reporting.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the profit centre is domiciled (e.g., AUS, CAN, CHL).
The timestamp when this profit centre record was first created in the system.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the profit centre's local reporting currency (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD).
Boolean flag indicating whether this profit centre applies IFRS 6 exploration and evaluation asset accounting.
reference: IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources

description: The functional currency as defined by IAS 21 for this profit centre's primary economic environment.
reference: IAS 21 The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: The geographic region or jurisdiction where the profit centre operates (e.g., Western Australia, Queensland, South America).
The hierarchical node or path representing this profit centre's position in the organizational profit centre hierarchy.

business_glossary_term: International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) Segment Flag
description: Boolean flag indicating whether this profit centre is a reportable operating segment under IFRS 8.

business_glossary_term: Joint Ore Reserves Committee (JORC) Resource Category
description: The highest JORC resource classification category applicable to this profit centre's mineral resources (measured, indicated, inferred), if the profit centre is a mine site.
The username or identifier of the user who last modified this profit centre record.
The timestamp when this profit centre record was last updated or modified.
Reference identifier to the Life of Mine plan document or system record associated with this profit centre, if applicable.
The externally-known alphanumeric code identifying the profit centre in SAP CO. Used for reporting and integration with external systems.
Detailed description of the profit centre's business scope, operations, and responsibilities.
The full business name of the profit centre (e.g., 'Iron Ore Mine Site A', 'Copper Processing Plant B', 'Lithium Business Unit').
Current lifecycle status of the profit centre indicating whether it is operational, suspended, or closed.
Classification of the profit centre by operational function (e.g., mine site, processing plant, commodity business unit, corporate function, exploration division, logistics hub).
The ore reserve classification (proved or probable) for this profit centre's economically mineable reserves, per JORC or equivalent standards.
The operating segment code aligned to IFRS 8 segment reporting requirements. Used for external financial disclosures.
Abbreviated name or acronym for the profit centre used in reports and dashboards.
The date from which this profit centre became active and operational for financial reporting.
The date until which this profit centre is valid. Null for open-ended profit centres.
Unique surrogate identifier for the GL account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this data product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI Chart of Accounts (SKA1/SKB1)

description: Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: GL accounts in SAP FI are defined per company code (legal entity) via the chart of accounts assignment. The general_ledger_account table currently stores company_code as a denormalized string. Adding

ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the GL account (SAP WAERS). Mining enterprises operating across multiple jurisdictions (Australia, USA, Canada, South Africa) may maintain accounts in local currencies (AUD, USD, CAD, ZAR) with group currency translation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.WAERS; ISO 4217 Currency Codes; IFRS IAS 21 Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates

description: SAP account group (KTOKS) that controls the number range and field selection for the GL account. Groups accounts by functional category (e.g., revenue, OPEX, CAPEX, exploration, balance sheet). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue|opex|capex|exploration|balance_sheet|intercompany|tax|depreciation|provisions — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1.KTOKS; IFRS 6; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Extended description of the GL account providing full business context, including the nature of costs or revenues captured (e.g., 'OPEX — Drill and Blast Consumables — Open Pit'). Supports financial reporting and audit.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKAT; IFRS IAS 1

description: Short descriptive name of the GL account as defined in the chart of accounts (SAP field TXT20/TXT50). Used in financial reports, trial balances, and cost postings across mining operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKAT.TXT50; IFRS IAS 1

description: The externally-known SAP GL account number (SAKNR) from the chart of accounts, uniquely identifying the account within the company code. Used in all journal entries and cost postings across the mining enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1.SAKNR; IFRS IAS 1 Presentation of Financial Statements

description: Current lifecycle status of the GL account. Active accounts accept postings; inactive accounts are retained for historical reporting; blocked accounts are temporarily suspended; accounts marked for deletion are pending removal from the chart of accounts after period-end.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Financial Controls

description: SAP account type (GVTYP) classifying the GL account as balance sheet or profit and loss. Determines how the account is treated during fiscal year-end closing and financial statement preparation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1.GVTYP; IFRS IAS 1; JORC Code 2012

description: Mapping of the GL account to the World Gold Council AISC cost category framework, enabling AISC and C1 cash cost reporting. Categories include mining, processing, site G&A, sustaining CAPEX, royalties, and by-product credits. Used for commodity cost benchmarking across iron ore, copper, coal, lithium, and nickel operations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: c1_mining|c1_processing|c1_site_ga|royalties|by_product_credits|sustaining_capex|corporate_ga|reclamation — promote to reference product]
reference: World Gold Council AISC Standard 2018; ICMM; CIM Cost Estimation Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Alternative Account Number (Country Chart of Accounts)
description: SAP alternative account number (ALTKT) from a country-specific or group chart of accounts. Used in mining companies operating across multiple jurisdictions to map local statutory accounts to the group chart of accounts for consolidated IFRS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.ALTKT; IFRS IAS 27; SEC S-K 1300; SAMREC

description: For balance sheet GL accounts, the IFRS balance sheet category (e.g., property plant and equipment, mineral rights, exploration assets, inventory, provisions for rehabilitation). Supports IFRS financial statement presentation and mine closure liability accounting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ppe|mineral_rights|exploration_assets|inventory|trade_receivables|trade_payables|rehabilitation_provision|deferred_tax — promote to reference product]
reference: IFRS IAS 16 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS IAS 37 Provisions; IFRS 6; JORC Code 2012

description: SAP user ID (USNAM) of the person who last modified this GL account master record. Required for SOX internal controls, financial audit trails, and segregation of duties compliance in the mining finance function.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1.USNAM; Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Audit

description: SAP chart of accounts key (KTOPL) to which this GL account belongs. A mining enterprise may operate multiple charts of accounts across jurisdictions (e.g., Australian GAAP, IFRS, US GAAP for SEC reporting).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI T004.KTOPL; IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources

description: Indicates whether purchase order commitments and funds reservations are tracked against this GL account in SAP CO. When true, uncommitted CAPEX and OPEX budget balances are visible, supporting mine budget control and CAPEX approval workflows.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Commitment Management; CAPEX/OPEX Budgeting; ICMM

description: Indicates whether postings to this GL account require a cost centre assignment in SAP CO. When true, all OPEX postings must be assigned to a cost centre (e.g., drill and blast, haulage, processing plant, maintenance) for mine cost centre accounting and AISC reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-CCA; AISC World Gold Council Standard; ICMM

description: SAP Controlling (CO) cost element category (KATYP) indicating whether this is a primary cost element (linked to a P&L GL account, e.g., OPEX, CAPEX) or a secondary cost element (internal allocations, activity types). Critical for mine cost centre accounting and AISC/C1 cash cost reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO CSKA.KATYP; AISC (All-In Sustaining Cost) World Gold Council Standard; CIM Cost Estimation Guidelines

description: Timestamp when this GL account record was first created in the source SAP FI system. Provides audit trail for chart of accounts governance and financial control compliance.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Audit; ISO 45001

description: SAP deletion flag (XLOEV) indicating the GL account has been marked for deletion from the chart of accounts. Accounts with this flag set will be removed during the next chart of accounts reorganisation run. Retained for audit trail purposes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1.XLOEV; Internal Financial Controls

description: The date from which this GL account became active and available for postings. Used to track the introduction of new accounts during chart of accounts restructuring, mine commissioning, or new project setup.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Financial Controls

description: The date on which this GL account was deactivated or closed. Null for currently active accounts. Used to manage account lifecycle during mine closure, asset disposal, or chart of accounts rationalisation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI; IFRS IAS 1; Mine Rehabilitation and Closure Accounting

description: Mining-specific classification of the GL account as Operating Expenditure (OPEX), Capital Expenditure (CAPEX), exploration expenditure (IFRS 6), rehabilitation provision, sustaining CAPEX, or growth CAPEX. Drives AISC and C1 cash cost reporting and LOM financial modelling.
reference: IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources; AISC World Gold Council; ICMM; JORC Code 2012

description: SAP field status group (FSTAG) controlling which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting to this GL account. Determines mandatory entry of cost centre, WBS element, profit centre, or other account assignment objects for mining cost control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.FSTAG; Internal Financial Controls

description: Classification of the GL account within the financial statement structure (e.g., Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow Statement). Supports IFRS-compliant financial reporting and SEC S-K 1300 mining disclosure requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: income_statement|balance_sheet|cash_flow_operating|cash_flow_investing|cash_flow_financing|equity_statement — promote to reference product]
reference: IFRS IAS 1; SEC S-K 1300; GRI Mining and Metals Sector Supplement

description: SAP functional area (FKBER) classifying the GL account by business function for segment reporting (e.g., mining operations, mineral processing, exploration, administration, sales). Supports IFRS 8 Operating Segments disclosure for mining companies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mining_operations|mineral_processing|exploration|administration|sales_marketing|logistics|hse|rehabilitation — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO FKBER; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; SEC S-K 1300

description: Indicates whether this GL account is designated for exploration and evaluation expenditure under IFRS 6. When true, costs posted to this account are subject to IFRS 6 impairment testing and disclosure requirements. Relevant for junior and major mining companies with active exploration programs.
reference: IFRS 6 Exploration for and Evaluation of Mineral Resources; SEC S-K 1300; NI 43-101

description: SAP interest calculation indicator (XZPMO) specifying whether interest is calculated on balances in this GL account. Relevant for intercompany loan accounts, rehabilitation provision accounts, and deferred tax accounts in mining group structures.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.XZPMO; IFRS IAS 37 Provisions; IFRS IAS 23 Borrowing Costs

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this GL account master record in SAP FI. Supports change management tracking, SOX compliance, and chart of accounts version control for the mining enterprise.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKA1; Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) Section 404; IFRS IAS 1; Internal Audit

description: SAP line item display flag (XKRES) indicating whether individual journal entry line items are stored and displayable for this account. When true, full drill-down to individual postings is available, supporting audit and cost analysis for mining operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.XKRES; Internal Audit; MSHA Recordkeeping Requirements

description: SAP open item management flag (XOPVW) indicating whether individual line items posted to this account must be cleared against each other (e.g., GR/IR clearing accounts, bank clearing accounts). Critical for accounts payable and procurement reconciliation in mining supply chain.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.XOPVW; IFRS IAS 1; Pronto Xi Procurement Integration

description: Indicates whether cost planning is blocked for this GL account in SAP CO (XSPEP). When true, budget planning entries cannot be made against this account, used to control CAPEX and OPEX budget planning integrity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO SKB1.XSPEP; CAPEX/OPEX Budgeting Controls

description: Indicates whether direct postings to this GL account are blocked (SAP XSPEB). When true, no journal entries can be posted to this account, typically used for accounts under review, year-end closing accounts, or accounts pending reclassification.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.XSPEB; Internal Financial Controls

description: Indicates whether postings to this GL account are relevant for SAP Profit Centre Accounting (PCA). When true, all postings are distributed to profit centres representing individual mine sites or commodity business units, enabling site-level profitability reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO-PCA; IFRS 8 Operating Segments; ICMM Reporting Framework

description: Indicates whether this GL account is a reconciliation account (SAP XINTB) that is automatically updated from a subledger (accounts payable, accounts receivable, asset accounting). Reconciliation accounts cannot be posted to directly and are critical for financial integrity in mining ERP environments.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.XINTB; IFRS IAS 1

description: Indicates whether this GL account is used to record mine rehabilitation and closure provisions under IFRS IAS 37. Rehabilitation provision accounts are subject to annual actuarial review and are disclosed in the notes to financial statements and environmental impact statements.
reference: IFRS IAS 37 Provisions Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets; ISO 14001; EIS (Environmental Impact Statement); ICMM

description: Indicates whether this GL account is designated for recording mineral royalty payments to government or private landowners. Royalty accounts are separately tracked for AISC reporting, tax deductibility assessment, and regulatory disclosure under mining tenement agreements.
reference: AISC World Gold Council Standard; JORC Code 2012; Australian Minerals Resource Rent Tax; SEC S-K 1300

description: SAP sort key (ZUAWA) defining the default assignment field populated when posting to this GL account (e.g., document date, posting date, purchase order number, cost centre). Controls how line items are sorted in account statements and financial reports.

SAP tax category (MWSKZ) controlling whether the GL account is relevant for input tax (GST/VAT on purchases), output tax (GST/VAT on sales), or not tax-relevant. Critical for mining royalty and resource rent tax compliance across jurisdictions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.MWSKZ; IFRS IAS 12 Income Taxes; Australian GST Act

description: SAP tolerance group (TOLGR) assigned to the GL account, defining acceptable variance limits for payment differences and clearing transactions. Used in accounts payable and receivable processing for mining procurement and commodity sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI SKB1.TOLGR; Internal Financial Controls

business_glossary_term: Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Element Relevance Flag
description: Indicates whether postings to this GL account require a WBS element assignment in SAP PS (Project System). When true, costs must be assigned to a project WBS element, typically for CAPEX projects, mine development, or exploration programs tracked under IFRS 6.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PS WBS; IFRS 6; CAPEX Project Accounting; JORC Code 2012

description: Unique identifier for the journal entry header record in the general ledger system. Primary key for all financial postings.

Foreign key linking to finance.cost_centre. Business justification: Journal entries can be cost centre specific for management accounting. The cost_center code should be replaced with FK to cost_centre.cost_centre_id to enable joining to cost centre master data.

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: SAP FI journal entry headers for asset transactions reference the fixed asset via asset number and subnumber. The journal_entry table currently stores asset_number and asset_subnumber as denormalized

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: In SAP FI, every journal entry is posted to a company code which maps 1:1 to a legal entity. The journal_entry table currently stores company_code as a denormalized string. Adding legal_entity_id as a

Foreign key linking to mine.mine_site. Business justification: Journal entries are posted against mine sites for site-level P&L reporting and cost allocation. Every mining journal entry is coded to a mine site — a fundamental financial posting requirement for sit

Foreign key linking to processing.processing_plant. Business justification: Plant-specific journal entries (accruals, inventory adjustments, depreciation) reference the plant for cost centre derivation and financial reporting. Enables plant-level P&L and balance sheet reporti

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_centre. Business justification: Journal entries can be profit centre specific for segment reporting. The profit_center code should be replaced with FK to profit_centre.profit_centre_id to enable joining to profit centre master data.

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Journal entries can be project-related and posted to WBS elements. The wbs_element code should be replaced with FK to wbs_element.wbs_element_id to enable joining to project master data.
The business area for cross-company code reporting. Represents business segments that span multiple legal entities, such as global copper operations or integrated iron ore value chain.
The date on which this journal entry was cleared. Populated when open items are settled. Used for aging analysis and cash flow reporting.
The document number of the clearing entry if this journal entry has been cleared (e.g., invoice cleared by payment). Used for open item management and reconciliation.

The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this journal entry was originally posted (e.g., USD, AUD, CAD, CLP for Chilean Peso, ZAR for South African Rand). All line items in this entry share this currency.
The date printed on the original source document (invoice, receipt, contract). May differ from posting date. Used for document tracking and audit purposes.
Free-form text description at the header level providing context for this journal entry. Used for explanatory notes, period-end adjustments, and manual posting descriptions.
The externally-known unique document number assigned by SAP FI to this journal entry. Used for audit trails and cross-system reconciliation.

Classifies the journal entry by its business purpose: SA (General Ledger Document), AB (Accounting Document), KR (Vendor Invoice), DR (Customer Invoice), DZ (Payment Document), KA (Customer Document), KG (Vendor Credit Memo), KN (Net Customer Document), KZ (Payment Request), RE (Invoice Receipt), RV (Transfer Posting), WA (Goods Issue), WE (Goods Receipt), ZP (Payment Posting). Controls number ranges and posting rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SA|AB|KR|DR|DZ|KA|KG|KN|KZ|RE|RV|WA|WE|ZP — 14 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The calendar date when this journal entry was physically entered into the SAP system. Used for audit trail and user activity tracking.
The time of day when this journal entry was entered into the system. Stored in HH:MM:SS format. Used for detailed audit trails.
The exchange rate used to convert the document currency to the company code local currency at the time of posting. Used for multi-currency consolidation and reporting.
The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when this journal entry was posted. Typically 1-12 for regular periods, 13-16 for special closing periods.
The fiscal year in which this journal entry is posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and IFRS compliance.

The functional area for cost-of-sales accounting. Classifies costs by function: mining operations, processing, logistics, sales and marketing, administration. Used for functional P&L presentation under IFRS.

Flag indicating whether this journal entry represents an intercompany transaction between different company codes within the mining group. True for intercompany postings requiring elimination entries during consolidation.
The internal order number for tracking specific cost collection objects such as maintenance campaigns, exploration programs, or overhead cost pools. Used for detailed OPEX analysis.

Identifies the ledger to which this entry is posted: 0L (Leading Ledger - IFRS), 1L (Local GAAP), 2L (Management Reporting), 3L (Tax Ledger), 9L (Consolidation Ledger). Supports parallel accounting under IFRS and local standards.
reference: SAP FI New General Ledger - RLDNR

description: The total amount of this journal entry converted to the company code local currency using the exchange rate. Used for statutory financial statements and IFRS reporting.
reference: SAP FI Local Currency Amount - DMBTR

description: The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the company code's local currency. All amounts are converted to this currency for statutory reporting.
The material master number when this journal entry relates to inventory movements, ROM stockpile valuations, or concentrate sales. Links to material master for commodity tracking.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry is parked (saved but not yet posted). True for parked documents awaiting approval, False for posted documents. Used for workflow and approval processes.

The date on which this journal entry was posted to the general ledger. This is the accounting date that determines which fiscal period the entry affects. Critical for period-end close and financial statement preparation.
Two-digit code that controls the type of posting at the line item level: 01-49 (Debit postings), 50-99 (Credit postings). Determines account type, debit/credit indicator, and field status.
External reference number from the source document (purchase order, invoice, contract). Used to link journal entries back to originating business transactions.
Flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a previous posting. True if this entry reverses another entry, False otherwise. Used for accrual reversals and error corrections.

Code indicating the reason for reversing the original entry: 01 (Reversal in Current Period), 02 (Reversal in Next Period), 03 (Incorrect Posting), 04 (Accrual Reversal), 05 (Other). Used for financial control and audit purposes.
The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Maintains audit trail for reversed transactions.
The company code of the intercompany trading partner when intercompany_indicator is True. Used for intercompany reconciliation and consolidation elimination entries.
The total amount of this journal entry in the original transaction currency. Sum of all debit or credit line items in document currency.
Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for the journal entry line product.
